name: actionlib
description:
The actionlib stack provides a standardized interface for
    interfacing with preemptable tasks. Examples of this include moving
    the base to a target location, performing a laser scan and returning
    the resulting point cloud, detecting the handle of a door, etc.
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Vijay Pradeep, Mikael Arguedas

Tips remove jumper block Power link pins provide device. add user dialout group acces /dev/ttyACM* sudo adduser your_user_name dialout takes seconds /dev/ttyACM* uninstall modemmanager sudo apt remove modemmanager ADIS16470 Overview ADIS16470 complete inertial system includes triaxis gyroscope triaxis accelerometer. Breakout board easy Connection build flat cable connect USB-ISS ADIS16470 breakout board. picture shows implementation. simple schematic USB-ISS pin pin headers ADIS16470 breakout board. Service /imu/bias_estimate service activate ADIS16470’s internal bias estimation function. call service IMU steady seconds. bias gyro sensors calcuarated average duration. sensor obtained substracted bias bias stored chip cleared powered reset. ADXL345 Overview ADXL345 small, thin, power, 3-axis accelerometer high resolution (13-bit) measurement moment, support device experimental. name: agni_tf_tools
description:
This package provides a gui program as well as a rviz plugin to publish static transforms.
  Both support the transformation between various Euler angle representations.
  The rviz plugin also allows to configure the transform with an interactive marker.
readme: ROS package tools facilitating definition (static) ROS transforms. extensions rviz: EulerProperty class allowing definition arbitrary sets Euler angles conversion RotationProperty class combining EulerProperty QuaternionProperty provide flexible means entering orientation information. interactive transform publisher rviz::Display plugin allowing define transforms rviz interactive marker. interactively perform frame transformations. cmdline utilities: static_transform_publisher adapted version tf2_ros’s source code, providing fine-grained command-line options define orientations arbitrary set Euler angles. static_transform_publisher_gui interactive version static_transform_publisher allowing modify transform interactively.

name: angles
description:
This package provides a set of simple math utilities to work
        with angles. The utilities cover simple things like
        normalizing an angle and conversion between degrees and
        radians. But even if you're trying to calculate things like
        the shortest angular distance between two joint space
        positions of your robot, but the joint motion is constrained
        by joint limits, this package is what you need. The code in
        this package is stable and well tested. There are no plans for
        major changes in the near future.

Register export attributes notify location define <export> tag package.xml. package_root/package.xml <package> <run_depend>app_manager</run_depend> <export> <app_manager app_dir="${prefix}/apps"/> </export> </package> launch app_manager argument: rosrun app_manager app_manager app_manager node automatically searches .installed files register applications. name: asr_calibration_tool_dome
description: This package provides a tool for calibraiting the kinematic chain of the PdB-Dome. It provides a data-tracking for recording 6D positions of Flock of Birds data glove and a checkerboard mounted on it. With this construction, the kinematic chain is closed. The recorded data is written to a file and can be used for the asr_kinematic_chain_optimizer.
authors: Heller Florian, Stöckle Patrick, Wittenbeck Valerij
readme: asr_calibration_tool_dome documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_calibration_tool_dome

name: asr_cyberglove_visualization

This package is used to test the functionalities of the CyberGloves and FlockOfBirds. 
    It uses an URDF model of a human hand to provide a visualization of the glove movements in RViz.

authors: Allgeyer Tobias, Hutmacher Robin, Meißner Pascal
readme: asr_descriptor_surface_based_recognition Documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_descriptor_surface_based_recognition

name: asr_direct_search_manager
description:
This package can be used to generate and manage poses for the direct mode of 3-D
    object search. The direct mode is used as an opening procedure for Active Scene Recognition.
    The poses will be generated so that they cover the current environment map.
    There are different modes to generate these poses. One is based on a grid and a
    second on a recording of the "cropbox record" mode in the asr_state_machine.
authors: Borella Jocelyn, Karrenbauer Oliver, Meißner Pascal
readme: asr_direct_search_manager Documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_direct_search_manager

name: asr_fake_object_recognition
description: This package provides a 'perception algorithm'-independent simulation of 6-D object localization for 3D object search by a mobile robot: Based on the poses of the searched objects with respect to the current viewing frustum(s) of the robot, the detectability of the objects is estimated.
authors: Allgeyer Tobias, Aumann Florian, Borella Jocelyn, Meißner Pascal, Qattan Mohamad
readme: asr_fake_object_recognition Documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_fake_object_recognition

description: asr_flir_ptu_controller is a package to control a flir ptu unit from a action server
readme: asr_flir_ptu_driver documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_flir_ptu_controller

description:
asr_flir_ptu_driver is a package for controlling a flir ptu via (external) msg
     authors: Valerij Wittenbeck, Joachim Gehrung, Pascal Meissner, Patrick Schlosser
authors: Valerij Wittenbeck, Joachim Gehrung, Pascal Meißner, Patrick Schlosser
readme: asr_flir_ptu_driver documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_flir_ptu_driver

name: asr_flock_of_birds
description: The asr_flock_of_birds package is the driver for Ascension Technology Corp magnet-tracking system "Flock of Birds". It provides the remote server which publishes two 6d posemarker and the nessesary TF from the magnettracking system. It also contains launchfiles and calibration tools for the use of it.
authors: Bernhardt Andre, Engelmann Stephan, Giesler Björn, Heller Florian, Jäkel Rainer, Nguyen Trung, Pardowitz Michael, Weckesser Peter, Yi Xie, Zöllner Raoul
readme: asr_flock_of_birds documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_flock_of_birds

name: asr_flock_of_birds_tracking
description: This package controls a motorized robot head (a sensor setup equipped with a PTU) in order to ensure that the hand of a human user remains inside its field of view. The human hand is tracked with the help of an Ascension - Flock of Birds system. The purpose of this package is to enable continuous localization of objects during their manipulation through the tracked hand.
authors: Engelmann Stephan, Heller Florian, Meißner Pascal, Stöckle Patrick, Wittenbeck Valerij
readme: asr_flock_of_birds_tracking documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_flock_of_birds_tracking

name: asr_ftc_local_planner
description: A local planner which based on the "follow the carrot" algorithm. Drives accurate along the global plan
readme: asr_ftc_local_planner Documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_ftc_local_planner

name: asr_gazebo_models
description: This package provides our gazebo_models
authors: Dehmani Souheil, Mayr Matthias, Meißner Pascal
readme: asr_gazebo_models documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_gazebo_models

name: asr_grid_creator
description:
This package can generate a grid for the current map. Each grid_point
    defines a position where the robot has to move to. The grid can be used
    later on in the asr_direct_search_manager as a basis to generate poses to
    search the map. At each grid_point the robot will search around him. All
    gird_points together should cover the map.
authors: Borella Jocelyn, Karrenbauer Oliver, Meißner Pascal
readme: asr_grid_creator documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_grid_creator

name: asr_halcon_bridge
description: This package is used to convert between image-messages of the ROS environment and HALCON-images.
readme: asr_halcon_bridge Documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_halcon_bridge

name: asr_intermediate_object_generator
description:
The intermediate object generator generates intermediate objects for a domain composed from scenes. 
    It is used to restrain the amount of objects searched during the direct search phase by selecting appropriate objects to search.
readme: asr_intermediate_object_generator documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_intermediate_object_generator

name: asr_ism
description:
This package contains nodes which make up the Passive-Scene-Recognition interface to Implicit Shape Model (ISM) trees. The Active-Scene-Recognition interface to ISM trees is located in asr_recognizer_prediction_ism, instead. A short outline of the functionalities provided by the nodes are:
      1. Recording of scenes
      2. Training of an ISM tree (Implicit shape model tree)
      3. Recognition of scenes
      4. Visualization of ISM (tree) data 
	  etc.
authors: Borella Jocelyn, Hanselmann Fabian, Heller Florian, Heizmann Heinrich, Kübler Marcel, Mehlhaus Jonas, Meißner Pascal, Qattan Mohamad, Reckling Reno, Stroh Daniel
readme: asr_ism Documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_ism

name: asr_ism_visualizations
description: This package provides visualization logic for ism data, e.g. visualization with marker in rviz.
authors: Hanselmann Fabian, Heller Florian, Heizmann Heinrich, Kübler Marcel, Meißner Pascal, Reckling Reno, Stöckle Patrick, Trautmann Jeremias
readme: asr_ism_visualizations Documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_ism_visualizations

name: asr_ivt
description: Ros wrapper for the Integrated Vision Toolkit (IVT) library (version 1.3.22)
authors: Allgeyer Tobias, Hutmacher Robin, Kleinert Daniel, Meißner Pascal, Scholz Jonas, Stöckle Patrick
readme: asr_ivt Documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_ivt

name: asr_ivt_bridge
description: This package is used to convert between ROS messages and IVT images
authors: Hutmacher Robin, Kleinert Daniel, Meißner Pascal
readme: asr_ivt_bridge Documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_ivt_bridge

name: asr_kinematic_chain_dome
description: This package provides information about the PbD dome's kinematic chain and contains launch-files to publish the chain to a robot_state_publisher. Available launchfiles are: dome with real PTU, dome with simulated PTU, dome with real PTU and Flock of Birds and additional transformation_publisher from guppy-cameras to the kinect cameras mounted above. It also contains the urdf model.
authors: Heizmann Heinrich, Heller Florian, Meißner Pascal, Wittenbeck Valerij
readme: asr_kinematic_chain_dome documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_kinematic_chain_dome

name: asr_kinematic_chain_optimizer
description: This package calculates the best approximation for a kinematic chain's parameters using rosenbrock optimization. For calculation, a layout of the kinematic chain must be provided, as well as a set of transformations between two frames of the chain that has been acquired during calibration.
authors: Aumann Florian, Heller Florian, Jäkel Rainer, Wittenbeck Valerij
readme: asr_kinematic_chain_optimizer documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_kinematic_chain_optimizer

name: asr_lib_ism
description: This package contains the ROS-independent library which provides the actual scene-recognition functionality of Implicit Shape Model (ISM) trees. It is referred to both by asr_ism for Passive Scene Recognition and by asr_recognizer_prediction_ism for Active Scene Recognition.
authors: Hanselmann Fabian, Heller Florian, Heizmann Heinrich, Kübler Marcel, Mehlhaus Jonas, Meißner Pascal, Qattan Mohamad, Reckling Reno, Stroh Daniel
readme: asr_lib_ism Documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_lib_ism

name: asr_lib_pose_prediction_ism
description: This package contains classes and algorithms to predict poses of searched objects by the help of a tree of ISMs. It is organized as a library and contains only a small program to evaluate the performance of different algorithms.

This package contains all messages that are particular to our Active Scene Recognition - Framework at

	  They are of critical importance and structured by the ROS communication capabilities.

name: asr_navfn
description:
navfn provides a fast interpolated navigation function that can be used to create plans for
        a mobile base. The planner assumes a circular robot and operates on a costmap to find a
        minimum cost plan from a start point to an end point in a grid. The navigation function is
        computed with Dijkstra's algorithm, but support for an A* heuristic may also be added in the
        near future. navfn also provides a ROS wrapper for the navfn planner that adheres to the
        nav_core::BaseGlobalPlanner interface specified in
authors: Kurt Konolige, Eitan Marder-Eppstein, contradict <AT> gmail.com, Felix Marek
readme: asr_navfn documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_navfn

name: asr_next_best_view

name: asr_psm
description:
This package contains a system to recognize scenes called the Probabilistic Scene Model (PSM).
    It uses objects and relative poses (relations) between the objects. The realations can be dynamic and each object can be a reference object.
    The system consists of a training subsystem that trains new scenes and a scene inference subsystem that calculates scene probabilities of previously trained scenes.
authors: Braun Kai, Gehrung Joachim, Heizmann Heinrich, Meißner Pascal
readme: asr_psm Documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_psm

name: asr_recognizer_prediction_ism
description:
This package includes the algorithmic link between object pose prediction and scene recognition, both based on ISM trees. While it only refers to other packages with respect to the aforementioned
    functionalities, it includes the algorithms realizing Scene Model Sampling. E.g., it provides a (service-based) interface for the scene recognition, including an ASR-optimized visualization of ISM trees.
    The reason for linking scene recognition and object pose prediction within a package instead of message passing, is the size and number of scene recognition results occurring.
authors: Aumann Florian, Heller Florian, Hutmacher Robin, Meißner Pascal, Stöckle Patrick, Stroh Daniel
readme: asr_recognizer_prediction_ism documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_recognizer_prediction_ism

name: asr_recognizer_prediction_psm
description: This package provides object pose prediction on the basis of the alternative scene model PSM (Probabilistic Scene Model): It generates hypotheses (type and pose) for missing objects in the context of 3D object search. Moreover, it integrates scene recognition and object pose prediction (both on the basis of PSM) into Active Scene Recognition. Its output are asr_next_best_view/AttributedPointCloud.msg messages which can be processed by asr_next_best_view.
readme: asr_recognizer_prediction_psm documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_recognizer_prediction_psm

name: asr_state_machine
description:
This package implements the logic which connects different functionalities such as (passive) scene recognition, object pose prediction and Next-Best-View estimation
    to Active Scene Recognition. In particular, it is the interface for executing different 3D-object-search procedures one of which Active Scene Recognition is.
    Active Scene Recognition is equivalent to the "object search", which consists of the "direct search" and "indirect search" modes. The search will be executed by
    using different packages of the asr framework.
authors: Allgeyer Tobias, Aumann Florian, Borella Jocelyn, Hutmacher Robin, Karrenbauer Oliver, Marek Felix, Meißner Pascal, Trautmann Jeremias, Wittenbeck Valerij
readme: asr_state_machine documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_state_machine

name: asr_visualization_server
description: This package is used for the visualization of the models in the dome/mild environment
authors: Allgeyer Tobias, Braun Kai, Heller Florian, Mehlhaus Jonas, Meißner Pascal, Qattan Mohamad, Wittenbeck Valerij
readme: asr_visualization_server Documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_visualization_server

name: asr_world_model
description:
This package implements the logic of our Online World Model, while the Offline Knowledge Base is distributed across asr_object_database, mild_navigation and asr_visualization_server. It contains four types of information:
      1. All estimations of objects which were localized by object detection (object models)
      2. All views in which objects of given types have been searched due to advice of asr_next_best_view and asr_direct_search_manager
      3. All recognized complete patterns by the recognizer_prediction_ism (scene models)
      4. Some additional information about the Objects: IntermediateObjectWeight, RecognizerName
authors: Aumann Florian, Borella Jocelyn, Hutmacher Robin, Karrenbauer Oliver, Meißner Pascal, Schleicher Ralf, Stöckle Patrick, Trautmann Jeremias
readme: asr_world_model documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_world_model

description: AWS RoboMaker package containing ROS service definitions for service endpoints provided inside of an AWS RoboMaker simulation.
readme: AWS RoboMaker ROS Message Service package ROS service definitions service endpoints AWS RoboMaker simulation. Visit AWS RoboMaker website learn building intelligent robotic applications Amazon Web Services. Install source code workspace. Add .rosinstall file contents workspace folder run rosws update: git: {local-name: src/aws-robomaker-simulation-ros-pkgs, uri: 'https://github.com/aws-robotics/aws-robomaker-simulation-ros-pkgs.git', version: master} Add dependency package.xml access service types (.srv): <package> <depend>aws_robomaker_simulation_ros_pkgs</depend> </package> Follow Python usage build bundle workspace usual: build ROS rosws update rosdep install --from-paths --ignore-src colcon build colcon bundle Usage tag, untag, list tags simulation job ROS command-line ROS application running. AWS RoboMaker documentation. IAM role permissions Replace account# account number. "Version": "Statement": "Action": "robomaker:TagResource", "robomaker:UntagResource", "robomaker:ListTagsForResource" "Resource": "arn:aws:robomaker:*:account#:simulation-job/*" "Effect": AddTags Python import rospy robomaker_simulation_msgs.msg import Tag robomaker_simulation_msgs.srv import AddTags def add_tags_to_my_sim(): add_tags([Tag(key="name", value="my_test")]) def add_tags(tags): timeoutInSeconds rospy.wait_for_service('/robomaker/job/add_tags', timeout=timeoutInSeconds) requestAddTags rospy.ServiceProxy('/robomaker/job/add_tags', AddTags) response requestAddTags(tags) response.success: AddTags failed print response.message RemoveTag Python import rospy robomaker_simulation_msgs.srv import RemoveTags def remove_tags_from_my_sim(): remove_tags([Tag(key="name", value="my_test")]) def remove_tags(tags): timeoutInSeconds rospy.wait_for_service('/robomaker/job/remove_tags', timeout=timeoutInSeconds) requestRemoveTags rospy.ServiceProxy('/robomaker/job/remove_tags', RemoveTags) response requestRemoveTags(tags) response.success: RemoveTags failed print response.message ListTags Python import rospy robomaker_simulation_msgs.srv import ListTags def list_tags(): timeoutInSeconds rospy.wait_for_service('/robomaker/job/list_tags', timeout=timeoutInSeconds) requestListTags rospy.ServiceProxy('/robomaker/job/list_tags', ListTags) response requestListTags() response.success: ListTags succeeded print response.tags ListTags failed print response.message CancelSimulation cancel simulation job ROS command-line ROS application running. AWS RoboMaker documentation. IAM role permissions Replace account# account number. "Version": "Statement": "Action": "robomaker:CancelSimulationJob" "Resource": "arn:aws:robomaker:*:account#:simulation-job/*" "Effect": Python import rospy robomaker_simulation_msgs.srv import Cancel def cancel_job(): timeoutInSeconds rospy.wait_for_service('/robomaker/job/cancel', timeout=timeoutInSeconds) requestCancel rospy.ServiceProxy('/robomaker/job/cancel', Cancel) response requestCancel() response.success: Request cancel job failed print response.message License library licensed Apache License.

name: bfl
description:
This package contains a recent version of the Bayesian Filtering
  Library (BFL), distributed by the Orocos Project.  For stability
  reasons, this package is currently locked to revision 31655 (April
  19, 2010), but this revision will be updated on a regular basis to
  the latest available BFL trunk.  This ROS package does not modify
  BFL in any way, it simply provides a convenient way to download and
  compile the library, because BFL is not available from an OS package
  manager.  This ROS package compiles BFL with the Boost library for
  matrix operations and random number generation.
authors: Klaas Gadeyne, Wim Meeussen, Tinne Delaet and many others. See web page for a full contributor list. ROS package maintained by Wim Meeussen.

GNU General Public License details. received copy GNU General Public License program; write Free Software Foundation, Temple Place Suite Boston, USA. library encoporates ideas software libraries: Scene (Andrew Davison). <http://www.robots.ox.ac.uk/~ajd/Scene/> Bayes++ ACFR). <http://www.acfr.usyd.edu.au/technology/bayesianfilter/Bayes++.htm> CES programming library (Sebastian Thrun). <http://www-2.cs.cmu.edu/afs/cs.cmu.edu/user/thrun/public_html/papers/thrun.ces-tr.html> research Bayesian methods compliant motion problems <http://www.mech.kuleuven.be/pma/research/manip/default_en.phtml> important features Released GNU LGPL licence Wrapper matrix RNG libraries, favourite matrix library. wrappers exist matrix/RNG wrapper library LTIlib <http://ltilib.sourceforge.net/doc/homepage/index.shtml>: library algorithms data structures frequently image processing computer vision. NEWMAT <http://www.robertnz.net/nm_intro.htm> Matrix Library boost <http://www.boost.org/> RNG "Bayesian unifying Design". incorporate Bayesian filtering algorithm! filter schemes implemented. Standard EKF, IEKF Non-minimal State <http://people.mech.kuleuven.ac.be/~tlefebvr/publicaties/BayesStat.ps.gz> Standard Particle filter (arbitrary proposal), BootstrapFilter (Proposal System Model PDF), Auxiliary Particle filter, Extended Kalman Particle Filter. details design ideas, poster library presented Valencia conference Bayesian Statistics, <http://people.mech.kuleuven.ac.be/~kgadeyne/doctoraat.html> filtering libraries home page <http://www.orocos.org/bfl> Tinne Laet Contributed tutorial website. <http://people.mech.kuleuven.be/~tdelaet/bfl_doc/getting_started_guide/getting_started_guide.html> discusses construct filter bfl. name: bond
description:
A bond allows two processes, A and B, to know when the other has
    terminated, either cleanly or by crashing.  The bond remains
    connected until it is either broken explicitly or until a
    heartbeat times out.

name: bond_core
description:

description:

name: smclib
description:
The State Machine Compiler (SMC) from http://smc.sourceforge.net/
    converts a language-independent description of a state machine
    into the source code to support that state machine.

    This package contains the libraries that a compiled state machine
    depends on, but it does not contain the compiler itself.

     then updating the URDF.

    the calibration stack to run on your robot.

    Expect its APIs to change.

    in on several topics. This package is experimental and unstable.
    Expect its APIs to change.

description:

     settled. That is, it calculates how long a set of joints has remained
     within a specified threshold. This package is experimental and unstable.
     Expect its APIs to change.

     This package is experimental and unstable. Expect its APIs to change.

     This package is experimental and unstable.
     Expect its APIs to change.

    Expect its APIs to change.

name: class_loader
description: The class_loader package is a ROS-independent package for loading plugins during runtime and the foundation of the higher level ROS "pluginlib" library. class_loader utilizes the host operating system's runtime loader to open runtime libraries (e.g. .so/.dll files), introspect the library for exported plugin classes, and allows users to instantiate objects of said exported classes without the explicit declaration (i.e. header file) for those classes.

name: cnn_bridge
description: The cnn_bridge package take freeze graphs and publishes them as ROS messages
readme: Overview package support parsing convolution neural networks (CNN), publishing ROS messages. package supports detection segmentation networks. Input camera topics, OpenCV camera, video. ROS Nodes cnn_publisher ROS node opens freeze graph run images. Publishes detection type cnn_bridge/Detection Detection data. Published boxes, scores, classes. addition, header image network run statistics, hmi). segmentation type cnn_bridge/Netmask Segmentation data. Published 2-dimensional array mask values. addition, header image network run statistics, hmi). Parameters source type string required True source images run network. types inputs allowed. path video file OpenCV compatible files work). device number OpenCV device. ROS Image CompressedImage topic. option works ‘usb_cam’ (subscribes ‘usb_cam/image_raw’), ‘cv_cam’ (subscribes ‘cv_camera/image_raw’), ‘ueye’ subscribes ‘ueye_0/image_raw’). logdir type string required True Path hypes file. bellow JSON file. metadata_source type string required True Path metadata file. bellow JSON file. mode type string required True ‘detection’ ‘segmentation’ depending mode. input_tensor type string required True explanatory. output_tensor type string/[string] required True segmentation, explanatory. detection array tensors [boxes,scores,classes] display type Boolean default True display output video_save type Boolean/String default True implemented! save output False disable. True saves ‘/Camera__datetime'. string video title. node saves raw video video mask boxes overlay. running video file, raw saved. cpu type string default False implemented! Sets run network CPU Nvidia GPU present. gpu_percent type Float default Sets percentage Nvidia GPU generally running simultaneous networks. Launch File Examples Start cnn_bridge segmentation mode: roslaunch cnn_bridge segmentation_publisher.launch Start cnn_bridge detection mode: roslaunch cnn_bridge detection_publisher.launch Hypes “frozen_graph_path”: "image_height": "image_width": "resize_image": true, //TODO Add additional fields Metadata JSON segmentation mode: “classes”: [“CLASS_NAME_1”, “CLASS_NAME_2”, “CLASS_NAME_N”] detection mode: “classes”: “color”: (red, green, blue), class outputted network assigned class “id_category”: parent class class dog parent Animal) “category”: parent class class dog parent Animal)

name: scenario_test_tools
description:
The scenario_test_tools package implements helpers for scriptable scenario testing.
    It allows to set up a test harness for eg. a state machine or other high level behavior by
    providing mocked implementations for various action servers and services that work together
readme: Automated testing automated tests scenario_test_tools provide basic framework mocked, scriptable introspectable ROS services action servers. operated process offer correct, integral mock aspects robot. guide high-level behavior edge-cases verify handled intended. core idea tests specifies sequence services/actions behavior call. test inspect request/goal reply success failure check failure handled correctly Framework core framework ScriptableBase, ScriptableActionserver ScriptableServiceServer based. ScriptableBase introspect goals received_goals. simply list received goals. combined remember_goals context manager, goals forgotten entering context exiting multiple tests sequence. set result to-be-received goal reply method. similar methods: reply_conditionally: takes callable returns True False based goal sends result await_goal reply_directly. await_goal blocks returns goal received, goal inspected. Based reply reply_directly. Set default results default_result bothering reply* time, actions expected success real robot, text-to-speech. override temporarily, syntax: scriptable_thing.custom_reply(): scriptable_thing.reply(SomeActionResult('non-default')) Examples scripts directory scripts: example_test.py dummy_behavior.py work dummy behavior wait commands robot charge plug test sends commands checks behavior handles command correctly. Run dummy_behavior rosrun scenario_test_tools dummy_behavior.py start test rosrun scenario_test_tools example_test.py --start_scenario=test_all move_base_success.py default_result feature move_base implementation succeeds time example_move_base elaborate version handling goal differently show features framework. goal received succeeds, aborted goals expected handled

name: cob_base_velocity_smoother
description: The 'cob_base_velocity_smoother' reads velocity messages and publishes messages of the same type for "smoothed" velocity to avoid shaking behavior.

name: cob_cartesian_controller
description:
This package provides nodes that broadcast tf-frames along various (model-based) Cartesian paths (e.g. Linear, Circular).
    The tf-frames are interpolated using a given velocity profile (e.g. Ramp, Sinoid) and can be used as targets for the cob_frame_tracker/cob_twist_controller.

name: cob_control

description: The cob_frame_tracker package contains nodes that publish Twist commands based on the distance to the desired tf frame target.

name: cob_hardware_emulation
description: The cob_hardware_emulation package provides idealized nodes emulating real robot hardware and/or physics simulation.

name: cob_mecanum_controller

The cob_mecanum_controller_node provides a lightweight base controller for mecanum drive robots. 
    The out/input for the wheel command/state are wheel velocities in rad/s for the wheels 
    [front left, front right, rear left, rear right]

name: cob_omni_drive_controller
description: The cob_omni_drive_controller package provides a ros_controller plugin for the Care-O-bot omni-directional base platform.

name: cob_trajectory_controller
description: This package provides a trajectory controller which controlls velocities for a chain of joints. This controller can be used e.g. with [[schunk_powercube_chain]].

name: cob_tricycle_controller
description: The cob_omni_drive_controller package provides a ros_controller plugin for the Care-O-bot tricycle base platform.

name: cob_twist_controller
description:
The main purpose of the cob_twist_controller is to convert target twists into joint velocities. 
  Therefore it makes use of several implemented inverse kinematics approaches at the first order differential level. 
  The inverse differential kinematics solver considers kinematic chain extensions, singularity robustness, 
  redundancy resolution and priority-based methods.
  To avoid hardware destruction there is a limiter interface active as well. 
  Via parameter server users can dynamically configure the solving strategy.
authors: Felix Messmer, Marco Bezzon, Christoph Mark, Francisco Moreno

name: cob_base_drive_chain
description: This package contains classes that are able to control the platform of the Care-O-Bot. This means to establish a CAN communication to drive and steering motors of the platform and later send motion commands and receive motor information.

name: cob_bms_driver
description: Driver package for interfacing the battery management system (BMS) on Care-O-bot.

name: cob_canopen_motor
description: The package cob_canopen_motor implements a controller-drive component which is connected to a can-bus and works with a canopen-interface. "CanDriveItf" provides a - more or less - generic interface to the controller-drive components. "CanDrvie..." then implements a specific setup, e.g. an ELMO Harmonica Controller in case of the "CanDriveHarmonica".

name: cob_driver

name: cob_generic_can
description: The package cob_generic_can provides an interface for nodes on a can-bus and examplary wrappers for two PeakSys-can-libs. When a can-bus-device is generated (for an example see base_dirve_chain) you can use generic_can to create as many itfs as there will be components communicating via this can-bus. Assign type of the can communication device (e.g. usb-to-can or can-card of a specific vendor) and can-address of the target device. This package comes with wrappers for PeakSys and PeakSysUSB adapters.

name: cob_utilities
description:
Deprecated
    "cob_utilities" subsumes a number of classes, which are used in the original COb3 software. E.g. "IniFile.h" supports the original inifile structure of Care-O-bot 3. "MathSup.h" provides some basic functions like conversion from degree to radion or norming of angles within +/- PI. The package is currently used while the drivers are ported to ROS and Orocos respectively. Midterm it shall be removed and the ROS structures shall be used for reading parameters during initialization. So, don't use this package in new code!

name: cob_map_accessibility_analysis
description: cob_map_accessibility_analysis receives the map from navigation as well as obstacles and inflates_obstacles topics to assemble a common obstacle map. Upon request, this node checks the accessibility of poses within thin map by (i) checking whether the pose itself is free and by (ii) checking whether there is a closed path from robot to the goal pose.

description: CMake configuration to run coverage
readme: code_coverage ROS package run coverage testing Examples robot_calibration package generate coverage code Travis-CI Codecov.io Usage ROS package: Add code_coverage test depend package.xml Update CMakeLists.txt, testing section add NOTE order test targets coverage macros: if(CATKIN_ENABLE_TESTING ENABLE_COVERAGE_TESTING) find_package(code_coverage REQUIRED) Add compiler flags coverage instrumentation defining targets APPEND_COVERAGE_COMPILER_FLAGS() endif() Add targets (CATKIN_ENABLE_TESTING) Add tests Create target ${PROJECT_NAME}_coverage_report if(ENABLE_COVERAGE_TESTING) set(COVERAGE_EXCLUDES "*/${PROJECT_NAME}/test*" "*/${PROJECT_NAME}/other_dir_i_dont_care_about*") add_code_coverage( ${PROJECT_NAME}_coverage_report DEPENDENCIES _run_tests_${PROJECT_NAME} endif() endif() Note: variable COVERAGE_EXCLUDES content! build run tests debug build reasonable coverage numbers): CATKIN_MAKE: catkin_make -DENABLE_COVERAGE_TESTING=ON -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug catkin_make -DENABLE_COVERAGE_TESTING=ON -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug PACKAGE_NAME_coverage_report CATKIN_TOOLS: catkin config --cmake-args -DENABLE_COVERAGE_TESTING=ON -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug catkin build catkin build PACKAGE_NAME --no-deps --catkin-make-args PACKAGE_NAME_coverage_report output print coverage report located Python rostest Support interface Python-based unit tests require modification coverage information, Python-based nodes run rostest launch files bit additional instrumentation turned <launch> Add argument launch file turn coverage <arg name="coverage" default="false"/> fancy line forces nodes generate coverage <arg name="pythontest_launch_prefix" value="$(eval 'python-coverage run arg('coverage') node generate coverage information <node pkg="example_pkg" name="publisher_node" type="publisher_node.py" node generate coverage <node pkg="example_pkg" name="subscriber_node" type="subscriber_node.py" launch-prefix="$(arg pythontest_launch_prefix)" test generate coverage information include launch-prefix <test time-limit="10" test-name="sample_rostest" pkg="example_pkg" type="sample_rostest.py" launch-prefix="$(arg pythontest_launch_prefix)" </launch> CMakeLists, pass argument: add_rostest(example_rostest.test ARGS coverage:=ENABLE_COVERAGE_TESTING)

name: collada_parser
description:
This package contains a C++ parser for the Collada robot
    description format. The parser reads a Collada XML robot
    description, and creates a C++ URDF model. Although it is possible
    to directly use this parser when working with Collada robot
    descriptions, the preferred user API is found in the urdf package.
authors: Rosen Diankov, Kei Okada, Ioan Sucan, Jackie Kay

name: collada_urdf
description:
This package contains a tool to convert Unified Robot Description Format (URDF) documents into COLLAborative Design Activity (COLLADA) documents.

authors: Tim Field, Rosen Diankov, Ioan Sucan, Jackie Kay

name: crane_x7_control
description: The CRANE-X7 control package
readme: crane_x7_control CRANE-X7のためのパッケージ、 crane_x7_ros のcontrolパッケージです。 crane_x7_controlはU2D2(USB-RS485変換ケーブル)を使用してCRANE-X7のモータ制御を行います。 U2D2はLinuxに接続すると/dev/ttyUSB0として認識されるので、次のコマンドでアクセス権限を変更します。 sudo chmod /dev/ttyUSB0 ケーブルの接続ポート名はデフォルトで/dev/ttyUSB0です。 /dev/ttyUSB1)を使う場合は次のコマンドを実行します。 roslaunch crane_x7_control crane_x7_control.launch port:=/dev/ttyUSB1 crane_x7_controlはデフォルト200Hz周期で制御しています。 制御周期を変更する場合はcrane_x7_control/src/hardware.cppを編集してください。 #define CONTROL_HZ rostopic /crane_x7/crane_x7_control/crane_x7_gripper_finger_a_joint/current subscribed [/crane_x7/crane_x7_control/crane_x7_gripper_finger_a_joint/current] average rate: min: max: std dev: window: average rate: min: max: std dev: window: 実際の制御周期が200Hzに達せず、100Hz程度に低くなってしまう場合、USB通信ポートのlatency_timerを変更してください。 cat /sys/bus/usb-serial/devices/ttyUSB0/latency_timer echo /sys/bus/usb-serial/devices/ttyUSB0/latency_timer cat /sys/bus/usb-serial/devices/ttyUSB0/latency_timer 参考資料：https://emanual.robotis.com/docs/en/software/dynamixel/dynamixel_wizard2/#usb-latency-setting crane_x7_controlは/crane_x7をルートとするネームスペースにパラメータやトピックを定義します。 各jointについて次のトピックを配信します。 current：電流値[mA] dxl_position：現在角度[360/4096度] temp：温度[度] dynamic_reconfigure crane_x7_controlはdynamic_reconfigureに対応しています。次のコマンドでrqt_reconfigureを起動してアクセスすると各Jointのサーボパラメータを変更することができます。 rosrun rqt_reconfigure rqt_reconfigure 各パラメータの詳細についてはROBOTIS公式のXM430およびXM540のサーボマニュアルを参照して下さい。 XM430-W350 XM540-W270 CRANE-X7は位置制御モードと電流制御モードに対応しています。基本設定は位置制御モードになっていますが、複数箇所の設定変更を行うことで電流制御モードへ切り替えることが可能です。 サーボモータのOperating Mode（Address：11）を3：位置制御モードから0：電流制御モードへ設定変更する Dynamixel Wizard crane_x7_controlの設定変更 crane_x7_control/config/crane_x7_control.yamlのハンド部分について次のように変更する gripper_controller: type: "position_controllers/GripperActionController" type: "effort_controllers/GripperActionController" publish_rate: joint: crane_x7_gripper_finger_a_joint gains: crane_x7_gripper_finger_a_joint: action_monitor_rate: state_publish_rate: stall_velocity_threshold: goal_tolerance: stall_timeout: dynamixel_port: port_name: "/dev/ttyUSB0" baud_rate: joints: crane_x7_shoulder_fixed_part_pan_joint crane_x7_shoulder_revolute_part_tilt_joint crane_x7_upper_arm_revolute_part_twist_joint crane_x7_upper_arm_revolute_part_rotate_joint crane_x7_lower_arm_fixed_part_joint crane_x7_lower_arm_revolute_part_joint crane_x7_wrist_joint crane_x7_gripper_finger_a_joint crane_x7_shoulder_fixed_part_pan_joint: center: home: effort_const: mode: crane_x7_shoulder_revolute_part_tilt_joint: center: home: effort_const: mode: crane_x7_upper_arm_revolute_part_twist_joint: center: home: effort_const: mode: crane_x7_upper_arm_revolute_part_rotate_joint: center: home: effort_const: mode: crane_x7_lower_arm_fixed_part_joint: center: home: effort_const: mode: crane_x7_lower_arm_revolute_part_joint: center: home: effort_const: mode: crane_x7_wrist_joint: center: home: effort_const: mode: crane_x7_gripper_finger_a_joint: center: home: effort_const: mode: crane_x7_gripper_finger_a_joint: center: home: effort_const: mode: PIDの設定値はCRANE-X7を制御するROS環境によって特性が異なる可能性があります。

name: cv_camera
description:
cv_camera uses OpenCV capture object to capture camera image.
  This supports camera_image and nodelet.
readme: ROS OpenCV camera driver easy capture video device cv::VideoCapture OpenCV. searching ROS2 driver, check cv_camera_node node camera_info_manager dealing camera_info. calibration data set, dummy values width height. Publish ~image_raw (sensor_msgs/Image) ~camera_info (sensor_msgs/CameraInfo) Service ~set_camera_info (sensor_msgs/SetCameraInfo) Parameters ~rate (double, default: publish rate ~device_id (int, default: capture device ~device_path (string, default: path camera device file, /dev/video0. ~frame_id (string, default: “camera”) frame_id message header. ~image_width (int) set capture image width. ~image_height (int) set capture image height. ~camera_info_url (string) url camera info yaml. ~file (string, default: movie file device. ~capture_delay (double, default: estimated duration capturing receiving image. ~rescale_camera_info (bool, default: false) rescale camera calibration info automatically. ~camera_name (bool, default: frame_id) camera camera_info_manager. Supports CV_CAP_PROP_*, params. ~cv_cap_prop_pos_msec (double) ~cv_cap_prop_pos_avi_ratio (double) ~cv_cap_prop_frame_width (double) ~cv_cap_prop_frame_height (double) ~cv_cap_prop_fps (double) ~cv_cap_prop_fourcc (double) ~cv_cap_prop_frame_count (double) ~cv_cap_prop_format (double) ~cv_cap_prop_mode (double) ~cv_cap_prop_brightness (double) ~cv_cap_prop_contrast (double) ~cv_cap_prop_saturation (double) ~cv_cap_prop_hue (double) ~cv_cap_prop_gain (double) ~cv_cap_prop_exposure (double) ~cv_cap_prop_convert_rgb (double) ~cv_cap_prop_rectification (double) ~cv_cap_prop_iso_speed (double) supports props. Hernan Badino! ~property_$(i)_code (int) set code property ~property_$(i)_value, start ~property_$(i)_value (double) set ~property_$(i)_code set property code rosrun cv_camera cv_camera_node _property_0_code:=404 _property_0_value:=1 set ~property_1_code ~property_1_value. Nodelet node works nodelet (cv_camera/CvCameraNodelet). Contributors I’ll review code consistency, patient. Oleg Kalachev Mikael Arguedas Maurice Meedendorp Max Schettler Lukas Bulwahn

Subscribed Topics ulc_cmd (dataspeed_ulc_msgs/UlcCmd) Input command ULC. addition speed steering command inputs, topic configures behavior ULC. user turn speed steering components ULC switch shifting steering modes, configure longitudinal lateral acceleration limits. cmd_vel (geometry_msgs/Twist) Simplified interface ULC. messages topic command ULC: speed component ULC activated tracks linear.x field geometry_msgs/Twist message, assuming units steering component ULC activated yaw rate mode tracks angular.z field geometry_msgs/Twist message, assuming units rad/s longitudinal lateral acceleration limits default settings outlined ULC User’s Guide cmd_vel_stamped (geometry_msgs/TwistStamped) header incoming geometry_msgs/TwistStamped messages treated identically cmd_vel topic. can_rx (can_msgs/Frame) topic listens traffic Dataspeed ADAS Kit bus data publish messages ulc_report topic. Published Topics can_tx (can_msgs/Frame) topic transmits ULC command configuration messages Dataspeed ADAS Kit bus control ULC running ADAS Kit firmware. messages constructed based user publishing command messages input topics. ulc_report(dataspeed_ulc_msgs/UlcReport) Feedback data ULC running ADAS Kit firmware.

name: ddynamic_reconfigure
description: The ddynamic_reconfigure package
readme: #ddynamic_reconfigure ddynamic_reconfigure package extension dynamic_reconfigure modifying parameters ROS node dynamic_reconfigure framework write cfg files. Usage Modifying place variable: #include <ros/ros.h> #include <ddynamic_reconfigure/ddynamic_reconfigure.h> int main(int argc, char **argv) ROS init stage ros::init(argc, argv, "ddynamic_tutorials"); ros::NodeHandle ddynamic_reconfigure::DDynamicReconfigure ddr; int int_param ddr.registerVariable<int>("int_param", &int_param, "param description"); ddr.publishServicesTopics(); parameter modified dynamic_reconfigure GUI tools variable int_param updated automatically int_param update dynamic_reconfigure tools ros::spin(); return Modifying variable callback: #include <ros/ros.h> #include <ddynamic_reconfigure/ddynamic_reconfigure.h> int global_int; void paramCb(int new_value) global_int new_value; ROS_INFO("Param modified"); int main(int argc, char **argv) ROS init stage ros::init(argc, argv, "ddynamic_tutorials"); ros::NodeHandle ddynamic_reconfigure::DDynamicReconfigure ddr; ddr.registerVariable<int>("int_param", initial boost::bind(paramCb, "param description"); ddr.publishServicesTopics(); parameter modified dynamic_reconfigure GUI tools callback called update ros::spin(); return Registering enum: #include <ros/ros.h> #include <ddynamic_reconfigure/ddynamic_reconfigure.h> int main(int argc, char **argv) ROS init stage ros::init(argc, argv, "ddynamic_tutorials"); ros::NodeHandle ddynamic_reconfigure::DDynamicReconfigure ddr; std::map<std::string, std::string> enum_map {{"Key {"Key std::string enum_value enum_map["Key ddr.registerEnumVariable<std::string>("string_enum", &enum_value,"param description", enum_map); ddr.publishServicesTopics(); ros::spin(); return Issues Undefined reference registerVariable registerEnumVariable methods templated, implementation hidden, explicit template instantiations int, bool, double std::string. undefined reference methods, passing parameters type.

name: ddynamic_reconfigure_python
description:
The ddynamic_reconfigure_python package contains
    a class to instantiate dynamic reconfigure servers on the fly
    registering variables
readme: Dynamic dynamic reconfigure python Register variables dynamic reconfigure server run server fly. .cfg files CMakeLists.txt tinkering! import rospy ddynamic_reconfigure_python.ddynamic_reconfigure import DDynamicReconfigure def dyn_rec_callback(config, level): rospy.loginfo("Received reconf call: str(config)) return config '__main__': rospy.init_node('test_ddynrec') Create D(ynamic)DynamicReconfigure ddynrec DDynamicReconfigure("example_dyn_rec") Add variables description, default min, max, edit_method) ddynrec.add_variable("decimal", "float/double variable", ddynrec.add_variable("integer", "integer variable", ddynrec.add_variable("bool", "bool variable", True) ddynrec.add_variable("string", "string variable", "string dynamic variable") enum_method ddynrec.enum([ ddynrec.const("Small", "int", small constant"), ddynrec.const("Medium", "int", medium constant"), ddynrec.const("Large", "int", large constant"), ddynrec.const("ExtraLarge", "int", extra large constant")], enum ddynrec.add_variable("enumerate", "enumerate variable", edit_method=enum_method) Start server ddynrec.start(dyn_rec_callback) rospy.spin() rosrun ddynamic_reconfigure_python example.py. Run rosrun rqt_reconfigure rqt_reconfigure play values. couple examples class scripts folder.

name: dynamixel_workbench
description:
Dynamixel-Workbench is dynamixel solution for ROS.
    This metapackage allows you to easily change the ID, baudrate and operating mode of the Dynamixel.
    Furthermore, it supports various controllers based on operating mode and Dynamixel SDK.
    These controllers are commanded by operators.

description: This package contains examples of applying the 'dynamixel_workbench_toolbox' library developed on the basis of dynamixel_sdk to various operating modes of Dynamixel.

name: dynamixel_workbench_toolbox
description:
This package is composed of 'dynamixel_item', 'dynamixel_tool', 'dynamixel_driver' and 'dynamixel_workbench' class.
    The 'dynamixel_item' is saved as control table item and information of Dynamixels.
    The 'dynamixel_tool' class loads its by model number of Dynamixels.
    The 'dynamixel_driver' class includes wraped function used in DYNAMIXEL SDK.
    The 'dynamixel_workbench' class make simple to use Dynamixels
readme: run dynamixel_workbench linux ROS Downloads DynamixelSDK git clone https://github.com/ROBOTIS-GIT/DynamixelSDK Build DynamixelSDK http://emanual.robotis.com/docs/en/software/dynamixel/dynamixel_sdk/library_setup/cpp_linux/#build-the-library Downloads Dynamixel-Workbench git clone https://github.com/ROBOTIS-GIT/dynamixel-workbench Build Dynamixel-Workbench Run examples ${YOUR_DOWNLOAD_PATH}/dynamixel_workbench/dynamixel_workbench_toolbox/examples mkdir build build cmake sudo chmod a+rw ${USB_PORT} ./monitor

name: ecl_command_line
description:
Embeds the TCLAP library inside the ecl. This is a very convenient
     command line parser in templatised c++.

name: ecl_concepts
description:
Introduces a compile time concept checking mechanism that can be used
     most commonly to check for required functionality when passing
     template arguments.

name: ecl_containers
description:
The containers included here are intended to extend the stl containers.
    In all cases, these implementations are designed to implement
    c++ conveniences and safety where speed is not sacrificed. 

    Also includes techniques for memory debugging of common problems such
    as buffer overruns.

name: ecl_converters
description:
Some fast/convenient type converters, mostly for char strings or strings.
     These are not really fully fleshed out, alot of them could use the addition for
     the whole range of fundamental types (e.g. all integers, not just int, unsigned int).
     
     They will come as the need arises.

name: ecl_core
description:
A set of tools and interfaces extending the capabilities of c++ to 
    provide a lightweight, consistent interface with a focus for control
    programming.

name: ecl_core_apps
description:
This includes a suite of programs demo'ing various aspects of the
     ecl_core. It also includes various benchmarking and utility programs for
     use primarily with embedded systems.

name: ecl_devices
description: Provides an extensible and standardised framework for input-output devices.

name: ecl_eigen
description: This provides an Eigen implementation for ecl's linear algebra.

name: ecl_exceptions
description:
Template based exceptions - these are simple and practical
     and avoid the proliferation of exception types. Although not
     syntatactically ideal, it is convenient and eminently practical.

name: ecl_filesystem
description: Cross platform filesystem utilities (until c++11 makes its way in).

name: ecl_formatters
description:
The formatters here simply format various input types to a specified
   text format. They can be used with most streaming types (including both
   ecl and stl streams).

name: ecl_geometry
description:
Any tools relating to mathematical geometry. 
     Primarily featuring polynomials and interpolations.

name: ecl_ipc
description:
Interprocess mechanisms vary greatly across platforms - sysv, posix, win32, there
  are more than a few. This package provides an infrastructure to allow for developing 
  cross platform c++ wrappers around the lower level c api's that handle these 
  mechanisms. These make it not only easier to utilise such mechanisms, but allow it 
  to be done consistently across platforms.

name: ecl_linear_algebra
description: Ecl frontend to a linear matrix package (currently eigen).

name: ecl_math
description:
This package provides simple support to cmath, filling in holes
    or redefining in a c++ formulation where desirable.

name: ecl_mpl
description:
Metaprogramming tools move alot of runtime calculations to be shifted to
    compile time. This has only very elementary structures at this stage.

name: ecl_sigslots
description:
Provides a signal/slot mechanism (in the same vein as qt sigslots, 
     boost::signals etc for intra-process communication. These include 
     some improvements - they do not need a preprocessor, are fully type safe,
     allow for simple connections via a posix style string identifier 
     and are multithread-safe.

name: ecl_statistics
description: Common statistical structures and algorithms for control systems.

name: ecl_streams
description:
These are lightweight text streaming classes that connect to standardised
     ecl type devices.

name: ecl_threads
description:
This package provides the c++ extensions for a variety of threaded 
     programming tools. These are usually different on different 
     platforms, so the architecture for a cross-platform framework
     is also implemented.

name: ecl_time
description:
Timing utilities are very dependent on the system api provided for their use.
	This package provides a means for handling different timing models. Current support
	
	- posix rt : complete.
	- macosx : posix timers only, missing absolute timers.
	- win : none.

name: ecl_type_traits
description: Extends c++ type traits and implements a few more to boot.

name: ecl_utilities
description: Includes various supporting tools and utilities for c++ programming.

name: ecl_config
description:
These tools inspect and describe your system with macros, types 
     and functions.

name: ecl_console
description: Color codes for ansii consoles.

name: ecl_converters_lite
description:
These are a very simple version of some of the functions in ecl_converters 
     suitable for firmware development. That is, there is no use of new, 
     templates or exceptions.

name: ecl_errors
description:
This library provides lean and mean error mechanisms. 
    It includes c style error functions as well as a few 
    useful macros. For higher level mechanisms, 
    refer to ecl_exceptions.

name: ecl_io
description:
Most implementations (windows, posix, ...) have slightly different api for 
     low level input-output functions. These are gathered here and re-represented 
     with a cross platform set of functions.

name: ecl_lite
description: Libraries and utilities for embedded and low-level linux development.

name: ecl_sigslots_lite
description:
This avoids use of dynamic storage (malloc/new) and thread safety (mutexes) to
     provide a very simple sigslots implementation that can be used for *very*
     embedded development.

name: ecl_time_lite
description:
Provides a portable set of time functions that are especially useful for 
     porting other code or being wrapped by higher level c++ classes.

name: ecl
description: Metapackage bringing all of ecl together.

name: ecl_manipulation
description:
Includes basic manipulation related structures as well as a few 
    algorithms currently used at Yujin Robot.

name: ecl_manipulators
description:
Deploys various manipulation algorithms, currently just 
    feedforward filters (interpolations).

name: ecl_mobile_robot
description:
Contains transforms (e.g. differential drive inverse kinematics)
    for the various types of mobile robot platforms.

name: ecl_navigation
description:
This stack aims to bring the common tools and algorithms needed to
    develop navigation algorithms, in particular slam. It does not focus on
    the end-point solution, rather the tools needed to create a variety of
    end-point solutions.

name: ecl_build
description:
Collection of cmake/make build tools primarily for ecl development itself, but also
     contains a few cmake modules useful outside of the ecl.

name: ecl_license
description:
Maintains the ecl licenses and also provides an install
     target for deploying licenses with the ecl libraries.

name: ecl_tools
description: Tools and utilities for ecl development.

name: eigen_stl_containers
description:
This package provides a set of typedef's that allow
  using Eigen datatypes in STL containers

name: eigenpy
description: Bindings between Numpy and Eigen using Boost.Python
readme: EigenPy Versatile efficient Python bindings Numpy Eigen EigenPy open-source framework binding famous Eigen library Python Boost.Python. EigenPy full memory sharing Numpy Eigen, avoiding memory allocation full support Eigen::Ref avoiding memory allocation full support Eigen::Tensor module exposition Geometry module Eigen easy code prototyping standard matrix decomposion routines Eigen Cholesky decomposition (SVD decompositions added) full support SWIG objects full support runtime declaration Numpy scalar types extended API expose STL types Boost equivalents: optional types, std::pair, maps, variants… full support vectorization Python hold objects properly aligned memory) Installation installation EigenPy computer easy Linux/BSD, Mac Windows environments. Conda simply simple line: conda install eigenpy conda-forge Ubuntu easily install EigenPy binaries. Add robotpkg apt repository Add robotpkg source repository apt: sudo "echo 'deb [arch=amd64] http://robotpkg.openrobots.org/packages/debian/pub $(lsb_release robotpkg' /etc/apt/sources.list.d/robotpkg.list" Register authentication certificate robotpkg: curl http://robotpkg.openrobots.org/packages/debian/robotpkg.key sudo apt-key add run apt update fetch package descriptions: sudo apt-get update Install EigenPy installation EigenPy dependencies line: sudo apt install robotpkg-py35-eigenpy replaced Python work robotpkg-py36-eigenpy work Python Mac installation EigenPy Mac HomeBrew. register tap software repository. brew tap gepetto/homebrew-gepetto install EigenPy Python brew install eigenpy Build Build instruction Credits people involved development EigenPy: Justin Carpentier (Inria): main developer manager project Nicolas Mansard (LAAS-CNRS): initial project instructor Wolfgang Merkt (University Edinburgh): ROS integration support Sean Yen (Microsoft): Windows integration Loïc Estève (Inria): Conda integration Wilson Jallet (Inria/LAAS-CNRS): core developer Joris Vaillant (Inria): core developer manager project part development EigenPy, feel free add contribution Acknowledgments development EigenPy supported Gepetto team @LAAS-CNRS Willow team @INRIA.

name: executive_smach
description:
This metapackage depends on the SMACH library and ROS SMACH integration
    packages.

name: smach
description:
SMACH is a task-level architecture for rapidly creating complex robot
    behavior. At its core, SMACH is a ROS-independent Python library to build
    hierarchical state machines. SMACH is a new library that takes advantage of
    very old concepts in order to quickly create robust robot behavior with
    maintainable and modular code.

name: smach_msgs
description:
this package contains a set of messages that are used by the introspection
    interfaces for smach.

name: smach_ros
description:
The smach_ros package contains extensions for the SMACH library to
    integrate it tightly with ROS.  For example, SMACH-ROS can call
    ROS services, listen to ROS topics, and integrate
    with

description: This metapackage depends on the SMACH visualization tools.
maintainers: Jonathan Bohren, ROS Orphaned Package Maintainers

name: smach_viewer
description:
The smach viewer is a GUI that shows the state of hierarchical
    SMACH state machines. It can visualize the possible transitions
    between states, as well as the currently active state and the
    values of user data that is passed around between states. The
    smach viewer uses the SMACH debugging interface based on
    the

name: tf
description:
tf is a package that lets the user keep track of multiple coordinate
frames over time. tf maintains the relationship between coordinate
frames in a tree structure buffered in time, and lets the user
transform points, vectors, etc between any two coordinate frames at
any desired point in time.
authors: Tully Foote, Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Wim Meeussen

name: tf_conversions
description:
This package contains a set of conversion functions to convert
common tf datatypes (point, vector, pose, etc) into semantically
identical datatypes used by other libraries. The conversion functions
make it easier for users of the transform library (tf) to work with
the datatype of their choice. Currently this package has support for
the Kinematics and Dynamics Library (KDL) and the Eigen matrix
library. This package is stable, and will get integrated into tf in
the next major release cycle (see roadmap).

name: hector_gazebo
description: hector_gazebo provides packages related to to simulation of robots using gazebo (gazebo plugins, world files etc.)

name: hector_gazebo_plugins
description:
hector_gazebo_plugins provides gazebo plugins from Team Hector.
     Currently it contains a 6wd differential drive plugin, an IMU sensor plugin,
     an earth magnetic field sensor plugin, a GPS sensor plugin and a
     sonar ranger plugin.

name: hector_gazebo_thermal_camera
description: hector_gazebo_thermal_camera provides a gazebo plugin that produces simulated thermal camera images. The plugin uses modified code from the gazebo_ros_camera plugin.

name: hector_gazebo_worlds
description: hector_gazebo_worlds provides gazebo scenarios used by Team Hector Darmstadt

name: hector_sensors_gazebo
description: hector_sensors_gazebo depends on the necessary plugins for using the sensors from the hector_models repository.

name: hector_localization
description:
The hector_localization stack is a collection of packages, that provide the full 6DOF pose of a robot or platform.
    It uses various sensor sources, which are fused using an Extended Kalman filter.

    Acceleration and angular rates from an inertial measurement unit (IMU) serve as primary measurements.
    The usage of other sensors is application-dependent. The hector_localization stack currently supports
    GPS, magnetometer, barometric pressure sensors and other external sources that provide a geometry_msgs/PoseWithCovariance
    message via the poseupdate topic.

name: hector_pose_estimation
description: hector_pose_estimation provides the hector_pose_estimation node and the hector_pose_estimation nodelet.

name: hector_pose_estimation_core
description:
hector_pose_estimation_core is the core package of the hector_localization stack. It contains the Extended Kalman Filter (EKF)
    that estimates the 6DOF pose of the robot. hector_pose_estimation can be used either as a library, as a nodelet or as a standalone node.

name: message_to_tf
description:
message_to_tf translates pose information from different kind of common_msgs message types to tf. Currently the node supports nav_msgs/Odometry, geometry_msgs/PoseStamped and sensor_msgs/Imu messages as input.
    The resulting transform is divided into three subtransforms with intermediate frames for the footprint and the stabilized base frame (without roll and pitch).

name: hector_components_description
description: hector_components_description contains URDF xacro macros for robot components, so they are easily attachable to robot models.

name: hector_models
description: hector_models contains (urdf) models of robots, sensors etc.

name: hector_sensors_description
description: hector_sensors_description contains URDF xacro macros for sensors, so they are easily attachable to robot models and usable in gazebo.

name: hector_xacro_tools
description: hector_xacro_tools

name: hector_compressed_map_transport
description: hector_compressed_map_transport provides means for transporting compressed map data through the use of image_transport.

name: hector_geotiff
description: hector_geotiff provides a node that can be used to save occupancy grid map, robot trajectory and object of interest data to RoboCup Rescue compliant GeoTiff images.

name: hector_geotiff_plugins
description: hector_geotiff_plugins contains plugins that extend geotiff maps generated by hector_geotiff.

name: hector_imu_attitude_to_tf
description: hector_imu_attitude_to_tf is a lightweight node that can be used to publish the roll/pitch attitude angles reported via a imu message to tf.

name: hector_imu_tools
description: hector_imu_tools provides some tools for processing IMU messages

name: hector_map_server
description:
hector_map_server provides a service for retrieving the map, as well as for raycasting based obstacle queries (finds next obstacle in the map, given start and endpoint
    in any tf coordinate frame).

name: hector_map_tools
description:
hector_map_tools contains some functions related to accessing information from OccupancyGridMap maps.
    Currently consists of a single header.

name: hector_mapping
description:
hector_mapping is a SLAM approach that can be used without odometry as well as on platforms that exhibit roll/pitch motion (of the sensor, the platform or both).
    It leverages the high update rate of modern LIDAR systems like the Hokuyo UTM-30LX and provides 2D pose estimates at scan rate of the sensors (40Hz for the UTM-30LX).
    While the system does not provide explicit loop closing ability, it is sufficiently accurate for many real world scenarios. The system has successfully been used on
    Unmanned Ground Robots, Unmanned Surface Vehicles, Handheld Mapping Devices and logged data from quadrotor UAVs.

name: hector_marker_drawing
description: hector_marker_drawing provides convenience functions for easier publishing of visualization markers.

name: hector_nav_msgs
description: hector_nav_msgs contains messages and services used in the hector_slam stack.

name: hector_slam
description: The hector_slam metapackage that installs hector_mapping and related packages.

name: hector_slam_launch
description: hector_slam_launch contains launch files for launching hector_slam with different robot systems/setups/postprocessing scenarios.

name: hector_trajectory_server
description: hector_trajectory_server keeps track of tf trajectories extracted from tf data and makes this data accessible via a service and topic.

name: hls_lfcd_lds_driver
description:
ROS package for LDS(HLS-LFCD2).
    The LDS (Laser Distance Sensor) is a sensor sending the data to Host for the simultaneous localization and mapping (SLAM). Simultaneously the detecting obstacle data can also be sent to Host. HLDS(Hitachi-LG Data Storage) is developing the technology for the moving platform sensor such as Robot Vacuum Cleaners, Home Robot, Robotics Lawn Mower Sensor, etc.

This package provides a C++ interface for camera calibration
     information.  It provides CameraInfo, and handles SetCameraInfo
     service requests, saving and restoring the camera calibration
     data.

name: image_common
description: Common code for working with images in ROS.

name: image_transport
description:
image_transport should always be used to subscribe to and publish images. It provides transparent
     support for transporting images in low-bandwidth compressed formats. Examples (provided by separate
     plugin packages) include JPEG/PNG compression and Theora streaming video.

name: polled_camera
description:
polled_camera contains a service and C++ helper classes for implementing a polled
     camera driver node and requesting images from it. The package is currently for
     internal use as the API is still under development.

name: camera_calibration
description:
camera_calibration allows easy calibration of monocular or stereo
     cameras using a checkerboard calibration target.

name: depth_image_proc
description:
Contains nodelets for processing depth images such as those
     produced by OpenNI camera. Functions include creating disparity
     images and point clouds, as well as registering (reprojecting)
     a depth image into another camera frame.

name: image_pipeline
description: image_pipeline fills the gap between getting raw images from a camera driver and higher-level vision processing.

name: image_proc
description: Single image rectification and color processing.
authors: Patrick Mihelich, Kurt Konolige, Jeremy Leibs

name: image_view
description:
A simple viewer for ROS image topics. Includes a specialized viewer
  for stereo + disparity images.

name: stereo_image_proc
description: Stereo and single image rectification and disparity processing.
authors: Patrick Mihelich, Kurt Konolige, Jeremy Leibs

name: compressed_depth_image_transport
description:
Compressed_depth_image_transport provides a plugin to image_transport for transparently sending
    depth images (raw, floating-point) using PNG compression.

name: compressed_image_transport
description:
Compressed_image_transport provides a plugin to image_transport for transparently sending images
    encoded as JPEG or PNG.

name: image_transport_plugins
description:
A set of plugins for publishing and subscribing to sensor_msgs/Image topics
    in representations other than raw pixel data. For example, for viewing a
    stream of images off-robot, a video codec will give much lower bandwidth
    and latency. For low frame rate tranport of high-definition images, you
    might prefer sending them as JPEG or PNG-compressed form.

name: theora_image_transport
description:
Theora_image_transport provides a plugin to image_transport for
    transparently sending an image stream encoded with the Theora codec.

name: joy
description:
ROS driver for a generic Linux joystick.
    The joy package contains joy_node, a node that interfaces a
    generic Linux joystick to ROS. This node publishes a "Joy"
    message, which contains the current state of each one of the
    joystick's buttons and axes.
authors: Morgan Quigley, Brian Gerkey, Kevin Watts, Blaise Gassend
readme: ROS Driver Generic Linux Joysticks joy package joy_node, node interfaces generic Linux joystick ROS. node publishes “Joy” message, current state joystick’s buttons axes. Supported Hardware node work joystick supported Linux. Published Topics joy (sensor_msgs/Joy): outputs joystick state. INFO]: joystick: Microsoft X-Box pad (/dev/input/js0). [ERROR]: find joystick Falling default device. node started device list. rosrun joy joy_node _dev_name:="Microsoft X-Box pad" Advanced Parameters ~deadzone (double, default: Amount joystick move considered off-center. parameter relative axis normalized means joystick move edge axis’s range axis output non-zero Linux deadzone processing, cases set ~autorepeat_rate (double, default: (disabled)) Rate joystick non-changing state resend previously message. ~coalesce_interval (double, default: Axis events received coalesce_interval (seconds) single ROS message. kernel sends axis motion separate event, coalescing greatly reduces rate messages option limit rate outgoing messages. name: ps3joy
description:
Playstation 3 SIXAXIS or DUAL SHOCK 3 joystick driver.
    Driver for the Sony PlayStation 3 SIXAXIS or DUAL SHOCK 3
    joysticks. In its current state, this driver is not compatible
    with the use of other Bluetooth HID devices. The driver listens
    for a connection on the HID ports, starts the joystick
    streaming data, and passes the data to the Linux uinput device
    so that it shows up as a normal joystick.
authors: Blaise Gassend, pascal <AT> pabr.org, Melonee Wise
readme: PlayStation Joystick Driver ROS package driver (SIXAXIS DUALSHOCK3) bluetooth joystick. driver reliable connection, access joystick’s accelerometers gyroscope. Linux’s native support joystick lacks functionality. Replace joystick’s mac address command: sudo rosrun ps3joy sixpair Starting joystick Run command rosrun ps3joy ps3joy.py Open terminal reboot bluez run joy sudo systemctl restart bluetooth rosrun joy joy_node Open terminal echo joy topic rostopic echo joy joystick /dev/input/js? check working jstest /dev/input/js? name: wiimote
description:
The wiimote package allows ROS nodes to communicate with a Nintendo Wiimote
    and its related peripherals, including the Nunchuk, Motion Plus, and
    (experimentally) the Classic. The package implements a ROS node that uses
    Bluetooth to communicate with the Wiimote device, obtaining accelerometer
    and gyro data, the state of LEDs, the IR camera, rumble (vibrator),
    buttons, joystick, and battery state. The node additionally enables ROS
    nodes to control the Wiimote's LEDs and vibration for feedback to the human
    Wiimote operator. LEDs and vibration may be switched on and off, or made to
    operate according to a timed pattern.
authors: Andreas Paepcke, Melonee Wise, Mark Horn
readme: Wiimote Nodes Additional Documentation Testing procedure ROS Wiki Tutorials Teleop Wiimote wiimote_node.py Original Python version wiimote node. wiimote_node implementation designed focus reduced resource consumption.   implementation of the SLIC Superpixel algorithm by Achanta et al. [PAMI'12,
  vol. 34, num. 11, pp. 2274-2282].

  This implementation is created for the specific purpose of creating
  over-segmentations in an OpenCV-based environment.

name: google_chat_ros
description: Use Google Chat API clients via ROS
readme: Google Chat ROS ROS wrapper Google Chat API Installation Guide Sending message Receiving message Handling event Optional functions Helper nodes Installation Guide API KEY permission access Google Chat API. Google Official Document. ensure JSON credetial file save LOST JSK members, keys Google Drive. API keys, upload Select recieve Google Chat event recieve Google Chat event API server depends system. system static allowed recieve https request specific port, HTTPS mode. Pub/Sub mode. HTTPS mode send message, node Google REST API. recieve message, Google Chat API sends https request machine node handles prepare SSL certificate. Self-signed Google security issue. service Let’s Encrypt. Google Cloud console, choose App URL connection settings fill URL App URL form. Pub/Sub mode send message, node Google REST API. recieve message, node Google Pub/Sub API’s subscription. node established connection Google Pub/Sub API launch set Google Cloud console shows Authorize existing Google Chat API project access Google Cloud Pub/Sub service IAM settings console, add role Pub/Sub Admin service account. Create Pub/Sub topic subscriber Pub/Sub settings console, add topic subscriptions. figure, set topic chat, subscription chat-sub Note set topic chat, full projects/<project_name>/topics/chat. confirm subsciptions subscribes full short Grant publish rigts topic order Google Chat publish messages topic, publishing rights topic. grant Google Chat permissions, assign Pub/Sub Publisher role service account chat-api-push@system.gserviceaccount.com Set Google Chat API Connection settings choose Cloud Pub/Sub connection settings fill full topic Topic form. Install/Build ROS node build source source /opt/ros/${ROS_DISTRO}/setup.bash mkdir ~/catkin_ws/src ~/catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_3rdparty rosdep install --ignore-src --from-paths catkin build Launch node HTTPS mode set rosparams receiving_mode=https, google_cloud_credentials_json, host, port, ssl_certfile, ssl_keyfile. Pub/Sub mode set rosparams receiving_mode=pubsub, google_cloud_credentials_json, project_id, subscription_id. subscription_id chat-sub follow Pub/Sub mode roslaunch google_chat_ros google_chat.launch receiving_mode:=pubsub google_cloud_credentials_json:=/path/to/<project_id>-XXXXXXXX.json project_id:=<project_id> subscription_id:=chat-sub Sending message Understanding Google Chat Room Google Chat browsers smartphone’s apps, space, thread. send message, space thread send message space chat room’s URL. https://mail.google.com/chat/u/0/#chat/space/XXXXXXXXXXX, XXXXXXXXXXX space Message format types messages, text card. card basically original json structure. node covers units ros action msgs, complicated Examples sections, show simple Sending message actionlib send Actionlib goal ~send/goal. Examples Showing message examples rostopic pub command bash. Sending text message rostopic pub /google_chat_ros/send/goal google_chat_ros/SendMessageActionGoal "goal: text: 'Hello!' space: 'spaces/<space name>'" Sending message KeyValue card rostopic pub /google_chat_ros/send/goal google_chat_ros/SendMessageActionGoal "goal: text: 'Something FATAL errors happened computer, fix ASAP' cards: sections: widgets: key_value: top_label: 'Process ID' content: bottom_label: 'rospy' icon: 'DESCRIPTION' space: 'spaces/<space name>'" Sending Interactive button rostopic pub /google_chat_ros/send/goal google_chat_ros/SendMessageActionGoal "goal: cards: header: title: 'What eat?' subtitle: 'Please choose food shop!' sections: widgets: buttons: text_button_name: 'STARBUCKS' text_button_on_click: action: action_method_name: 'vote_starbucks' parameters: key: 'shop' 'starbucks' text_button_name: 'SUBWAY' text_button_on_click: action: action_method_name: 'vote_subway' parameters: key: 'shop' 'subway' space: 'spaces/<space name>'" Sending message image Receiving messages ROS Topic bot mentioned, node publishes ~message_activity topic. readme: respeaker_ros ROS Package Respeaker Mic Array Supported Devices Respeaker Mic Array Preparation Install package Assumed ROS properly installed. install package apt-get. sudo apt-get install ros-$ROS_DISTRO-respeaker-ros build source. mkdir ~/catkin_ws/src ~/catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_3rdparty.git ~/catkin_ws source /opt/ros/kinetic/setup.bash rosdep install --from-paths src catkin config --init catkin build respeaker_ros source ~/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash Register respeaker udev rules access USB device permission user space. udev, give permission respeaker device automatically. run command followings install setting file: roscd respeaker_ros sudo $(rospack find respeaker_ros)/config/60-respeaker.rules /etc/udev/rules.d/60-respeaker.rules sudo systemctl restart udev re-connect device. Update firmware git clone https://github.com/respeaker/usb_4_mic_array.git usb_4_mic_array sudo python dfu.py --download 6_channels_firmware.bin channels version Run executables roslaunch respeaker_ros sample_respeaker.launch rostopic echo /sound_direction Result DoA rostopic echo /sound_localization Result DoA Pose rostopic echo /is_speeching Result VAD rostopic echo /audio Raw audio rostopic echo /speech_audio Audio data speeching rostopic echo /speech_to_text Voice recognition Voice recognition result Japanese rostopic echo --filter "print(m.transcript[0])" /speech_to_text rostopic echo --filter "print('transcript: [%s]\n---'%(', '.join(map(str, m.transcript))))" /speech_to_text rostopic echo /speech_to_text ascii2uni set parameters `dynamic_reconfigure`. sudo apt install ros-kinetic-rqt-reconfigure Install rosrun rqt_reconfigure rqt_reconfigure set LED color, publish desired color: rostopic pub /status_led std_msgs/ColorRGBA cases Voice Recognition ros_speech_recognition julius_ros Notes configuration file dynamic_reconfigure package created automatically reading parameters devices. rare case, configuration file updated followings: Connect device computer. Run generator script. rosrun respeaker_ros respeaker_gencfg.py updated configuration file $(rospack find respeaker_ros)/cfg/Respeaker.cfg. Author Yuki Furuta «furushchev@jsk.imi.i.u-tokyo.ac.jp» License Apache License
