name: cmd_vel_smoother
description: The cmd_vel_smoother package
readme: cmd_vel_smoother Interpolate velocity command published periodically acceleration deceleration limit. Basically node interpolates velocity violate acceleration limit decelating received velocity. control loop base_controller cmd_vel seconds, controller stops moving motors safety. move_base node publishes cmd_vel continues publishing, controllers send signal motors stop “rattle” moves suddenly stops repeatedly) damages motors hardwares. Parameters ~x_acc_lim (Double, default: limit x-axis translational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~y_acc_lim (Double, default: limit y-axis translational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~yaw_acc_lim (Double, default: limit z-axis rotational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~desired_rate (Double, default: node interpolates velocity commands rate ~interpolate_max_frame (Int, default: Max message count published received velocity command. Discussion package originated discussion https://github.com/ros-planning/navigation/issues/405 looked detail implementation similar nodes turtlebot robots exist: https://github.com/yujinrobot/yujin_ocs/tree/devel/yocs_velocity_smoother Author Yuki Furuta «furushchev@jsk.imi.i.u-tokyo.ac.jp»

name: contact_states_observer
description: The contact_states_observer package
readme: contact_states_observer Euslisp server check grasping contact states. Grasp state checking grasping state checking, hand joint angle topics. users configure setting :set-grasp-state-param joint angles success-candidates failure-candidates checked joint names. observer program checks difference candidates current hand joint angles find nearest candidate. Finally, observer program publishes results users obtain :get-contact-states. Setting parameter (list (list :rarm Success failure candidates (list (list :success (list :fail Checked joint names (list rarm setting (list :larm larm setting list Contact force checking contact force state checking, force sensor thresholding. Observer program checks current force exceeds threshold. Observer program publishes results users obtain :get-contact-states. real robot Start real robot (rtcd ros bridge servoOn) Run program roseus (progn (ros::roseus "test_grasp_check") ROBOT Initialization. HRP2JSKNTS sample (load "package://hrpsys_ros_bridge_tutorials/euslisp/hrp2jsknts-interface.l") (boundp '*robot*) (hrp2jsknts-init) (setq *robot* *hrp2jsknts*)) Loading Contact State Client (load "package://contact_states_observer/euslisp/contact-states-client.l") (setq csc (instance contact-state-client :init)) Set grasp parameter (send csc :set-grasp-state-param (list check larm. returns (list :larm) Set rarm parameter. :success hand's reset-pose, flag :fail hand's hook-pose, flag Check joint angles joints (list :rarm (list (list :success (list :fail (list Test1. Set reset-pose. return '("On" (send *robot* :hand :rarm :reset-pose) (send :hand-angle-vector (send *robot* :hand-angle-vector) (send :hand-wait-interpolation) (unix:usleep Wait hand motion stopping (format '(:rarm :larm) (send csc :get-contact-states '(:rarm :larm))) Test2. Set hook-pose. return '("Off" (send *robot* :hand :rarm :hook-pose) (send :hand-angle-vector (send *robot* :hand-angle-vector) (send :hand-wait-interpolation) (unix:usleep Wait hand motion stopping (format '(:rarm :larm) (send csc :get-contact-states '(:rarm :larm))) Publishing Topics ore_contact_states (contact_states_observer/OREContactStates) Array object, robot, environment contact state. contact state consists rleg, rarm,.. state On/Off grasping, foot, hand contact) mode grasp, contact-force, contact_grasp_states (hrpsys_ros_bridge/ContactStatesStamped) Contact states contact point rleg, lleg, rarm, larm) grasp_states (contact_states_observer/GraspState) Grasp state

Export xml file ROS package manifest.xml package.xml. implement plugin Write xml file define plugins jsk_teleop_joy reads plugin definition xml file plugin.xml. xml <library> <class name="Foo" type="your_package.foo"> </class> </library> xml file <library> tag top level. define plugins <class> tag. attribute means plugin. choose plugins launch files type attribute means python class plugin. jsk_teleop_joy instantiate plugin class thie type Export xml file ROS package export xml file <export> tag <jsk_teleop_joy> tag manifest.xml package.xml. files, manifest.xml, package.xml, good examples. Implement plugin Finally, implement jsk_teleop_joy plugin. VerboseStatus Plugin good implement plugin. __init__ method plugins required inherits jsk_teleop_joy.joy_plugin.JSKJoyPlugin call JSKJoyPlugin.__init__ __init__ constructor. class VerboseStatus(jsk_teleop_joy.joy_plugin.JSKJoyPlugin): def __init__(self): jsk_teleop_joy.joy_plugin.JSKJoyPlugin.__init__(self, 'VerbosePlugin') joyCB method time jsk_teleop_joy receives /joy message, calls joyCB method active plugin. def joyCB(self, status, history): rospy.loginfo('analog left (status.left_analog_x, status.left_analog_y)) argument joyCB instance JoyStatus. JoyStatus XboxStatus, PS3Status PS3WiredStatus. means latest message /joy. classes provide interface don’t care controller user hand, argument joyCB (history), sequence JoyStatus. instance StatusHistory. means hisotry JoyStatus. SELECT button jsk_teleop_joy system occupies SELECT button SELECT button plugin. SELECT button switching plugins. MIDI controllers interactive_midi_config.py configure MIDI devices script interactively. movie Connect MIDI device. script asks device, choose device push buttons/slide faders order sensor_msgs/Joy. script maps buttons sensor_msgs/Joy/axes sensor_msgs/Joy/buttons type quit. configure saved /tmp/midi.yaml. Troubleshooting: Ubuntu18.04 pygame2.0.1 installed pip, python "import pygame.midi; pygame.midi.init()" returns error ALSA lib conf.c:3558:(snd_config_hooks_call) open shared library libasound_module_conf_pulse.so (/usr/lib/alsa-lib/libasound_module_conf_pulse.so: libasound_module_conf_pulse.so: open shared object file: file directory) ALSA lib seq.c:935:(snd_seq_open_noupdate) Unknown SEQ default case, pygame apt pip uninstall pygame apt-get install python-pygame RECOMMENDED: pip installed pygame, symbolic link /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/alsa-lib /usr/lib/alsa-lib: RECOMMED midi_write.py order control LEDs active faders, output MIDI commands computer. script midi_write.py helps dig command save configuration yaml file option. midi_config_player.py script publishes sensor_msgs/Joy /joy based yaml file configured interactive_midi_config.py midi_write.py. configs directory includes yaml files MIDI devices.

readme: pr2eus_tutorials repository tutorials pr2eus Installation play package, choose installation methods. assume ROS environment kinetic. ROS indigo distribution, replace word kinetic indigo distributions). pre-built package Install ROS Follow instruction ROS installation Install package sudo apt install ros-kinetic-pr2eus-tutorials Load ROS Environment source /opt/ros/kinetic/setup.bash source package Install ROS Follow instruction ROS installation Setup catkin workspace source /opt/ros/kinetic/setup.bash sudo apt install python-catkin-tools python-wstool python-rosdep git sudo rosdep init rosdep update Create catkin workspace download source repository mkdir ~/ros/kinetic/src ~/ros/kinetic/src wstool init wstool set jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_pr2eus --git https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_pr2eus.git master wstool update Install dependencies building package rosdep install --from-paths Build package ~/ros/kinetic catkin init catkin build Load ROS Environment source ~/ros/kinetic/devel/setup.bash Tabletop Object Grasping Demo Tabletop Object Grasping Demo Startup nodes start nodes demo. real robot real robot ssh <robot address> roslaunch pr2eus_tutorials pr2_tabletop.launch compressed topic real local machine roslaunch pr2eus_tutorials pr2_tabletop.launch remote:=true locate desk front robot put objects simulator set physics engine roslaunch argument. local machine time download materials time roslaunch pr2eus_tutorials pr2_tabletop_sim.launch physics:=dart robot spawned scene desk objects. Run demo start demo program picking objects. Start Euslisp program local machine rosrun pr2eus_tutorials pr2-tabletop-object-grasp.l Start Rviz running demo program object bounding boxes RViZ window. means robot recognizes objects individual objects camera sensor inputs. local machine rviz $(rospack find pr2eus_tutorials)/config/pr2_tabletop.rviz roslaunch pr2eus_tutorials pr2_tabletop.launch remote:=true, meaning compressed topic real execute local machine rviz $(rospack find pr2eus_tutorials)/config/pr2_tabletop_remote.rviz Additional setup Kinetic local machine real robot node, switch Tabletop Object rviz panel topic /bounding_box_interactive_marker/update /bounding_box_interactive_marker/kinetic/update local machine rosrun jsk_robot_utils marker_msg_from_indigo_to_kinetic.py rosrun topic_tools relay /bounding_box_interactive_marker/kinetic/feedback /bounding_box_interactive_marker/feedback Click object bouding Rviz click object robot pick Click object image_view2 click pixels robot pick rosrun image_view2 image_view2 image:=/kinect_head/rgb/throttled/image_rect_color camera_info:=/head_mount_kinect/rgb/camera_info roslaunch pr2eus_tutorials pr2_tabletop.launch remote:=true, execute rosrun image_view2 image_view2 image:=/kinect_head_remote/rgb/throttled/image_rect_color camera_info:=/head_mount_kinect/rgb/camera_info Step-by-step Description Demo program bottom demo program pr2-tabletop-object-grasp.l, main function demo. name: audio_to_spectrogram
description: Convert audio (audio_common_msgs/AudioData) to spectrogram (sensor_msgs/Image)
readme: audio_to_spectrogram package converts audio data time-series data) spectrum spectrogram data. Usage command, publish audio, spectrum spectrogram topics. set correct args microphone configuration, mic_sampling_rate bitdepth. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram audio_to_spectrogram.launch data conversion pipeline audio_to_spectrum.py spectrum normalized_half_spectrum log_spectrum preprocess node(s) preprocessed spectrum spectrum_to_spectrogram.py spectrogram rosbag audio. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram sample_audio_to_spectrogram.launch Audio Amplitude Spectrum Spectrogram convert data audio spectrum spectrogram data package. rosbag force torque sensor sensing drill vibration. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram sample_wrench_to_spectrogram.launch Z-axis Force Amplitude Normalized Spectrum Spectrogram Source Spectrum Spectrogram Scripts audio_to_spectrum.py script convert audio spectrum. Publishing topics ~spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data calculated audio FFT. usual “amplitude spectrum”. https://ryo-iijima.com/fftresult/ details. ~normalized_half_spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data non-negative frequencies (0Hz-Nyquist frequency)) “normalized” (consistent amplitude original signal). details. https://ryo-iijima.com/fftresult/ https://stackoverflow.com/questions/63211851/why-divide-the-output-of-numpy-fft-by-n https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_recognition/issues/2761#issue-1550715400 ~log_spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Log-scaled spectrum data. calculated applying log absolute FFT result. log applied “power spectrum”, don’t simplicity. details. https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_recognition/issues/2761#issuecomment-1445810380 http://makotomurakami.com/blog/2020/05/23/5266/ Subscribing topics ~audio (audio_common_msgs/AudioData) Audio stream data microphone. audio format wave. Parameters ~mic_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate microphone. sampling rate audio topic. ~fft_sampling_period (Double, default: Period sample audio data FFT. ~n_channel (Int, default: Number channel microphone. ~target_channel (Int, default: Target channel. ~bitdepth (Int, default: Number bits audio data. ~fft_exec_rate (Double, default: Rate execute FFT publish results. data_to_spectrum.py Generalized version audio_to_spectrum.py. script convert multiple message types spectrum. Publishing topics audio_to_spectrum.py. Subscribing topics ~input (AnyMsg) Topic message including data convert spectrum published. Parameters ~expression_to_get_data (String, default: m.data) Python expression data input message input std_msgs/Float64, m.data. accessing field recommended. complex calculation numpy), transform topic_tools node. ~data_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate input data. ~fft_sampling_period (Double, default: Period sample input data FFT. ~fft_exec_rate (Double, default: Rate execute FFT publish results. ~is_integer (Bool, default: false) input data integer input std_msgs/Float64, false. ~is_signed (Bool, default: true) input data signed input std_msgs/Float64, true. ~bitdepth (Int, default: Number bits input data. input std_msgs/Float64, ~n_channel (Int, default: input scalar, input flattened matrix, number channel original matrix. ~target_channel (Int, default: input scalar, input flattened matrix, target channel. spectrum_filter.py script filter spectrum. Publishing topics ~output (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Filtered spectrum data (low_cut_freq-high_cut_freq). Subscribing topics ~input (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Original spectrum data. Parameters ~data_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate data generation original spectrum data. ~high_cut_freq (Int, default: Threshold limit maximum frequency output spectrum. ~low_cut_freq (Int, default: Threshold limit minimum frequency output spectrum. spectrum_to_spectrogram.py script convert spectrum spectrogram. Publishing topics ~spectrogram (sensor_msgs/Image) Spectrogram data, concatenation spectrum time series. Image format Subscribing topics ~spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data calculated audio FFT. Parameters ~image_height (Int, default: Number vertical (frequency axis) pixels output spectrogram. ~image_width (Int, default: Number horizontal (time axis) pixels output spectrogram. ~spectrogram_period (Double, default: Period store spectrum data create spectrogram topic. ~publish_rate (Double, default: image_width spectrogram_period) Publish rate spectrogram topic. audio_amplitude_plot.py script publish audio amplitude plot image. Publishing topics ~output/viz (sensor_msgs/Image) Audio amplitude plot image. Subscribing topics ~audio (audio_common_msgs/AudioData) Audio stream data microphone. audio format wave. Parameters ~mic_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate microphone. sampling rate audio topic. ~n_channel (Int, default: Number channel microphone. ~target_channel (Int, default: Target channel. ~bitdepth (Int, default: Number bits audio data. ~maximum_amplitude (Double, default: Maximum range amplitude plot. ~window_size (Double, default: Window size sound input plot. ~rate (Double, default: Publish rate audio amplitude image topic. data_amplitude_plot.py Generalized version audio_amplitude_plot.py. Publishing topics ~output/viz (sensor_msgs/Image) Data amplitude plot image. Subscribing topics ~input (AnyMsg) Topic message including data amplitude plot published. Parameters ~expression_to_get_data (String, default: m.data) Python expression data input message input std_msgs/Float64, m.data. accessing field recommended. complex calculation numpy), transform topic_tools node. ~data_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate input data. ~is_integer (Bool, default: false) input data integer input std_msgs/Float64, false. ~is_signed (Bool, default: true) input data signed input std_msgs/Float64, true. ~bitdepth (Int, default: Number bits input data. input std_msgs/Float64, ~n_channel (Int, default: input scalar, input flattened matrix, number channel original matrix. ~target_channel (Int, default: input scalar, input flattened matrix, target channel. ~maximum_amplitude (Double, default: Maximum range amplitude plot. ~window_size (Double, default: Window size input data plot. ~rate (Double, default: Publish rate data amplitude image topic. spectrum_plot.py script publish frequency amplitude plot image. Publishing topics ~output/viz (sensor_msgs/Image) Frequency amplitude plot image. Subscribing topics ~spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data calculated audio FFT. Parameters ~min_amp (Double, default: Minimum amplitude plot. ~max_amp (Double, default: Maximum amplitude plot. ~queue_size (Int, default: Queue size spectrum subscriber. ~max_rate (Double, default: Maximum publish rate frequency amplitude plot image. Setting reduces CPU load. means maximum limit.

name: sound_classification
description: The sound_classification package
readme: Sound Classification ROS package classify sound stream. Contents Setup Usage Quick demo Setup Install ROS. Ubuntu Ubuntu Create workspace mkdir ~/sound_classification_ws/src ~/sound_classification_ws/src git clone https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_recognition.git rosdep install --from-paths --ignore-src catkin build sound_classification source ~/sound_classification_ws/devel/setup.bash Install packages. cuda cupy needed chainer. installation guide JSK GPU highly recommended. Usage Check microphone parameters. device, n_channel, bitdepth mic_sampling_rate bash commands params device. card device device:="hw:0,0" arecord List CAPTURE Hardware Devices card PCH [HDA Intel PCH], device ALC293 Analog [ALC293 Analog] Subdevices: Subdevice subdevice n_channel, bitdepth sample_rate, Note sources means input microphone) sinks means output speaker) pactl list short sources alsa_input.pci-0000_00_1f.3.analog-stereo module-alsa-card.c s16le SUSPENDED Pass params launch file arguments launching `device:=hw:0,0 n_channel:=2 bitdepth:=16 mic_sampling_rate:=44100`). `/audio` topic computer publish `/audio`, set `use_microphone:=false` launch file launching. Save environmental noise train_data/noise.npy. subtracting noise, spectrograms clear. script, give sound sensor. update noise data everytime sound recognition, environmental sound differs everytime. noise samples roslaunch sound_classification save_noise.launch Publish audio spectrum spectrogram topics. set max/min frequency included spectrum high_cut_freq/low_cut_freq args audio_to_spectrogram.launch. gui:=true, spectrum spectrogram visualized. roslaunch sound_classification audio_to_spectrogram.launch gui:=true spectrogram quiet environment. Horiozntal axis time Vertical axis frequency |Spectrogram noise subtraction|Spectrogram noise subtraction| |![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19769486/86824253-d9e6df80-c0c8-11ea-8946-ca1367c1b1b0.png)|![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19769486/86824246-d81d1c00-c0c8-11ea-8c13-dc9660e89ea0.png)| Collect spectrogram classify. volume exceeds threshold, save spectrogram train_data/original_spectrogram/TARGET_CLASS. rosbag stream sound sources. Rosbag version (Recommended) recommend rosbag collect spectrograms. rosbag easy save_sound.launch parameters. target_class:=TARGET_CLASS, set class target sound. use_rosbag:=true filename:=PATH_TO_ROSBAG, save spectrograms rosbag. default, rosbag paused Press ‘Space’ key terminal start playing rosbag. rosbag ends, press ‘Ctrl-c’ terminate. newly saved spectrograms appended existing spectrograms. change threshold sound saving threshold:=xxx. smaller easily sound saved. Save audio rosbag roslaunch sound_classification record_audio_rosbag.launch filename:=PATH_TO_ROSBAG play rosbag collecting data export ROS_MASTER_URI=http://localhost:11311 roslaunch sound_classification save_sound.launch use_rosbag:=true filename:=PATH_TO_ROSBAG target_class:=TARGET_CLASS threshold:=0.5 setting `threshold:=0` `save_when_sound:=false`, collect spectrogram sound". play rosbag collecting no-sound data export ROS_MASTER_URI=http://localhost:11311 roslaunch sound_classification save_sound.launch use_rosbag:=true filename:=PATH_TO_ROSBAG target_class:=no_sound threshold:=0 save_when_sound:=false Stream version Recommended) collect spectrogram audio topic stream. `use_rosbag:=true`. args rosbag version. roslaunch sound_classification save_sound.launch save_when_sound:=true target_class:=TARGET_CLASS threshold:=0.5 save_data_rate:=5 Create dateaset chainer saved spectrograms. data augmentation executed. --number means maximum images class dataset. rosrun sound_classification create_dataset.py --number Visualize dataset. train arg train dataset (augmented dataset), test arg test dataset. spectrograms dataset visualized random order. rosrun sound_classification visualize_dataset.py test train/test Train dataset. Default model NIN (Recommended). vgg16, pretrained weights VGG16 downloaded scripts/VGG_ILSVRC_16_layers.npz time run script. rosrun sound_classification train.py --epoch Classify sounds. takes seconds neural network weights loaded. use_rosbag:=true filename:=PATH_TO_ROSBAG classify sound rosbag. roslaunch sound_classification classify_sound.launch fix class names' color classification result image order class names <rosparam> target_names: chip_bag] </rosparam> classification result: |no_sound|applause|voice| |![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19769486/86828259-ed487980-c0cd-11ea-9f51-7ccb0bc93321.png)|![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19769486/86828249-ec174c80-c0cd-11ea-854c-da1b0fa08e33.png)|![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19769486/86828260-ede11000-c0cd-11ea-9b4e-94ee7b1c1a5f.png)| Quick demo Sound classification demo laptop’s built-in microphone. create dataset rosbag files sample_rosbag/ directory. Classification gif Commands Setup environment write Microphone settings Usage section) Save environmental noise roslaunch sound_classification save_noise.launch Collect spectrograms sample rosbags. Press ‘Space’ start rosbag. no_sound class roslaunch sound_classification save_sound.launch use_rosbag:=true filename:=$(rospack find sound_classification)/sample_rosbag/no_sound.bag target_class:=no_sound threshold:=0 save_when_sound:=false applause class roslaunch sound_classification save_sound.launch use_rosbag:=true filename:=$(rospack find sound_classification)/sample_rosbag/applause.bag target_class:=applause threshold:=0.5 voice class roslaunch sound_classification save_sound.launch use_rosbag:=true filename:=$(rospack find sound_classification)/sample_rosbag/voice.bag target_class:=voice threshold:=0.5 Create dataset rosrun sound_classification create_dataset.py --number Train (takes minites) rosrun sound_classification train.py --epoch Classify sound roslaunch sound_classification classify_sound.launch

readme: roseus_mongo parameters mongodb related parameters mongo::*mongo-database* (string, default: param robot/database test) Database mongodb mongo::*mongo-collection* (string, default: param robot/name test) Collection mongodb mongo::*mongo-query-default-buffer-size* (int, default [byte]) Buffer size reserved storing json raw string serialization mongo::*mongo-service-query* (string, default: /message_store/query_messages) Service querying message mongo::*mongo-service-insert* (string, default: /message_store/insert) Service inserting message mongo::*mongo-service-update* (string, default: /message_store/update) Service updating message mongo::*mongo-service-delete* (string, default: /message_store/delete) Service deleting message mongo::*mongo-service-timeout* (int, default: param ~timeout Seconds wait database server. Setting means waiting euslisp simulation mongodb, recommended set avoid stuck. json(bson) related parameters *json-parse-object-as* (:alist :plist, default: :alist) Destination type evaluating json object *json-parse-key-function* (#'identity, #'string->keyword functions, default: #'string->keyword) Destination type key evaluation json object euslisp/mongo-client-sample.l roseus json Parse json string load "package://roseus_mongo/euslisp/json/json-decode.l" setq *parsed* (json::parse-from-string "{\"key1\" \"key2\" parse json-string assoc :key1 *parsed* (:key1 car (assoc :key1 *parsed*) :key1 cdr (assoc :key1 *parsed*) Convert lisp-alist json string load "package://roseus_mongo/euslisp/json/json-encode.l" setq *alist* '(("key1" ("key2" json::stream->string #'json::encode-alist *alist* "{\"key1\":\"value1\",\"key2\":\"value2\"}"

name: roseus_smach
description:
roseus_smach

     * Euslisp state machine class. it will be moved.
     * Message publisher for visualizing current state by smach_viewer.
     * Simple pickle dump script for debugging state machine.
     * Execute state machine as a action server.
readme: roseus_smach package includes euslisp implementation state machine smach. requirements roseus smach smach_viewer Optional visualization. install package manually: sudo apt install ros-$ROS_DISTRO-smach-viewer sample Sample codes sample directory. rosrun roseus_smach state-machine-ros-sample.l simple state machine rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py rosrun roseus_smach state-machine-ros-sample.l (exec-smach-simple) nested state machine rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py rosrun roseus_smach state-machine-ros-sample.l (exec-smach-nested) state machine userdata rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py rosrun roseus_smach state-machine-ros-sample.l (exec-smach-userdata) sample/parallel-state-machine-sample.l state machine parallel action execution rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py rosrun roseus_smach parallel-state-machine-sample.l (demo) Writing Simple Smach(state-machine) codes #!/usr/bin/env roseus (load "package://roseus_smach/src/state-machine.l") (setq count (defun func-foo (userdata-alist) (format "Execute state FOO~%") (cond count (incf count) :outcome1) :outcome2))) (defun func-bar (userdata-alist) (format "Execute state BAR~%") :outcome2) (defun smach-simple (instance state-machine :init))) (send :add-node (instance state :init :FOO 'func-foo)) (send :add-node (instance state :init :BAR 'func-bar)) goal-states generated method (send :goal-state (list :outcome4 :outcome5)) select node start-node (send :start-state :FOO) nodes selected symbol (send :add-transition :FOO :BAR :outcome1) (send :add-transition :FOO :outcome4 :outcome2) (send :add-transition :BAR :FOO :outcome2) (send (smach-simple) :execute nil) Code Explained (load "package://roseus_smach/src/state-machine.l") line imports state-machine class, state class, transition class. (defun func-foo (userdata-alist) (format "Execute state FOO~%") (cond count (incf count) :outcome1) :outcome2))) (defun func-bar (userdata-alist) (format "Execute state BAR~%") :outcome2) lines define functions hooked state. Note functions called argument, alist declared user-data arguments, return equal transition action) state. (instance state-machine :init)) line creates state-machine instance binds (send :add-node (instance state :init :FOO 'func-foo)) line creates state add node state machine. create state instance, state hook state function. lisp object state names. case, state :FOO hooked func-foo. (send :goal-state (list :outcome4 :outcome5)) (send :start-state :FOO) lines define goal state(s) start state(s) state machine. create multiple start states list state, define multiple goal state. Goal state(s) returns Note state machine state. goal state considered outcome state machine. (send :add-transition :FOO :BAR :outcome1) line defines transition. arguments :add-transition method OUTCOME. line means “adding transition FOO BAR, FOO node returns outcome1”. (send (smach-simple) :execute nil) line sets execute state machine. order view check progress smach_viewer, function exec-state-machine Writing Nested Smach codes add child state-machine node state-machine. #!/usr/bin/env roseus (load "package://roseus_smach/src/state-machine.l") (defun smach-nested ((sm-top (instance state-machine :init)) (sm-sub (instance state-machine :init))) state instance include state-machine function (send sm-top :add-node (instance state :init sm-sub)) (send sm-top :add-node (instance state :init "BAS" 'func-bas)) (send sm-top :goal-state :outcome5) (send sm-top :start-state "BAS") (send sm-top :add-transition "BAS" :outcome3) (send sm-top :add-transition :outcome5 :outcome4) node instance args :add-node, :start-state, :add-transition ((foo-node (instance state :init "FOO" 'func-foo)) (bar-node (instance state :init "BAR" 'func-bar))) (send sm-sub :add-node foo-node) (send sm-sub :add-node bar-node) (send sm-sub :goal-state :outcome4) (send sm-sub :start-state foo-node) (send sm-sub :add-transition foo-node bar-node :outcome1) (send sm-sub :add-transition foo-node :outcome4 :outcome2) (send sm-sub :add-transition bar-node foo-node :outcome2)) sm-top (send (smach-nested) :execute nil) Code Explained #!/usr/bin/env roseus (load "package://roseus_smach/src/state-machine.l") previous line imports state-machine class, state class, transition class. ((sm-top (instance state-machine :init)) (sm-sub (instance state-machine :init))) line creates state-machine instance. sm-sub state machine acting state node) sm-top. easy understand state machine function: state machine called, executed, state machine processing eventually returns goal state return function. (send sm-top :add-node (instance state :init sm-sub)) sm-sub instance hooked node sm-top line. line add state machine state function. (send sm-top :add-node (instance state :init "BAS" 'func-bas)) (send sm-top :goal-state :outcome5) (send sm-top :start-state "BAS") (send sm-top :add-transition "BAS" :outcome3) lines define node, goal-state, start-state, transition sm-top. (send sm-top :add-transition :outcome5 :outcome4) Remember goal state(s) state-machine returns outcome return sm-sub goal state(s). goal state sm-sub :outcome4, return sm-sub :outcome4. line adds transition :outcome5 node returns :outcome4. ((foo-node (instance state :init "FOO" 'func-foo)) (bar-node (instance state :init "BAR" 'func-bar))) (send sm-sub :add-node foo-node) (send sm-sub :add-node bar-node) (send sm-sub :goal-state :outcome4) (send sm-sub :start-state foo-node) (send sm-sub :add-transition foo-node bar-node :outcome1) (send sm-sub :add-transition foo-node :outcome4 :outcome2) (send sm-sub :add-transition bar-node foo-node :outcome2)) lines define behavior sm-sub detail previous simple state machine Note (send sm-sub :goal-state :outcome4) defines goal state, defines return state machine. (send (smach-nested) :execute nil) Finally, sm-top executed Writing Simple Smach (make-state-machine) make-state-machine function easy-way define simple state machine. requires graph-list, func-map, initial-state, goal-states arguments. simple state machine written (defun smach-simple2 (setq (make-state-machine define graph, list (<from-node> <transition> <to-node>) <transition> corresponds node returns nil. '((:foo :outcome2 :outcome4) (:foo :outcome1 :bar) (:bar :outcome2 :foo)) define function map '((:foo 'func-foo) foo returns :outcome1 times returns :outcome2 (:bar 'func-bar)) bar returns :outcome2 initial state '(:foo) goal state '(:outcome4))))) node :foo :bar :outcome4 terminate node. node corresponds 'func-foo 'func-bar functions. function 'func-foo returns :outcome1 times returns :outcome2. function 'func-bar returns :outcome2. (:foo :outcome2 :outcome4) means :foo returns :outcome2, transit :outcome4. (:foo :outcome1 :bar) means :foo returns :outcome1, transit :bar. (:bar :outcome2 :foo) means :bar returns :outcome2, transit :foo. simplify state machine definition, recommend users t/nil return node, users able (:foo :outcome4) graph definition. (defun smach-simple3 (setq (make-state-machine '((:foo :outcome4) (:foo :bar) (:bar :foo)) '((:foo '(lambda (&rest args) (cond count (incf count) nil) foo returns nil times returns (:bar '(lambda (&rest args) bar returns '(:foo) '(:outcome4))))) tested roscd roseus_smach/sample roseus state-machine-ros-sample.l "(progn (setq count 0)(exec-state-machine (smach-simple2)))" roseus state-machine-ros-sample.l "(progn (setq count 0)(exec-state-machine (smach-simple3)))" check state machine behavior rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py` Writing Nested Smach (make-state-machine) write nested state machine make-state-machie function. add state-machine function maps, added node (defun smach-simple-nested (sm-top sm-sub) (setq sm-sub (make-state-machine '((:foo :outcome2 :outcome4) transitions (:foo :outcome1 :bar) (:bar :outcome2 :foo)) '((:foo 'func-foo) function maps (:bar 'func-bar)) '(:foo) initial '(:outcome4) goal (setq sm-top (make-state-machine '((:bas :outcome3 transitions :outcome4 :outcome5)) `((:bas 'func-bas) functon maps ,sm-sub)) set "nestaed state machine" '(:bas) initial '(:outcome5) goal sm-top)) roseus write '((:bas 'func-bas) functon maps sm-sub)) set "nestaed state machine" careful add machine. `(backquote) (comma). tested roscd roseus_smach/sample roseus state-machine-ros-sample.l "(progn (setq count 0)(exec-state-machine (smach-simple-nested)))"

Automatic docking for Kobuki:

Bumper/cliff to pointcloud nodelet:
    Publish bumpers and cliff sensors events as points in a pointcloud, so navistack can use them
    for poor-man navigation. Implemented as a nodelet intended to run together with kobuki_node.

name: kobuki_description
description:
Description of the Kobuki model.
      Provides the model description of Kobuki for simulation and visualisation. The files in this 
      package are parsed and used by a variety of other components. Most users will not interact directly
      with this package.
      
      WARNING: This package is disabled because it cannot be catkinized by now, as xacro dependency is not
      catkin still. In the interim we use a unary pre-catkin stack named kobuki_description.
authors: Daniel Stonier, Marcus Liebhardt, Younghun Ju

name: kobuki_safety_controller
description:
A controller ensuring the safe operation of Kobuki.

    The SafetyController keeps track of bumper, cliff and wheel drop events. In case of the first two,
    Kobuki is commanded to move back. In the latter case, Kobuki is stopped.
    
    This controller can be enabled/disabled.
    The safety states (bumper pressed etc.) can be reset. WARNING: Dangerous!

name: kobuki_ftdi
description:
Utilities for flashing and enabling Kobuki's USB connection.
	    This package contains tools for flashing the Kobuki's FTDI chip (usually done at the factory).
	    The special firmware for the FTDI chip (USB to serial converter) enables it to appear as
	    /dev/kobuki on the user's PC.
readme: Kobuki ftdi Documentation Official Web Page home page, sales, specifications hardware howto. Protocol, Usage Api Documentation doxygen. description: A ROS driver for the KVH Geo Fog 3D INS family of systems.
readme: kvh-geo-fog-3d-driver Driver KVH GEO FOG inertial navigation systems. Connects serial KVH GEO FOG device publishes KVH-specific generic ROS-ified messages, performs basic transforms convert data ROS-conformant conventions. Initial testing MITRE Corporation “Dual” model, dual-antenna heading solutions. API match dual non-dual models. detailed information KVH GEO FOG functionality, consult technical reference manual. Product pages: KVH GEO FOG KVH GEO FOG Dual Copyright MITRE Corporation. Rights Reserved. ROS API kvh_geo_fog_3d_driver_node main driver node. Published Topics KVH-specific messages messages defined part kvh_geo_fog_3d_msgs package. direct pass-throughs KVH messages, universally conform ROS REPs KVH reports latitude longitude radians. ~<node_name>/kvh_system_state (kvh_geo_fog_3d_msgs/KvhGeoFog3DSystemState) ~<node_name>/kvh_satellites (kvh_geo_fog_3d_msgs/KvhGeoFog3DSatellites) ~<node_name>/kvh_detailed_satellites (kvh_geo_fog_3d_msgs/KvhGeoFog3DDetailSatellites) ~<node_name>/kvh_local_magnetics (kvh_geo_fog_3d_msgs/KvhGeoFog3DLocalMagneticField) ~<node_name>/kvh_utm_position (kvh_geo_fog_3d_msgs/KvhGeoFog3DUTMPosition) ~<node_name>/kvh_ecef_pos (kvh_geo_fog_3d_msgs/KvhGeoFog3DECEFPos) ~<node_name>/kvh_north_seeking_status (kvh_geo_fog_3d_msgs/KvhGeoFog3DNorthSeekingInitStatus) ~<node_name>/kvh_odometer_state (kvh_geo_fog_3d_msgs/KvhGeoFog3DSatellites) ~<node_name>/kvh_raw_sensors (kvh_geo_fog_3d_msgs/KvhGeoFog3DRawSensors) ~<node_name>/kvh_raw_gnss (kvh_geo_fog_3d_msgs/KvhGeoFog3DRawGNSS) Conventional ROS messages messages conform ROS REP-105 REP-145. “ROS Conformance Conventions” section details. Generally speaking, “_flu” “_enu” suffix conforms ROS REPs. angles measured radians suffixed “_deg”. NavSatFix latitude/longitude measured degrees message definition). KVH messages, report latitude/longitude radians. ~<node_name>/imu/data_raw_frd (sensor_msgs/Imu) ~<node_name>/imu/data_raw_flu (sensor_msgs/Imu) REP-105, REP-145 compliant ~<node_name>/imu/data_ned (sensor_msgs/Imu) ~<node_name>/imu/data_enu (sensor_msgs/Imu) REP-105, REP-145 compliant ~<node_name>/imu/rpy_ned (geometry_msgs/Vector3Stamped) ~<node_name>/imu/rpy_ned_deg (geometry_msgs/Vector3Stamped) ~<node_name>/imu/rpy_enu (geometry_msgs/Vector3Stamped) ~<node_name>/imu/rpy_enu_deg (geometry_msgs/Vector3Stamped) ~<node_name>/gps/fix (sensor_msgs/NavSatFix) Filtered GNSS location, degrees ~<node_name>/gps/raw_fix (sensor_msgs/NavSatFix) Raw GNSS location, degrees ~<node_name>/gps/mag (sensor_msgs/MagneticField) ~<node_name>/gps/utm_ned (nav_msgs/Odometry) ~<node_name>/gps/utm_enu (nav_msgs/Odometry) REP-105 compliant ~<node_name>/odom/wheel_encoder (nav_msgs/Odometry) ~<node_name>imu/raw_sensor_frd (nav_msgs/Odometry) ~<node_name>/odom/raw_sensor_flu (nav_msgs/Odometry) REP-105 compliant Parameters driver asks wheel odometer pulses meter passed parameter. KVH API odometer pulses directionally pulse count decreases) odometer distance absolute distance traveled backing increases distance traveled, negative). source non-differentiated data, fuse position filters doesn’t drift time velocity, drift). KVH API provide method obtaining current wheel odometer pulse distance hand-coded parameter. future, KVH exposes API. ~<node_name>/port (string, default: /dev/ttyUSB0) ~<node_name>/baud (int, default: ~<node_name>/debug (bool, default: false) ~<node_name>/filterVehicleType (string, default: (car, spatial_packets.h)) ~<node_name>/atmosphericAltitudeEnabled (bool, default: true) ~<node_name>/velocityHeadingEnabled (bool, default: false) ~<node_name>/reversingDetectionEnabled (bool, default: true) ~<node_name>/motionAnalysisEnabled (bool, default: true) ~<node_name>/odomPulseToMeters (double, default: Note: check KVH odometer_configuration packet exists. KVH parameter. determine_baud_node node determine optionally, set baud rate sensor. supported baud rates listed technical reference manual. Parameters ~<node_name>/port (string, default: /dev/ttyUSB0) Setup Installation instructions connected KVH. Serial Port Configuration connect KVH FTDI cable KVH serial cable connect. setup, FTDI cable connect laptops development debug, serial cable connect mission computer. default, serial port owned root.dialout group. run driver root privileges, add user dialout group. permission errors chmod change permissions manually. sudo chmod a+rw <port> port /dev/ttyUSB0 Beware ports limited bandwidth handle large amounts KVH data. case, enable multiplexer bios mission computer larger baud rates. FTDI cable KVH support baud rates. Running driver clone build repository git clone <url> kvh_geo_fog_3d catkin build kvh_geo_fog_3d important KVH messages installed properly. building initially check running: source .../devel/setup.bash required building rosmsg list grep kvh messages listed KVH-specific messages section able launch driver running roslaunch kvh_geo_fog_3d_driver kvh_geo_fog_3d_node.launch port:=<port> baud:=<baud> port="/dev/ttyUSB0" baud="921600" set properly, able data output topics listed driver hardcoded output data kvh packets: System State Satellites Detailed Satellites Local Magnetics UTM position ECEF position North Seeking Status Odometer State Raw Sensors Raw GNSS Odom Configuration Packet output) packets, development guide KVH defaults baud, recommend baud utility section set baud rate prevent data overflow. Baud Debug Note assumes baudrate, default. baud rate couple options: Relaunch node manually baud rates. Contact KVH access GEO-FOG manager, GUI tool interacting KVH. ros node attempts automatically detect baud rate buad rates checking returned data. launch node running command default start starting baud rate setting baud rate finding incorrect recieving random data skip baud rates). roslaunch kvh_geo_fog_3d_driver determine_baud.launch starting_baud:=<baud_rate> Due connecting library baud rates give option set buad rate recommended set baud rate smallest baud rate overflow. Note: number smaller work, due possibility detailed satellites packet maximum size recommend ROS conformance Conventions driver attempts conform ROS Enhancement Proposals (REPs) REP Coordinate Frames Mobile Platforms Link: https://www.ros.org/reps/rep-0105.html Summary: Data published default sensor conventions (FRD/NED) suffixes _frd _ned Data published FLU/ENU topics, suffixes _flu _enu sensor, navigation sensors, standard Front-Right-Down North-East-Down (FRD/NED) coordinate frame conventions. HARRIS. method focused finding corners set keypoints. BOUNDARY. method detects parts edges set keypoints. MULTISCALE. method able find keypoints relevance scales. recommended compairing cloud previous scan. NORMALS. in-house method based relation point clouds normals. finds correspondences orientation dominant normals. avoid pre alignment process clouds close start inmediatly GICP. Usage load_clouds publish clouds. Clouds path param /pointcloud_folder. examples pointclouds. rosrun leica_point_cloud_processing load_clouds rosservice call /publish_clouds "source_cloud_file: 'scan_fods.pcd' target_cloud_file: 'cad.ply'" Launch state machine node. Inspection process starts clouds ROS topics: /target/cloud /source/cloud. target cloud CAD file, set param using_CAD true. roslaunch leica_point_cloud_processing leica_point_cloud_processing.launch using_CAD:=true FOD detection process ends, results clouds ROS topics. visualize detected FODs pointclouds RViz. name: access_point_control
description:
Defines an API for access point control based on 
    dynamic_reconfigure. Other packages must
    implement the API for various access-point models: 
    for example: hostapd_access_point for hostapd-based control or
    linksys_access_point for Linksys router web interface.

name: asmach
description:
SMACH, which stands for 'state machine', is a task-level
    architecture for rapidly creating complex robot behavior. At its
    core, SMACH is a ROS-independent Python library to build
    hierarchical state machines.  SMACH is a new library that takes
    advantage of very old concepts in order to quickly create robust
    robot behavior with maintainable and modular code.

name: asmach_tutorials
description: This package containes numerous examples of how to use SMACH. See the examples directory.

name: ddwrt_access_point
description:
A ROS node that controls a Linksys WRT610Nv2 access point with
    a dd-wrt firmware. Other access points models/dd-wrt versions
    may be compatible as long as the web interface is identical.

name: hostapd_access_point
description:
A ROS node that controls a hostapd-based access
    point. It is mainly intended for use with a wireless 
    network adapter running in master mode. It implements 
    the dynamic_reconfigure interface defined
    in the [[access_point_control]] package.

name: ieee80211_channels
description:
This package provides mapping from frequencies to
    IEEE802.11 channels and vice-versa.

name: linksys_access_point
description:
A ROS node that controls a Linksys access point with
    a Linksys WRT610n-compatible web interface.

Test suite for the packages that are part of the "WiFi Test Setup" project:
    network_monitor_udp, network_traffic_control, hostapd_access_point, linksys_access_point,
    ddwrt_access_point.

name: network_detector
description:
A ROS node that watches a given network interface and publishes
     whether it is both UP and RUNNING (indicating that a cable is
     plugged into it and communication is happening, for instance) or
     not.

name: network_monitor_udp
description:
Facilities to monitor a network connection by sending UDP packets from
    a client to a server, which bounces them back to the client. The client
    collects statistics on latency and loss. The server is a C standalone utility
    or a ROS node. The client can be a ROS node, a standalone utility or a python class.

name: network_traffic_control
description: A ROS node that allows control of network emulation parameters such as bandwidth, loss and latency for a Linux network interface.

Simple scripts which help utilise, monitor, interact with computer
     hardware abstracted by a linux OS.

name: log_view
description:
The log_view package provides a ncurses based terminal GUI for
    viewing and filtering published ROS log messages.

    This is an alternative to rqt_console and swri_console that doesn't depend
    on qt and can be run directly in a terminal.
readme: log_view log_viewer ncurses text viewing rosout logs. Motivation Receive, filter, navigate published rosout log messages terminal support debugging robot live. Problem rosout log messages don’t printed terminal normal lots nodes/messages. convienient filter navigate real time. swri_console desired functionality based requires graphical interface. Supported schemas: Serial: /path/to/serial/device[:baudrate] Serial: serial:///path/to/serial/device[:baudrate][?ids=sysid,compid] Serial hardware flow control: serial-hwfc:///path/to/serial/device[:baudrate][?ids=sysid,compid] UDP: udp://[bind_host][:port]@[remote_host][:port][/?ids=sysid,compid] UDP broadcast GCS discovery: udp-b://[bind_host][:port]@[:port][/?ids=sysid,compid] UDP broadcast (permanent): udp-pb://[bind_host][:port]@[:port][/?ids=sysid,compid] TCP client: tcp://[server_host][:port][/?ids=sysid,compid] TCP server: tcp-l://[bind_port][:port][/?ids=sysid,compid] Note: ids URL overrides ids system_id component_id parameters. readme: MAVROS MAVLink extendable communication node ROS proxy Ground Control Station QGroundControl). ROS API documentation moved wiki.ros.org. Features Communication autopilot serial port, UDP TCP Pro ArduPilot) Internal proxy Ground Control Station (serial, UDP, TCP) Plugin system ROS-MAVLink translation Parameter manipulation tool Waypoint manipulation tool PX4Flow support mavros_extras) OFFBOARD mode support Geographic coordinates conversions. Limitations Linux. Depends Boost library, GCC support). Catkin build system required. package dependent ros-*-mavlink build mavlink-gbp-release. exists ROS package index updates month. Supported schemas: Serial: /path/to/serial/device[:baudrate] Serial: serial:///path/to/serial/device[:baudrate][?ids=sysid,compid] Serial hardware flow control: serial-hwfc:///path/to/serial/device[:baudrate][?ids=sysid,compid] UDP: udp://[bind_host][:port]@[remote_host[:port]][/?ids=sysid,compid] UDP broadcast GCS discovery: udp-b://[bind_host][:port]@[:port][/?ids=sysid,compid] UDP broadcast (permanent): udp-pb://[bind_host][:port]@[:port][/?ids=sysid,compid] TCP client: tcp://[server_host][:port][/?ids=sysid,compid] TCP server: tcp-l://[bind_host][:port][/?ids=sysid,compid] Note: Ids URL overrides system_id component_id parameters. bind_host default UDP default ports: UDP remote address updated time incoming packet bind port. TCP default port: Coordinate frames MAVROS translate Aerospace NED frames, FCUs ROS ENU frames vice-versa. translate airframe related data simply apply rotation ROLL axis. local apply ROLL YAW axes. read documents issue additional information. conversions handled src/lib/ftf_frame_conversions.cpp src/lib/ftf_quaternion_utils.cpp tested test/test_frame_conversions.cpp test/test_quaternion_utils.cpp Related issues: (outdated), (outdated), (outdated), (outdated), (outdated), Documents: Frame Conversions, Mavlink coordinate frames. name: mcl_3dl
description: 3-D/6-DOF localization for mobile robots with 3-D LIDAR(s)
readme: mcl_3dl Package summary mcl_3dl ROS node perform probabilistic 3-D/6-DOF localization system mobile robots LIDAR(s). implements pointcloud based Monte Carlo localization reference pointcloud map. node receives reference pointcloud environment map localizes 6-DOF yaw, pitch, roll) pose measured pointclouds assisted motion prediction odometry. supported motion model differential-wheeled-robot. node classic MCL; doesn’t implement adaptive feature KDL-sampling Algorithms fundamental algorithm mcl_3dl node Monte Carlo localization (MCL), aka particle filter localization. MCL represents probabilistic distribution estimated pose density weight particles estimates pose distribution. Algorithm details Parameters Node Install source Note: mcl_3dl_msgs package required build mcl_3dl package. clone /path/to/your/catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/at-wat/mcl_3dl.git git clone https://github.com/at-wat/mcl_3dl_msgs.git build /path/to/your/catkin_ws rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src Install dependencies catkin_make -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release Release build recommended apt repository ROS Indigo/Kinetic/Lunar Ubuntu) sudo apt-get install ros-${ROS_DISTRO}-mcl-3dl Running demo bag file 2+4-DOF tracked vehicle Hokuyo YVT-X002 LIDAR online. Pre-processed (filtered) pointcloud, IMU pose, odometry, map data packed bag. Download bag wget ~/Downloads https://openspur.org/~atsushi.w/dataset/mcl_3dl/short_test3.bag Running demo roslaunch mcl_3dl test.launch use_pointcloud_map:=false use_cad_map:=false use_bag_file:=true bag_file:=${HOME}/Downloads/short_test3.bag map data bag generated cartographer_ros filtered pcl_outlier_removal pcl_voxel_grid utilities. MarkerArray shows mcl_3dl internal information. Purple spheres: sampled points likelihood-model calculation Red lines: casted rays beam-model calculation Red boxes: detected collisions raycasting global localization, call /global_localization command. rosservice call /global_localization Demos odometry IMU Contributing mcl_3dl package developed GitHub flow. Feel free open Issue and/or Pull Request. code repository ROS Style Guide. configuration file clang-format https://github.com/seqsense/ros_style/. License mcl_3dl BSD license. Backport codes Point Cloud Library (PCL) BSD license.

name: mbf_abstract_core
description: This package provides common interfaces for navigation specific robot actions. It contains the AbstractPlanner, AbstractController and AbstractRecovery plugin interfaces. This interfaces have to be implemented by the plugins to make the plugin available for Move Base Flex. The abstract classes provides a meaningful interface enabling the planners, controllers and recovery behaviors to return information, e.g. why something went wrong. Derivided interfaces can, for example, provide methods to initialize the planner, controller or recovery with map representations like costmap_2d, grid_map or other representations.
readme: Move Base Flex Abstract Core mbf_abstract_core package Move Base Flex (MBF) abstract interfaces planning controlling recovering. Derived packages mbf_abstract_nav derived interfaces mbf_abstract_core method initialization plugin, handing pointer map representation choice. plugins loaded Move Base Flex derived package mbf_abstract_nav) implement derived interfaces interfaces defined package. mbf_abstract_core::AbstractPlanner interface planner plugin plan global paths. mbf_abstract_core::AbstractController interface controller plugin control move robot. mbf_abstract_core::AbstractRecovery interface recovery plugin recover robot deadlocked situations.

name: mbf_abstract_nav
description: The mbf_abstract_nav package contains the abstract navigation server implementation of Move Base Flex (MBF). The abstract navigation server is not bound to any map representation. It provides the actions for planning, controlling and recovering. MBF loads all defined plugins at the program start. Therefor, it loads all plugins which are defined in the lists *planners*, *controllers* and *recovery_behaviors*. Each list holds a pair of a *name* and a *type*. The *type* defines which kind of plugin to load. The *name* defines under which name the plugin should be callable by the actions.
readme: Move Base Flex Abstract Navigation Server mbf_abstract_nav package abstract navigation server implementation Move Base Flex (MBF). abstract navigation server bound map representation. Actions planning, controlling recovering. time start MBF loads defined plugins. Therefor, loads plugins defined lists planners, controllers recovery_behaviors. list holds pair type. type defines kind plugin load. defines plugin callable actions.

name: mbf_costmap_core
description: This package provides common interfaces for navigation specific robot actions. It contains the CostmapPlanner, CostmapController and CostmapRecovery interfaces. The interfaces have to be implemented by the plugins to make them available for Move Base Flex using the mbf_costmap_nav navigation implementation. That implementation inherits the mbf_abstract_nav implementation and binds the system to a local and a global costmap.
readme: Move Base Flex Costmap Navigation Core package common interfaces navigation specific robot actions. CostmapPlanner, CostmapController CostmapRecovery interfaces. interfaces implemented plugins Move Base Flex mbf_costmap_nav navigation implementation. implementation inherits mbf_abstract_nav implementation binds system local global costmap.

Each list holds a pair of a *name* and a *type*. The *type* defines which kind of plugin to load. The *name* defines under which name the plugin should be callable by the actions.

name: move_base_flex
description:
Move Base Flex (MBF) is a backwards-compatible replacement for move_base. MBF can use existing plugins for move_base, and provides an enhanced version of the planner, controller and recovery plugin ROS interfaces. It exposes action servers for planning, controlling and recovering, providing detailed information of the current state and the plugin’s feedback. An external executive logic can use MBF and its actions to perform smart and flexible navigation strategies. Furthermore, MBF enables the use of other map representations, e.g. meshes or grid_map
       
       This package is a meta package and refers to the Move Base Flex stack packages.The abstract core of MBF – without any binding to a map representation – is represented by the

readme: moveit_python set pure-python bindings ROS interface MoveIt! based earlier moveit_utils package developed part chess_player package. Overview interfaces MoveGroupInterface move arm move_group action. PlanningSceneInterface add/remove collision attached objects. set colors objects. PickPlaceInterface interface pick place actions. MoveGroupInterface MoveGroupInterface easy import rospy moveit_python import rospy.init_node("moveit_py") MoveGroupInterface("planning_group_name", "base_link") values base_link, planning group called “planning_group_name”. PlanningSceneInterface PlanningSceneInterface easily insert remove objects MoveIt! planning scene. Starting version module newer service-based approach apply planning scene updates, robust publishing messages topics asynchronously. import rospy moveit_python import rospy.init_node("moveit_py") create planning scene interface, provide root link PlanningSceneInterface("base_link") add cube size, base_link frame p.addCube("my_cube", remove cube p.removeCollisionObject("my_cube") reason prefer service, simply set “use_service” false add/remove calls. Calling waitForSync republish messages needed synchronize planning scene script MoveIt. p.addCube("my_cube", use_service=False) p.addCube("my_other_cube", use_service=False) p.waitForSync() PickPlaceInterface import rospy moveit_python import moveit_msgs.msg import Grasp, PlaceLocation rospy.init_node("moveit_py") provide arm group gripper group names takes parameter "plan_only" defaults False PickPlaceInterface("arm", "gripper") Grasp() fill setup object named object_name PlanningSceneInterface p.pickup("object_name", support_name "supporting_surface") PlaceLocation() fill p.place("object_name" goal_is_eef True, support_name "supporting_surface") Migration moveit_utils GraspingInterface renamed PickPlaceInterface pick/place functions return entire action result, moveit_utils returned error_code. access error code returned, result.error_code.val ObjectManager renamed PlanningSceneInterface remove function removeCollisionObject removeAttachedObject ArmInterface renamed MoveGroupInterface

name: movie_publisher
description: Node for using a video file as video topic source.
readme: movie_publisher package tools movie files ROS (playback, conversion bag files handles file formats system installation ffmpeg decode. Important: package meant work moviepy. due packaging issues, moviepy installed automatically dependency. fallback OpenCV, worse. install moviepy manually calling: sudo pip install moviepy rosdep install python-moviepy-pip Main tools movie_publisher_node: ROS node serves video file video topic source (sensor_msgs/Image friends). movie_publisher.launch: launch file convenient usage node. starting image_transport/republish node converts video stream raw compressed/theora. mode, convert video files bagfiles batch. movie_to_bag: batch script takes video input transforms bag file. add_movie_to_bag: batch script adds video topic existing bag file. Helper tools fix_bag_timestamps: batch script rewrites bag files message publication time message header. generate bagfile data high speed reprocess suitable publication timestamps. merge.py: copy srv_tools/merge.py indigo. movie_publisher_node node run backends moviepy opencv. moviepy strongly recommended, ffmpeg, versatile efficient. set backend param, autodetection run. Published topics movie (sensor_msgs/Image): published movie, raw format. Node-private parameters: movie_file (string, required): Path movie play. format ffmpeg decode. fps (float, optional): set, playback FPS (speeding up/slowing movie). start (float|tuple|string, optional): set, playback start time. expressed seconds (min, sec), (hour, min, sec), string: set duration. (float|tuple|string, optional): set, playback stop time affected start). expressed seconds (min, sec), (hour, min, sec), string: set start duration. duration (float|tuple|string, optional): set, playback duration. set, duration counted clip, duration start clip. expressed seconds (min, sec), (hour, min, sec), string: set start loop (bool, default False): loop movie node shut Exludes (bool, default False): True, movie processed published quickly waiting real time. timestamps resulting messages act “real-world-like” FPS means frames’ timestamps sec set fake_time_start timestamps non-zero time. Excludes loop. playback_rate (float, optional): set number, mode play fast rate (set rate number lose messages, image_transport/republish). fake_time_start (float, default timestamp message. frame_id (string, default frame_id messages’ headers. spin_after_end (bool, default False): True, rospy.spin() called movie published. verbose (bool, default False): True, logs info frame played. wait_after_publisher_created (float, default workaround case give subscribers time publisher created. Tweak number missing start messages. publisher_queue_size (int, default mode, queue_size movie publisher. backend (string, default “moviepy”): backend reading video. moviepy opencv. moviepy selected opencv support codecs). ffmpeg (string, default nonempty, specifies (absolute) path ffmpeg binary movie_publisher.launch launch file takes arguments node’s parameters. Additionally, takes arguments: transport (string, default 'raw'): Type image transport. launch file start image_transport/republish node publishes video encoded transport type. republished_topic_basename (string, default movie_$(arg transport)): Base topic serve republished messages. Full topic $(arg republished_topic_basename)/$(arg transport) $(arg republished_topic_basename) case raw transport). base topic movie_publisher_node subscribes (movie default). movie_to_bag Convert movie file bag file video topic. Bash script ROS node-like API pass parameters _param:=value commandline ROS param server. Node-private parameters: movie (string): Path source movie file. bag (string): Path result stored. topic (string): movie’s topic bag file overwrite_bag (bool, default false): true, overwrites existing bag file, exits error bag exists. tmp_bag (string, default /tmp/movie.bag): Path temporary bag file stored. transport (string, default compressed): image transport encoding image. raw, compressed, theora installed image transport. Usage Call script commandline setting node-private parameters, pass arg:=value arguments relayed movie_publisher.launch pass arguments collide node-private parameters script movie_file). rosrun movie_publisher movie_to_bag _movie:=movie.mp4 _bag:=movie.bag _topic:="/movie" start:=5 fake_time_start:=1548323340.24 add_movie_to_bag Add movie file existing bagfile topic. Bash script ROS node-like API pass parameters _param:=value commandline ROS param server. Node-private parameters: movie (string): Path source movie file. bag_in (string): Path source bag file. bag_out (string, default: output.bag): Path result stored. topic (string): movie’s topic bag file. movie_delay (int, default Delay seconds) movie file bag file start. negative. overwrite_out_bag (bool, default false): true, overwrites existing bag_out file, exits error bag_out exists. bag_tmp (string, default /tmp/movie_add_to.bag): Path temporary bag file stored. transport (string, default compressed): image transport encoding image. raw, compressed, theora installed image transport. Usage Call script commandline setting node-private parameters, pass arg:=value arguments relayed movie_publisher.launch pass arguments collide node-private parameters script movie_file). rosrun movie_publisher add_movie_to_bag _movie:=movie.mp4 _bag_in:=movie_in.bag _bag_out:=movie_out.bag _topic:="/movie" start:=5 movie_delay:=-1 fix_bag_timestamps in_bag messages header field sets publication time time stored header.stamp delay. topics set, works messages listed topics. Reading timestamps supported. Python script ROS node-like API pass parameters _param:=value commandline. Node-private parameters: in_bag (string): Path source bag file. out_bag (string): Path result stored. topics (string, default: nonempty, rewrite timestamps listed topics. Pass comma-separated list topics. delay (int, default Delay seconds) added timestamp read header. negative. overwrite_existing (bool, default false): true, overwrites existing out_bag file, exits error out_bag exists.

python3-pip sudo apt install ros-noetic-rosbridge-library sudo apt install mosquitto mosquitto-clients Install python modules pip3 install requirements.txt launch node roslaunch mqtt_bridge demo.launch Publish /ping, rostopic pub /ping std_msgs/Bool "data: true" response /pong. rostopic echo /pong data: True Publish /echo, rostopic pub /echo std_msgs/String "data: 'hello'" response rostopic echo data: MQTT messages mosquitto_sub mosquitto_sub Usage parameter file (config.yaml): mqtt: client: protocol: MQTTv311 connection: host: localhost port: keepalive: bridge: ping pong factory: mqtt_bridge.bridge:RosToMqttBridge msg_type: std_msgs.msg:Bool topic_from: /ping topic_to: ping factory: mqtt_bridge.bridge:MqttToRosBridge msg_type: std_msgs.msg:Bool topic_from: ping topic_to: /pong msg types sensor_msgs.msg:Imu. launch file: <launch> <node name="mqtt_bridge" pkg="mqtt_bridge" type="mqtt_bridge_node.py" output="screen"> <rosparam file="/path/to/config.yaml" command="load" </node> </launch> Configuration mqtt Parameters mqtt section creating paho’s mqtt.Client configuration. subsections client: mqtt.Client constructor tls: tls configuration account: username password configuration message: MQTT message configuration userdata: MQTT userdata configuration MQTT’s configuration mqtt_bridge.mqtt_client detail. mqtt private path mqtt/private_path parameter set, leading MQTT topic path replaced mqtt/pivate_path set “device/001”, MQTT path converted “device/001/value”. serializer deserializer mqtt_bridge msgpack serializer default. configure serializers. json serialization, add configuration. serializer: json:dumps deserializer: json:loads bridges list ROS MQTT tranfer specifications format. bridge: ping pong factory: mqtt_bridge.bridge:RosToMqttBridge msg_type: std_msgs.msg:Bool topic_from: /ping topic_to: ping factory: mqtt_bridge.bridge:MqttToRosBridge msg_type: std_msgs.msg:Bool topic_from: ping topic_to: /pong factory: bridge class transfering message ROS MQTT, vise versa. msg_type: ROS Message type transfering bridge. topic_from: topic incoming (ROS MQTT) topic_to: topic outgoing (ROS MQTT) create custom bridge class inheriting mqtt_brige.bridge.Bridge. name: mrpt1
description: Mobile Robot Programming Toolkit (MRPT) version 1.5.x
readme: MRPT project Introduction Mobile Robot Programming Toolkit (MRPT) libraries aimed researchers mobile robotics computer vision. Libraries include SLAM solutions, geometry, SE(2)/SE(3) Lie groups, probability density functions (pdfs) points, landmarks, poses maps, Bayesian inference (Kalman filters, particle filters), image processing, obstacle avoidance, MRPT GUI apps Stereo camera calibration, dataset inspection, Resources Download latest unstable code git clone https://github.com/MRPT/mrpt.git --depth questions Google group stackoverflow tag mrpt!) Main project website, including sources Windows installer downloads API reference ROS packages: mrpt_navigation, mrpt_slam Bindings documentation (Python, Matlab) Source code dozens examples configuration files MRPT applications MRPT/share/mrpt/config_files sample datasets stored MRPT/share/mrpt/datasets. complete dataset repository online. contribute code feaures, bug fixes, Install Ubuntu PPA PPA branch sudo add-apt-repository ppa:joseluisblancoc/mrpt-1.5 sudo apt-get update sudo apt-get install libmrpt-dev mrpt-apps Supported distributions: Ubuntu 14.04LTS Trusty. Ubuntu 16.04LTS Xenial. versions Ubuntu, recommended mrpt>=1.9.9. Build sources Minimum compiler requisites: mrpt (master branch): Ubuntu 16.04LTS Xenial: default gcc/clang versions Windows: Visual Studio mrpt maintenance mrpt-1.5 branch: Ubuntu 14.04LTS Trusty: default gcc/clang versions Windows: Visual Studio build Debian/Ubuntu follow steps full build docs online Windows instructions learn details. Install minimum recommended dependencies: sudo apt install build-essential pkg-config cmake libwxgtk3.0-dev libopencv-dev libeigen3-dev libgtest-dev Install additional dependencies enable MRPT features: sudo apt install libftdi-dev freeglut3-dev zlib1g-dev libusb-1.0-0-dev libudev-dev libfreenect-dev libdc1394-22-dev libavformat-dev libswscale-dev libassimp-dev libjpeg-dev libsuitesparse-dev libpcap-dev liboctomap-dev Build cmake usual: mkdir build build cmake License MRPT released BSD license.

name: multi_object_tracking_lidar
description: ROS package for Multiple objects detection, tracking and classification from LIDAR scans/point-clouds
readme: Multiple objects detection, tracking classification LIDAR scans/point-clouds PCL based ROS package Detect/Cluster Track Classify static dynamic objects real-time LIDAR scans implemented Features: tree based point cloud processing object feature detection point clouds Unsupervised euclidean cluster extraction k-means clustering based detected features refinement RANSAC Stable tracking (object data association) ensemble Kalman Filters Robust compared k-means clustering mean-flow tracking Usage: Follow steps (multi_object_tracking_lidar) package: Create catkin workspace setup Navigate src folder catkin workspace: ~/catkin_ws/src Clone repository: git clone https://github.com/praveen-palanisamy/multiple-object-tracking-lidar.git Compile build package: ~/catkin_ws catkin_make Add catkin workspace ROS environment: source ~/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash Run kf_tracker ROS node package: rosrun multi_object_tracking_lidar kf_tracker ROS node running! long point clouds published filtered_cloud rostopic, outputs node published obj_id, cluster_0, cluster_1, cluster_5 topics markers topic visualize RViz. Supported point-cloud streams/sources: input point-clouds real LiDAR simulated LiDAR point cloud dataset data source produces point clouds Note: package expects valid point cloud data input. point clouds publish “filtered_cloud” expected NaNs. point cloud filtering task application dependent module. PCL library pcl::removeNaNFromPointCloud method filter NaN points. refer code snippet easily filter NaN points point cloud. Citing code snippets repository work, cite: @software{praveen_palanisamy_2019_3559187, author {Praveen Palanisamy}, title {{praveen-palanisamy/multiple-object-tracking-lidar: Multiple-Object-Tracking-from-Point-Clouds_v1.0.2}}, month dec, year publisher {Zenodo}, version doi {10.5281/zenodo.3559187}, url {https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.3559186} Wiki Checkout Wiki pages Multiple-object tracking pointclouds Velodyne VLP-16

name: default_cfg_fkie
description:
The configuration node loads a given launch configuration and offers services to 
     list or start the contained nodes. It provides additional description 
     extracted from launch file. This is used by node_manager_fkie.

name: master_discovery_fkie
description:
Discover the running ROS Masters in local network. The 
     discovering is done by sending an echo heartbeat messages to a defined 
     multicast group.
     The alternative is to use a zeroconf/avahi daemon to register the ROS 
     master as service and discover other ROS masters.

name: master_sync_fkie
description:
Synchronize the local ROS master to the remote masters 
     discovered by master_discovery_fkie node. The registration
     of topics and services is only perform by local ROS master.

name: multimaster_fkie
description:
The metapackage to combine the nodes required to establish and manage a multimaster network. 
    This requires no or minimal configuration. The changes are automatically detected and synchronized.

name: multimaster_msgs_fkie
description: The messages required by multimaster packages.

name: node_manager_fkie
description:
Graphical interface, written in PySide, to manage the running and 
     configured ROS nodes on different hosts. For discovering 
     the running ROS master master_discovery node will be used.

maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy

name: base_local_planner
description: This package provides implementations of the Trajectory Rollout and Dynamic Window approaches to local robot navigation on a plane. Given a plan to follow and a costmap, the controller produces velocity commands to send to a mobile base. This package supports both holonomic and non-holonomic robots, any robot footprint that can be represented as a convex polygon or circle, and exposes its configuration as ROS parameters that can be set in a launch file. This package's ROS wrapper adheres to the BaseLocalPlanner interface specified in the
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Eric Perko, contradict <AT> gmail.com

name: carrot_planner
description: This planner attempts to find a legal place to put a carrot for the robot to follow. It does this by moving back along the vector between the robot and the goal point.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Sachin Chitta, contradict <AT> gmail.com

name: clear_costmap_recovery
description: This package provides a recovery behavior for the navigation stack that attempts to clear space by reverting the costmaps used by the navigation stack to the static map outside of a given area.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy

name: costmap_2d
description:
This package provides an implementation of a 2D costmap that takes in sensor
        data from the world, builds a 2D or 3D occupancy grid of the data (depending
        on whether a voxel based implementation is used), and inflates costs in a
        2D costmap based on the occupancy grid and a user specified inflation radius.
        This package also provides support for map_server based initialization of a
        costmap, rolling window based costmaps, and parameter based subscription to
        and configuration of sensor topics.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, David V. Lu!!, Dave Hershberger, contradict <AT> gmail.com

name: dwa_local_planner
description:
This package provides an implementation of the Dynamic Window Approach to
        local robot navigation on a plane. Given a global plan to follow and a
        costmap, the local planner produces velocity commands to send to a mobile
        base. This package supports any robot who's footprint can be represented as
        a convex polygon or cicrle, and exposes its configuration as ROS parameters
        that can be set in a launch file. The parameters for this planner are also
        dynamically reconfigurable. This package's ROS wrapper adheres to the
        BaseLocalPlanner interface specified in the
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy

name: fake_localization
description: A ROS node that simply forwards odometry information.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy

name: global_planner
description: A path planner library and node.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy

description: map_server provides the
