name: nav_core
description: This package provides common interfaces for navigation specific robot actions. Currently, this package provides the BaseGlobalPlanner, BaseLocalPlanner, and RecoveryBehavior interfaces, which can be used to build actions that can easily swap their planner, local controller, or recovery behavior for new versions adhering to the same interface.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy

name: navfn
description:
navfn provides a fast interpolated navigation function that can be used to create plans for
        a mobile base. The planner assumes a circular robot and operates on a costmap to find a
        minimum cost plan from a start point to an end point in a grid. The navigation function is
        computed with Dijkstra's algorithm, but support for an A* heuristic may also be added in the
        near future. navfn also provides a ROS wrapper for the navfn planner that adheres to the
        nav_core::BaseGlobalPlanner interface specified in
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy
authors: Kurt Konolige, Eitan Marder-Eppstein, contradict <AT> gmail.com

name: navigation
description:
A 2D navigation stack that takes in information from odometry, sensor
        streams, and a goal pose and outputs safe velocity commands that are sent
        to a mobile base.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy
readme: ROS Navigation Stack navigation stack takes information odometry, sensor streams, goal pose outputs safe velocity commands mobile base. Related stacks: http://github.com/ros-planning/navigation_tutorials http://github.com/ros-planning/navigation_experimental

name: robot_pose_ekf
description: The Robot Pose EKF package is used to estimate the 3D pose of a robot, based on (partial) pose measurements coming from different sources. It uses an extended Kalman filter with a 6D model (3D position and 3D orientation) to combine measurements from wheel odometry, IMU sensor and visual odometry. The basic idea is to offer loosely coupled integration with different sensors, where sensor signals are received as ROS messages.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy

name: rotate_recovery
description: This package provides a recovery behavior for the navigation stack that attempts to clear space by performing a 360 degree rotation of the robot.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy

name: voxel_grid
description: voxel_grid provides an implementation of an efficient 3D voxel grid. The occupancy grid can support 3 different representations for the state of a cell: marked, free, or unknown. Due to the underlying implementation relying on bitwise and and or integer operations, the voxel grid only supports 16 different levels per voxel column. However, this limitation yields raytracing and cell marking performance in the grid comparable to standard 2D structures making it quite fast compared to most 3D structures.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Eric Berger, contradict <AT> gmail.com

name: nav2d
description: Meta-Package containing modules for 2D-Navigation

name: nav2d_exploration
description:
This package holds a collection of plugins for the RobotNavigator, that provide
    different cooperative exploration strategies for a team of mobile robots.

name: nav2d_karto
description:
Graph-based Simultaneous Localization and Mapping module.
    Includes OpenKarto GraphSLAM library by "SRI International".

name: nav2d_localizer
description:
Wrapper around Particle Filter implementation.
    The SelfLocalizer can be used as library or as a ros-node.

name: nav2d_msgs
description: Messages used for 2D-Navigation.

name: nav2d_navigator
description:
This package provides a node for higher level navigation of a mobile
    robot in a planar environment. It needs a map and the robot's position
    within this map to create a plan for navigation. When used together with
    a SLAM module it can also be used to perform autonomous exploration of
    the robot's workspace.

name: nav2d_operator
description:
The operator is a lightweight, purely reactive obstacle-avoidance
    module for mobile robots moving in a planar environment. The operator node
    works by evaluating a set of predefined motion primitives based on a local
    costmap and a desired direction. The best evaluated motion command will be
    send to the mobile base.

name: nav2d_remote
description:
This package is used to manually control a robot that uses the operator and
    navigator node from navigation_2d. Currently there is one node to control one
    robot with a joystick and one to control multiple robots in simulation.
    It can send commands directly to the operator or start and stop navigator actions.

name: nav2d_tutorials
description: Contains a set of tutorials that run 2D-Navigation within Stage-Simulator.

name: assisted_teleop
description:
The assisted_teleop node subscribes to a desired trajectory topic
    (geometry_msgs/Twist) and uses TrajectoryPlannerROS to find a valid
    trajectory close to the desired trajectory before republishing. Useful for
    filtering teleop commands while avoiding obstacles. This package also
    contains LaserScanMaxRangeFilter, which is a LaserScan filter plugin that
    takes max range values in a scan and turns them into valid values that are
    slightly less than max range.
readme: assisted_teleop assisted_teleop node subscribes desired trajectory topic (geometry_msgs/Twist) TrajectoryPlannerROS find valid trajectory close desired trajectory republishing. filtering teleop commands avoiding obstacles. package LaserScanMaxRangeFilter, LaserScan filter plugin takes max range values scan turns valid values slightly max range.

name: goal_passer
description:
A global planner plugin for move_base that simply passes the target pose on
    as a global plan. Useful for debugging local planners.
readme: goal_passer global planner plugin move_base simply passes target pose global plan. debugging local planners.

name: navigation_experimental
description:
A collection of navigation plugins and tools: Various recovery behaviors,
    local and global planner plugins for move_base, a teleop filter for
    obstacle avoidance, a simple control-based move_base replacement
    etc.

name: pose_base_controller
description:
A node that provides the move_base action server interface, but instead of
    planning simply drives towards the target pose using a control-based
    approach.
readme: pose_base_controller node move_base action server interface, planning simply drives target pose control-based approach.

name: pose_follower
description: A implementation of a local planner that attempts to follow a plan as closely as possible.

name: nodelet

The nodelet package is designed to provide a way to run multiple
    algorithms in the same process with zero copy transport between
    algorithms.

    for instantiating nodelets.

name: omronsentech_camera
description: omronsentech_camera package contains a basic functionality to access OMRON SENTECH Camera. This package requires installation of SentechSDK.
readme: ROS上のOMRON SENTECHカメラSDK omronsentech_cameraパッケージはオムロン 本パッケージ対応カメラはLinux版のSentechSDK対応カメラと同様です。 カメラの機能やGenICam GenApiノードのアクセスはSentechSDKのカメラ制御機能(Transport Layer) GenICam GenApiノードとROSにあるノードとは全く別の物であることを注意下さい。 各GenICam GenApiノードは、汎用インタフェースを介してアクセス可能なカメラデバイスの機能またはレジスタに対応しています。GenICam GenApiの詳細はGenICam GenApiドキュメント（英語）を参照下さい。 本パッケージはUbuntu 64ビット上のROS-KineticとUbuntu 64ビット上のROS-Melodicで動作確認済みです。 SentechSDKのカメラ制御機能(Transport Layer) モジュールを利用するため、本パッケージをインストールする際は事前にSentechSDKをインストールする必要があります。下記のURLでSentechSDK (USB3VisionとGigEVision)がダウンロードできます： https://sentech.co.jp/products/USB/software.html. 本パッケージは下記のROSパッケージが必要となります： camera_info_manager image_transport message_generation sensor_msgs std_msgs pluginlib nodelet roscpp roslaunch roslint rospy インストール手順として、catkinのワークスペースにomronsentech_camera.gitをクローンするかこのレポジトリに全ファイルをコピーして下さい。 通常のROSパッケージとしてパッケージをビルドする前にSentechSDKの環境変数の設定を確認して下さい: echo $STAPI_ROOT_PATH 環境変数が未設定の場合は、下記のコマンドを実行して下さい（SentechSDKを/opt/sentechにインストールした場合）： source /opt/sentech/.stprofile omronsentech_cameraの内部構成は以下のとおりです。 |-./README.md |-./README-ja.md |-./CMakeLists.txt |-./nodelet_plugins.xml |-./package.xml |-./rosdoc.yaml |-./doc/ |-./html/ |-./mainpage-en.md |-./mainpage-ja.md |-./manifest.yaml |-./examples/ |-./cpp/grabber.cpp |-./python/grabber.py |-./include/ |-./omronsentech_camera/*.h |-./launch/ |-./*.launch |-./*.yaml |-./msg/ |-./*.msg |-./src/ |-./omronsentech_camera/*.cpp |-./srv/ |-./*.srv docフォルダにあるドキュメントを生成する場合、omronsentech_cameraフォルダで下記のコマンドを実行下さい： rosdoc_lite doc 注意：Doxygenとrosdoc_liteパッケージが必要となります。 stcamera_nodeノード パッケージにあるstcamera_nodeはパラメータcamera_to_connectにより自動的に最初見つけたカメラ、指定したカメラや全カメラと接続し、画像を取得します。 stcamera_nodeノードの実行は次のコマンドのいずれかを実行下さい： rosrun omronsentech_camera stcamera_node roslaunch omronsentech_camera stcamera_node.launch ノードをstcamera_node.launchファイルスクリプトで起動するとカメラ接続パラメータがstcamera_node.yamlで簡単に設定できます。 camera_to_connect 接続可能なカメラの設定。このパラメーターは、stcamera_nodeが実行される前に設定する必要があります。 実行中にこのパラメーターを変更しても、その変更は反映されません。設定無しの場合、最初に見つけたカメラを接続します。”all”を設定する場合、見つけた全てのカメラを接続します。CAMERA_ID又はCAMERA_MODEL(SERIAL)を設定する場合、指定したカメラのみと接続します。例： camera_to_connect:[]: camera_to_connect:["all"]: camera_to_connect:["00:11:1c:f6:yy:xx","STC-MCS510U3V(00XXYY0)"]: MACアドレス「00:11:1c:f6:yy:xx」のGigEVisionカメラとシリアル番号「00XXYY0」のUSB3Visionカメラ(STC-MCS510U3V)のみ接続します。 camera_to_connect:["14210003XXYY"]: ID「14210003XXYY」のカメラのみ接続します. default_calibration_file 指定するキャリブレーションファイルがないカメラを利用するデフォールトキャリブレーションファイルパス。stcamera_nodeが実行中にこのパラメータが変更され、カメラもすでに接続されている場合は、変更を適用するためにカメラの画像取得を無効にして再度有効にして下さい。キャリブレーションファイルの作成と使い方は下記のROSのチュートリアルを参照下さい（英語）： http://wiki.ros.org/camera_calibration/Tutorials/MonocularCalibration http://wiki.ros.org/camera_calibration_parsers http://wiki.ros.org/image_proc {dev_CAMERA-NS}/calibration_file ネームスペース「{dev_CAMERA-NS}」が持っているカメラ用のキャリブレーションファイルパス。stcamera_nodeが実行中にこのパラメータが変更され、カメラもすでに接続されている場合は、変更を適用するためにカメラの画像取得を無効にして再度有効にして下さい。カメラネームスペースの詳細は次の章をご参照下さい。 stcamera_nodeはカメラと接続する際にカメラに対するトピックとサービスを宣言します。接続されたカメラを区別するために、各カメラには独自の名前空間（ネームスペース）があります。カメラのネームスペースは下記のルールで自動的に生成します： camera_to_connectの設定無又は[“all”]の場合、ネームスペースの形式は dev_{CAMERA_ID}になります。{CAMERA_ID} はカメラのID。接続されたカメラIDが「14210003xxYY」の場合、ネームスペースは「dev_14210003xxYY」になります. GigEVisionのMACアドレスなどのカメラIDの英数字以外の文字は、アンダースコアに置き換えられます。 camera_to_connectがCAMERA_MODEL(SERIAL)或いはCAMERA_IDの場合、ネームスペースの形式はdev_{CAMERA_MODEL_SERIAL_}或いは dev_{CAMERA_ID}になります。{CAMERA_MODEL_SERIAL_}はCAMERA_MODEL(SERIAL)からの書き換えられた文字列です. {CAMERA_ID} {dev_CAMERA-NS}の値はサービスコールget_device_listまたdevice_connectionトピックでも得られます。 stcamera_nodeが発行するトピックは下記の通りです： device_connection {dev_CAMERA-NS}/camera_info ROSのimage_transport基準です。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/chunk {dev_CAMERA-NS}/event {dev_CAMERA-NS}/image_raw* 画像データを取得したときに発行されます（ROSのimage_transport基準）。 stcamera_nodeが提供するサービスは下記の通りです： （下記に記載するGenICamノードはROSのノードとは全く別の物です） get_device_list get_gige_ip 指定したGigEVisionカメラのIPアドレスを取得します。 get_module_list 対応するGenICam GenTLモジュール名のリストを取得します（System、Interface、LocalDevice、RemoteDevice、DataStream）。 モジュール名は特定のGenICamノードにアクセスする際に引数として渡す必要があります。たとえば、readまたはwrite_nodeサービス経由でカメラのGainにアクセスするには、GainがRemoteDeviceモジュールに存在するように指定する必要があります。 get_sdk_info SentechSDKバージョンとGenTLプロデューサの情報を取得します。 set_gige_ip 指定したGigEVisionカメラのIPアドレスを設定します。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/enable_chunk {dev_CAMERA-NS}/enable_event_acquisition {dev_CAMERA-NS}/enable_event_node 指定されるGenICamノードのイベントコールバックを有効または無効にします。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/enable_image_acquisition {dev_CAMERA-NS}/enable_trigger {dev_CAMERA-NS}/execute_node 指定されるCommandインタフェースタイプを持つ特定のGenicamノードを実行します。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/get_chunk_list {dev_CAMERA-NS}/get_enum_list 指定されるGenICam列挙ノードの列挙のリストを取得します。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/get_event_acquisition_status_list {dev_CAMERA-NS}/get_event_node_status_list 指定されるモジュールのGenICamノードイベント取得のステータスを取得します。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/get_genicam_node_info 指定されるGenICamノードの情報を取得します。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/get_image_acquisition_status {dev_CAMERA-NS}/get_last_error {dev_CAMERA-NS}/get_trigger_list {dev_CAMERA-NS}/image_raw* ROSのimage_transportに基づくサービスです. {dev_CAMERA-NS}/read_node ノードインタフェースタイプに関係なく、指定されるGenICamノードを読み込みます。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/read_node_bool ブール型のインタフェースタイプを持つ指定されるGenICamノードを読み取ります。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/read_node_enum 列挙型のインタフェースタイプを持つ指定されるGenICamノードを読み取ります。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/read_node_float Floatのインターフェイスタイプを持つ指定されるGenICamノードを読み取ります。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/read_node_int 整数のインターフェイスタイプを持つ指定されるGenICamノードを読み取ります。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/read_node_string Stringのインターフェイスタイプを持つ指定されるGenICamノードを読み取ります。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/send_soft_trigger {dev_CAMERA-NS}/set_camera_info ROSのimage_transportに基づくサービスです. {dev_CAMERA-NS}/write_node ノードインタフェースタイプに関係なく、指定されるGenICamノードを書き込みます。stcamera_nodeの内部は自動的に渡される文字列値をGenICamノードのインタフェースタイプに変換します。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/write_node_bool ブール型のインタフェースタイプを持つ指定されるGenICamノードを書き込みます。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/write_node_enum_int 列挙型のインタフェースタイプを持つ指定されるGenICamノードに整数値を書き込みます。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/write_node_enum_str 列挙型のインタフェースタイプを持つ指定されるGenICamノードに文字列値（列挙型エントリの記号名）を書き込みます。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/write_node_float Floatのインタフェースタイプを持つ指定されるGenICamノードに整数値を書き込みます。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/write_node_int 整数のインタフェースタイプを持つ指定されるGenICamノードに整数値を書き込みます。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/write_node_string Stringのインタフェースタイプを持つ指定されるGenICamノードに整数値を書き込みます。 機種によって画像取得中にはGenICamノードにアクセスできない場合があります（エラー発生）。その際には画像取得を停止にしてから再度設定やアクセスして下さい。サービスコールenable_image_acquisitionで画像取得停止ができます。例： rosservice call /stcamera_node/dev_CAMERA-NS/enable_image_acquisition false IntegerタイプのGenICamノードは設定できる倍数が決まっており、その倍数で設定しないとエラーになります。サービスコール get_genicam_node_infoで倍数の確認ができます。下記の例の倍数は16 (increment: rosservice call /stcamera_node/dev_CAMERA-NS/get_genicam_node_info RemoteDevice Width "Width" description: "Width image device pixels)." name_space: "Standard" interface_type: "IInteger" access_mode: "Read is_cachable: visibility: "Beginner" caching_mode: "Write register, write cache read" is_streamable: True enum_value_str_list: enum_value_int_list: current_value: min_value: max_value: increment: unit: child_node_list: GenTLエラーコード。GenICam GenTLドキュメント（英語）をご参照下さい。 GenICamのジェネリック例外が発生しました。 (GenICam) カメラのGainを変更し、image_viewで取得画像を表示する 全カメラとの接続を設定し、stcamera_nodeを実行します: roslaunch omronsentech_camera stcamera_node.launch rosservice call /stcamera_node/get_device_list device_list: timestamp: secs: nsecs: device_id: device_model: "STC-MCS510U3V" device_serial: "XXYYYZZ" device_tltype: device_tl_specific_field: device_tl_specific_value: device_gentl_info: vendor: "OMRON_SENTECH" version: producer_version: "1.5.X.X.X" full_path: "/opt/sentech/lib/libstgentl.cti" tltype: "Mixed" device_namespace: "dev_14210XXYYYZZ" connected: True image_viewを実行します： rosrun image_view image_view image:=/stcamera_node/dev_14210XXYYYZZ/image_raw、dev_14210XXYYYZZは接続されているカメラのネームスペースです。 カメラのGain（IFloatタイプ）を100.0に変更する場合は、次のようにGenicam書き込みノード・サービスを使用します： rosservice call /stcamera_node/dev_14210XXYYYZZ/write_node_float RemoteDevice Gain rosservice call /stcamera_node/dev_14210XXYYYZZ/write_node RemoteDevice Gain 現在のGain値を読み取るには、次のようにGenICam読み取りノードサービスを使用します： rosservice call /stcamera_node/dev_14210XXYYYZZ/read_node_float RemoteDevice rosservice call /stcamera_node/dev_14210XXYYYZZ/read_node RemoteDevice Gain get_genicam_node_info でGainノード情報を確認する場合は： rosservice call /stcamera_node/dev_14210XXYYYZZ/get_genicam_node_info RemoteDevice Gain "Gain" description: "Controls selected gain absolute physical amplification\ factor applied video signal." name_space: "Standard" interface_type: "IFloat" access_mode: "Read Write" is_cachable: visibility: "Beginner" caching_mode: "Write register, write cache read" is_streamable: True enum_value_str_list: enum_value_int_list: current_value: min_value: max_value: increment: unit: child_node_list: Launchファイルスクリプトの事例 Launchファイルスクリプトの例とそのパラメータはパッケージに含まれています。 stcamera_node.launch: このLaunchファイルスクリプトはstcamera_nodeを実行します。検出されたカメラと接続します。 このスクリプトはstcamera_node.yamlにキャリブレーションファイル（オプション）を設定せず、そのまま起動することができます。 stcamera_nodelet_manager.launch: このLaunchファイルスクリプトは、nodeletバージョンのstcamera_nodeを実行します。検出されたカメラと接続します。このスクリプトはstcamera_nodelet_manager.yamlにキャリブレーションファイル（オプション）を設定せず、そのまま起動することができます。 stcamera_node_imageproc.launch: このLaunchファイルスクリプトは、接続されたカメラからストリーミングされた画像を表示するstcamera_nodeとimage_viewという2つのノードを実行します。 このスクリプトを起動する際に、stcamera_node_imageproc.launchとstcamera_node_imageproc.yamlにCAMERA_IDまたはCAMERA_MODEL（SERIAL）および名前空間dev_CAMERA-NSの指定が必要となります： stcamera_node_imageproc.launchにあるcamera_namespace： <arg name="camera_namespace" default="dev_CAMERA-NS" stcamera_node_imageproc.yamlにあるcamera_to_connect： camera_to_connect: ["CAMERA_ID CAMERA_MODEL(SERIAL)"] stcamera_node_imageproc.yamlにあるdefault_calibration_file又は{dev_CAMERA-NS}/calibration_file：default_calibration_file: file:///home/mydata/calibration.xml ROSノードクライアント事例 exampleフォルダにあるノード「grabber」はシンプルなROSノードクライアント（両方C++とPythonベースがあります）事例として提供します。Grabberは下記の処理を行います： サービス「get_sdk_info」を呼び出して、SentechSDKとGenTL情報を取得し、画面に表示します。 サービス「get_device_list」を呼び出して、接続済みのカメラを検出し、カメラのネームスペースを取得します。 サービス「get_genicam_node_info」と「write_node_float」を呼び出して、カメラのGain値を読み取りと書き込みます。 トピック「image_raw」を参照して、十枚まで画像を取得し、画像サイズとエンコード情報を画面に表示します。 サービス「enable_trigger」を呼び出して、トリガモードを有効にして、十回まで一秒間ごとにサービス「send_soft_trigger」を呼び出して、画像を取得します。トリガモードを有効にする際、セレクタはFrameStartを指定し、ソースはSoftwareを指定します。 GigEVisionカメラを利用する場合は、カメラに有効なIPアドレスがあることを確認して下さい。IPアドレスは、サービス「get_device_list」または「get_gige_ip」を呼び出すことで確認できます。静的IPアドレスは、サービス「set_gige_ip」で設定できます。例： rosservice call /stcamera_node/get_gige_ip "00:11:1c:xx:xx:xx" （MACアドレス「00:11:1c:xx:xx:xx」を持っているGigEVisionカメラのIPアドレスを取得する場合）、及び rosservice call /stcamera_node/set_gige_ip "00:11:1c:xx:xx:xx" （MACアドレス「00:11:1c:xx:xx:xx」を持っているGigEVisionカメラの静的IPアドレスを設定する場合） stcamera_nodeは、OMRON SENTECHカメラを制御するために、一般的な機能を提供します。特定のカメラに対応するためのカスタマイズが必要な場合は、StCameraInterfaceクラスを継承し、別途のROSノードを作成するか既存のstcamera_node（StCameraNodeクラス）を編集する必要があります。stcamera_node（StCameraNodeクラス）を編集する場合、StCameraNodeクラスのinitializeCamera()関数にあるカメラインターフェイス初期化プロセスを編集する必要があります。 StCameraInterfaceやStCameraNodeクラスの構造については、doxygenドキュメントを参照下さい。 API コードのAPIやMsg/Srv APIのドキュメントは下記のURLでアクセスできます：https://wiki.ros.org/omronsentech_camera.

name: open_manipulator
description:
ROS-enabled OpenManipulator is a full open robot platform consisting of OpenSoftware​, OpenHardware and OpenCR(Embedded board)​.
    The OpenManipulator is allowed users to control it more easily by linking with the MoveIt! package. Moreover it has full hardware compatibility with TurtleBot3​. 
    Even if you do not have a real robot, you can control the robot in the Gazebo simulator​.
authors: Darby Lim, Hye-Jong KIM, Ryan Shim, Yong-Ho Na

name: oxford_gps_eth
description: Ethernet interface to OxTS GPS receivers (NCOM packet structure)
readme: Ethernet interface OxTS GPS receivers NCOM packet structure Tested RT3000v2 receiver. Published Topics gps/fix (sensor_msgs/NavSatFix) GPS position geodetic coordinates (latitude longitude) gps/vel (geometry_msgs/TwistWithCovarianceStamped) GPS velocity ENU frame linear.x Velocity due East linear.y Velocity due North linear.z Velocity gps/odom (nav_msgs/Odometry) GPS position heading UTM frame, velocity vehicle body frame pose.position.x UTM Easting meters pose.position.y UTM Northing meters pose.orientation Yaw quaternion representing heading UTM. mind UTM heading relative UTM grid, necessarily aligned due East. phenomenon “grid convergence”. Wikipedia details. twist.linear.x Local frame velocity vehicle twist.linear.y Local frame lateral velocity vehicle twist.angular.z Yaw rate vehicle rad/s imu/data (sensor_msgs/Imu) Orientation, angular rates, linear accelerations reported OxTS unit gps/pos_type (std_msgs/String) Position solution type reported OxTS unit (point position, differential pseudorange, RTK float, RTK integer, gps/nav_status (std_msgs/String) Navigation status reported OxTS unit. fix, vel, odom, imu/data topics published topic string “READY”. gps/time_ref (sensor_msgs/TimeReference) UTC equivalent GPS timestamp reported OxTS unit. Parameters interface Restrict single network interface, eth0. Default <empty> ip_address Local listen address. Default <empty> port Local UDP listen port. Default frame_id_gps frame-ID geodetic GPS position IMU. Default gps frame_id_vel frame-ID ENU GPS velocity. Default enu frame_id_odom frame-ID UTM odometry. Default utm usage rosrun oxford_gps_eth gps_node roslaunch oxford_gps_eth gps.launch FAQ Connected Oxford GPS <ip_address>:3000 GPS position velocity messages published. Data published OxTS unit reports completely initialized. Drive faster short period time powering OxTS unit initialize sensor. gps/nav_status string topic status initialization process. string shows “READY”, GPS position velocity messages start publishing.

name: pilz_trajectory_generation
description: The pilz_trajectory_generation package containing the MoveIt! plugin pilz_command_planner.
readme: Overview package trajectory generator plan standard robot motions PTP, LIN, CIRC form MoveIt! PlannerManager plugin. MoveIt! MoveIt! state art software mobile manipulation, incorporating latest advances motion planning, manipulation, perception, kinematics, control navigation. detailed information, refer MoveIt! website. ROS API User Interface MoveGroup package implements planning_interface::PlannerManager interface MoveIt!. loading planning pipeline (pilz_command_planner_planning_pipeline.launch.xml prbt_moveit_config package), trajectory generation functionalities accessed user interface python rviz) move_group node, /plan_kinematics_path service /move_group action. detailed tutorials refer MoveIt! Tutorials. Joint Limits commands planner limitations robot joint. limits calculated limits set urdf limits set parameter server put loading *.yaml file prbt_moveit_config/config/joint_limits.yaml). Note setting position limits velocity limits urdf parameter server setting acceleration limits parameter server. extension common has_acceleration max_acceleration parameter added ability set has_deceleration max_deceleration(<0!). limits merged premise limits parameter server stricter equal parameters set urdf. calculated trajectory respect limits strictest combination limits common limit joints. Cartesian Limits cartesian trajectory generation (LIN/CIRC) planner information maximum speed cartesian space. translational/rotational velocity/acceleration/deceleration set parameter server cartesian_limits: max_trans_vel: max_trans_acc: max_trans_dec: max_rot_vel: planners assume acceleration ratio translational rotational trapezoidal shapes. rotational acceleration calculated max_trans_acc max_trans_vel max_rot_vel deceleration Planning Interface defined user interface MoveIt!, package moveit_msgs::MotionPlanRequest moveit_msgs::MotionPlanResponse input output motion planning. message types designed comprehensive general. parameters needed specific planning algorithm explained detail. general introduction fill MotionPlanRequest Move Group Interface Tutorial. planner able handle commands. put “PTP”, “LIN” “CIRC” planner_id motion request. PTP motion command planner generates full synchronized point point trajectories trapezoidal joint velocity profile. joints assumed maximal joint velocity/acceleration/deceleration limits. strictest limits adopted. axis longest time reach goal selected lead axis. axes decelerated share acceleration/constant velocity/deceleration phases lead axis. Planning result joint trajectory. user adapt Cartesian velocity/acceleration scaling factor motion plan fails due violation joint space limits. name: pinocchio
description: A fast and flexible implementation of Rigid Body Dynamics algorithms and their analytical derivatives.
readme: Pinocchio instantiates state-of-the-art Rigid Body Algorithms poly-articulated systems based revisited Roy Featherstone’s algorithms. Pinocchio analytical derivatives main Rigid-Body Algorithms, Recursive Newton-Euler Algorithm Articulated-Body Algorithm. Pinocchio tailored robotics applications, contexts (biomechanics, computer graphics, vision, built Eigen linear algebra FCL collision detection. Pinocchio Python interface fast code prototyping, accessible Conda. Pinocchio heart robotics software Crocoddyl, open-source efficient Differential Dynamic Programming solver robotics, Stack-of-Tasks, open-source versatile hierarchical controller framework Humanoid Path Planner, open-source software Motion Manipulation Planning. learn Pinocchio internal behaviors main features, invite read related paper online documentation. dive Pinocchio single line sufficient (assuming Conda): conda install pinocchio conda-forge pip Linux): pip install pin Table contents Table contents Introducing Pinocchio Pinocchio main features Documentation Examples Tutorials Pinocchio continuous integrations Performances Ongoing developments Installation ROS Visualization Citing Pinocchio Questions Issues Credits Open-source projects relying Pinocchio Acknowledgments Introducing Pinocchio Pinocchio3 latest major release Pinocchio. multiple features, Sparse constrained dynamics analytical derivatives Full support closed-loop mechanisms State-of-the-art frictional contact solvers Low-complexity constrained articulated body algorithms Full support multiple-precision floating-point (MPFR) Python Full CasADi support Python Increased support CppAD CppADCodeGen SDF MJCF parsers Pinocchio main features Pinocchio fast: template library, cache friendly, automatic code generation support CppADCodeGen. Pinocchio versatile, implementing basic advanced rigid body dynamics algorithms: kinematics analytical derivatives, forward/inverse dynamics analytical derivatives, centroidal dynamics analytical derivatives, support multiple precision arithmetic Boost.Multiprecision similar framework, computations kinematic dynamic regressors system identification support modern open-source Automatic Differentiation frameworks CppAD CasADi. Pinocchio flexible: header compliant. Pinocchio extensible. Pinocchio multi-thread friendly. Pinocchio reliable extensively tested (unit-tests, simulations, real-world robotics applications). Pinocchio supported tested Windows, Mac Unix, Linux build status Documentation online Pinocchio documentation release cheat sheet pdf main functions algorithms Examples examples directory, provide basic examples Pinocchio Python. Additional examples introducing Pinocchio documentation. Tutorials Pinocchio large bunch tutorials aiming introducing basic tools robot control. Tutorial training documents listed interactive Jupyter notebook set tutorials developed Nicolas Mansard Yann Mont-Marin. Pinocchio continuous integrations Pinocchio constantly tested platforms distributions, reported ROS Linux APT OSX Conda Windows Conda Linux Robotpkg Performances Pinocchio exploits, sparsity induced kinematic tree robotics systems. modern programming language paradigms, Pinocchio unroll computations compile time, allowing achieve impressive performances large range robots, illustrated plot obtained standard laptop equipped Intel Core CPU GHz. benchmarks, capacity Pinocchio exploit, CPU capacities advanced code generation techniques, refer technical paper. addition, introspection understand compare performances modern rigid body dynamics libraries. Ongoing developments follow current developments, refer devel branch. master branch latest release. Pull Request submitted devel branch. Installation Pinocchio easily installed Linux (Ubuntu, Fedora, Unix distributions (Mac BSD, refer installation procedure. Conda simply simple line: conda install pinocchio conda-forge ROS Pinocchio deployed ROS. follow deployment status you’re interested Pinocchio systems and/or packages integrate ROS ecosystem, recommend installation Pinocchio binaries distributed ROS PPA. install Pinocchio sudo apt install ros-$ROS_DISTRO-pinocchio installs Pinocchio HPP-FCL support Python bindings. Pinocchio ROS packages Depending Pinocchio package.xml config (<depend>pinocchio</depend>) Including Pinocchio CMake (find_package(pinocchio REQUIRED)) linking Pinocchio (target_link_libraries(my_library pinocchio::pinocchio)) include support hooks discover package ROS ROS Examples repositories: ROS ROS note advise including pinocchio/fwd.hpp header include avoid compilation errors differing Boost-variant sizes. name: play_motion
description: Plays a pre-recorded motion on a robot
readme: play_motion tool play pre-recorded motions ros_control compliant robots simple actionlib interface. robot implements control joint_trajectory_controller put motion play_motion. Starting version play_motion default compute collision-free trajectory robot’s current state motion waypoint. requires robot running moveit setup, configure play_motion you’re interested motion planning capability, disable altogether. Refer config/ folder configuration examples. How-to Prepare motion. loaded rosparams. find samples config/ folder. rosparam load config/your_motion_file.yaml Launch play_motion motion planning capabilities, simplicity) rosparam set /play_motion/disable_motion_planning true rosrun play_motion play_motion Fire actionlib client publishes /play_motion action server, enter motion motion_name field set skip_planning=true motion planning.

name: plotjuggler
description: PlotJuggler: juggle with data
readme: PlotJuggler PlotJuggler tool visualize time series fast, powerful intuitive. Noteworthy features: Simple Drag Drop user interface. Load static data file. Connect live streaming data. Save visualization layout configurations re-use Fast OpenGL visualization. handle thousands timeseries millions data points. Transform data simple editor: derivative, moving average, integral, PlotJuggler easily extended plugins. Data sources (file streaming) Load CSV files. Load ULog Subscribe streaming sources: MQTT, Websockets, ZeroMQ, UDP, ROS, Understands data formats JSON, CBOR, BSON, Message Pack, Open Rosbags subscribe ROS topics ROS1 ROS2). Easily add custom data source and/or formats… Transform analyze data PlotJuggler easy visualize data analyze manipulate time series simple extendable Transform Editor. Alternatively, Custom Function Editor, create Multi-input Single-output functions scripting language based Lua. familiar Lua, don’t afraid, won’t minutes learn “Download Run” Linux, simplest install PlotJuggler snaps: Windows Installer: PlotJuggler_WIN_Installer-2.8.4.exe build Clone repository usual: git clone --recurse-submodules https://github.com/facontidavide/PlotJuggler.git binary dependency installed system Ubuntu, debians installed command: sudo apt install qtbase5-dev libqt5svg5-dev libqt5websockets5-dev libqt5opengl5-dev Fedora: sudo dnf install qt5-qtbase-devel qt5-qtsvg-devel qt5-websockets-devel qt5-qtopendl-devel compile cmake (qmake supported): mkdir build; build cmake sudo install Note: plugins installed folder executable. Note ROS users install PlotJuggler, type: sudo apt install ros-${ROS_DISTRO}-plotjuggler-ros run roslaunch plotjuggler_ros plotjuggler.launch version ROS plugins moved separate repository: https://github.com/PlotJuggler/plotjuggler-ros-plugins Refer instructions repository compile ROS plugins source. PlotJuggler, support PlotJuggler required lot work developed; goal build intuitive powerfull tool visualize data timeseries. find PlotJuggler making donation PayPal Github Sponsor. name: pluginlib
description:
The pluginlib package provides tools for writing and dynamically loading plugins using the ROS build infrastructure.
    To work, these tools require plugin providers to register their plugins in the package.xml of their package.
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Tully Foote, Dirk Thomas, Mirza Shah

name: pointcloud_to_laserscan
description: Converts a 3D Point Cloud into a 2D laser scan. This is useful for making devices like the Kinect appear like a laser scanner for 2D-based algorithms (e.g. laser-based SLAM).

description: The pr2_mannequin_mode package

name: pr2_position_scripts
description: This package contains a number of scripts to set various components of the PR2. They are used in the apps to improve usablity.

name: pr2_teleop
description: The pr2_teleop package

name: pr2_teleop_general
description: pr2_teleop_general

name: pr2_tuckarm
description:
Tucks the arms of the PR2 robot into a safe position for moving the base of the robot.
     This also moves the arms out of the view of the tilting laser scanner, as much as possible.

name: dense_laser_assembler
description:
Stores streaming data from a laser sensor in a
    dense representation. This allows the user to do 'image-like'
    processing on the data, and can also be used for very fast approx
    neighborhood searches.  This package is still experimental and unstable.
    Expect its APIs to change.

name: laser_joint_processor
description:
Computes joint angles associated with a specific set of detected checkerboard corners.
     This package is experimental and unstable.
     Expect its APIs to change.

name: laser_joint_projector
description:
Projects laser readings into a point cloud, based on a set of recorded joint angles
     This package is experimental and unstable.
     Expect its APIs to change.

name: pr2_calibration
description: The pr2_calibration package

name: pr2_calibration_launch
description:
Launch files and configuration files needed to run the calibration pipeline on PR2. This package is
     still experimental. Expect large changes tp occur.

name: pr2_dense_laser_snapshotter
description:
Stores the data from a series of laser scan messages in a dense representation, allowing
     users to easily perform image-like operations on intensity or range data. This package is
     experimental. Expect APIs to change.

name: pr2_se_calibration_launch
description: pr2_se_calibration_launch

name: pr2_common
description: URDF description of the robot kinematics and dynamics, 3D models of robot components, information required for gazebo to simulate the PR2, and messages specific to the PR2 such as detailed information about its power board and fingertip pressure sensors.

name: pr2_dashboard_aggregator
description: A simple script that aggregates all of the topics that a "pr2_dashboard" app might be interested in.

name: pr2_description
description:
This package contains the description (mechanical, kinematic, visual,
  etc.) of the PR2 robot.  The files in this package are parsed and used by
  a variety of other components.  Most users will not interact directly
  with this package.
authors: John Hsu, Eric Berger, Austin Hendrix

name: pr2_machine
description: This package contains the xxx.machine files that describe the different hosts a node can be spawned on. Currently there is one machine file for the pr2 robot, and one for the simulated pr2 robot.

name: pr2_msgs
description: Messages for representing PR2 state, such as battery information and the PR2 fingertip sensors.
authors: Eric Berger and many others, Austin Hendrix

name: joint_trajectory_action_tools
description: joint_trajectory_action_tools

name: joint_trajectory_generator
description:
joint_trajectory_generator action takes in a trajectory specified
    by a number of joint positions, and it generates a new smooth trajectory
    through these joint positions.

description: Move the pr2 arm using inverse kinematics

name: pr2_common_action_msgs
description: The pr2_common_action_msgs package

description:
Various actions which help in moving the arms of the PR2
    or getting data from its tilting laser.
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Wim Meeusen, Vijay Pradeep, Radu Rusu, Melonee Wise

name: pr2_tilt_laser_interface
description:
Provides a set of tools/actions for manipulating the pr2's tilting
    laser. Simplifies previously complex tasks, such as fetching
    a single sweep, given a set of desired parameters for both the laser
    driver and tilting platform.
authors: Radu Rusu, Wim Meeusen, Vijay Pradeep

name: pr2_tuck_arms_action
description: The pr2_tuck_arms_action package

description:
Controllers to operate the digital output of the motor controller
    boards and the projector board. This package has not been reviewed and
    should be considered unstable.

name: joint_trajectory_action
description:
The joint_trajectory_action is a node that exposes an action interface
     to a joint trajectory controller.

name: pr2_calibration_controllers
description:
The pr2_calibration_controllers package contains the controllers
     used to bring all the joints in the PR2 to a calibrated state.

name: pr2_controllers
description: Contains the controllers that run in realtime on the PR2 and supporting packages.

name: pr2_controllers_msgs
description: Messages, services, and actions used in the pr2_controllers stack.

name: pr2_gripper_action
description:
The pr2_gripper_action provides an action interface for using the
  gripper. Users can specify what position to move to (while limiting the
  force) and the action will report success when the position is reached or
  failure when the gripper cannot move any longer.

name: pr2_head_action
description:
The PR2 head action is a node that provides an action interface for
  pointing the head of the PR2.  It passes trajectory goals to the
  controller, and reports success when they have finished executing.

name: pr2_mechanism_controllers
description:
The pr2_mechanism_controllers package contains realtime
    controllers that are meant for specific mechanisms of the PR2.
authors: Sachin Chita, John Hsu, Melonee Wise

name: robot_mechanism_controllers
description: Generic Mechanism Controller Library
authors: John Hsu, Melonee Wise, Stuart Glaser

name: single_joint_position_action
description:
The single joint position action is a node that provides an action
  interface for commanding a trajectory to move a joint to a particular
  position. The action reports success when the joint reaches the desired
  position.

name: pr2_gripper_sensor
description: The pr2_gripper_sensor package

name: pr2_gripper_sensor_action
description:
The pr2_gripper_sensor_action package provides an action interface for talking to the pr2_gripper_sensor_controller real-time controller.

  It provides several different actions for getting high-level sensor information from the PR2 palm-mounted accelerometers, fingertip pressure arrays, and gripper motor/encoder, as well as several sensor-based gripper control actions that respond with low-latency in real-time.
readme: pr2_gripper_sensor_action Gripper sensor action pr2_object_manipulation stack, upgraded hydro pr2_delivery

name: pr2_gripper_sensor_controller
description: The pr2_gripper_sensor_controller package is a real-time controller that integrates signals from the PR2 hand-mounted accelerometer and finger-mounted pressure sensors with motor control of the gripper joint to do highly responsive sensing and low-latency closed-loop control.
readme: pr2_gripper_sensor_controller Gripper sensor controller package pr2_object_manipulation stack. Put seperate compilation pr2_delivery

name: pr2_gripper_sensor_msgs
description:
The pr2_gripper_sensor_msgs package contains various actions and messages that are used in the pr2_gripper_sensor* packages. The structure of the API used by pr2_gripper_sensor_action, and pr2_gripper_sensor_controller packages is as follows: 

Users will send a goal to an Action in the message format of PR2Gripper*Command (where * replaces the name of the particular Action from pr2_gripper_sensor_action). Feedback and Result information for the action is then returned in the format of PR2Gripper*Data.
readme: pr2_gripper_sensor_msgs Put pr2_delivery application, part pr2_object_manipulation stack

This package specifies the interface to a realtime controller. A

name: pr2_hardware_interface
description:
This package contains the C++ interfaces to the PR2 hardware
  components that are controlled over EtherCAT. This includes the
  motors and encoders needed to control the PR2 mechanism, as well as
  components like the pressure sensors in the fingertips, camera
  triggers, etc... All of the hardware components in this interface are
  directly available to the controllers inside the hard realtime
  control loop.

This package provides a node that monitors the state of the run stops of the pr2_robot. When the state of the
   run stop changes from off to on, this node will automatically enable the power to the motors, and reset
   the motors. This allows you to use the run stop as a 'pause' button. By using the run stop as a tool to
   power up the robot, the run stop is also in reach of the user once the robot starts moving.

name: pyquaternion
description: quaternion operations
readme: pyquaternion fully featured, pythonic library quaternion representation, manipulation, animation geometry. visit pyquaternion homepage full information latest documentation. Designed Python Smooth animation interpolation random orientations pyquaternion module. Quickstart Install PyPI pip install pyquaternion Run basic overview. copy [demo.py](./demo/demo.py). ```python import pyquaternion Create quaternion representing rotation degrees positive axis. my_quaternion pyquaternion.Quaternion(axis=[0, degrees=90) my_vector my_rotated_vector my_quaternion.rotate(my_vector) print('\nBasic Rotation') print('--------------') print('My Vector: {}'.format(my_vector)) print('Performing rotation {angle} deg {axis}'.format(angle=my_quaternion.degrees, axis=my_quaternion.axis)) print('My Rotated Vector: {}'.format(my_rotated_vector)) Create quaternion representing rotation null_quaternion pyquaternion.Quaternion(axis=[0, angle=0) print('\nInterpolated Rotation') print('---------------------') create sequence intermediate quaternion rotation objects pyquaternion.Quaternion.intermediates(null_quaternion, my_quaternion, include_endpoints=True): my_interpolated_point q.rotate(my_vector) print('My Interpolated Point: {point}\t(after rotation {angle} deg {axis})'.format( point=my_interpolated_point, angle=round(q.degrees, axis=q.axis print('Done!') output: Basic Rotation Vector: Performing rotation deg Rotated Vector: Interpolated Rotation Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg

name: python_qt_binding
description:
This stack provides Python bindings for Qt.
    There are two providers: pyside and pyqt.  PySide is released under
    the LGPL.  PyQt is released under the GPL.

    Both the bindings and tools to build bindings are included from each
    available provider.  For PySide, it is called "Shiboken".  For PyQt,
    this is called "SIP".

    Also provided is adapter code to make the user's Python code
    independent of which binding provider was actually used which makes
    it very easy to switch between these.
authors: Dave Hershberger, Dorian Scholz, Dirk Thomas

description: razor_imu_9dof is a package that provides a ROS driver for the Sparkfun OpenLog Artemis, 9DoF Razor IMU M0, 9DOF Razor IMU and 9DOF Sensor Stick. A node which displays the attitude (roll, pitch and yaw) of the board (or any IMU) is provided for testing.

name: rc_dynamics_api
description:
The rc_dynamics_api provides an API for easy handling of the dynamic-state data
      streams provided by Roboception's stereo camera with self-localization.
      See http://rc-visard.com

      Dynamic-state estimates of the rc_visard relate to its self-localization and
      ego-motion estimation. These states refer to rc_visard's current pose,
      velocity, or acceleration and are published on demand via several data streams.
      For a complete list and descriptions of these dynamics states and the
      respective data streams please refer to rc_visard's user manual.
authors: Heiko Hirschmueller, Christian Emmerich, Felix Endres

name: rc_genicam_api
description:
GenICam/GigE Vision Convenience Layer.

      This package combines the Roboception convenience layer for images with the
      GenICam reference implementation and a GigE Vision transport layer. It is a
      self contained package that permits configuration and image streaming of
      GenICam / GigE Vision 2.0 compatible cameras like the Roboception rc_visard.

      This package also provides some tools that can be called from the command line
      for discovering cameras, changing their configuration and streaming images.
      Although the tools are meant to be useful when working in a shell or in a
      script, their main purpose is to serve as example on how to use the API for
      reading and setting parameters, streaming and synchronizing images.

      See LICENSE.md for licensing terms of the different parts.
readme: Roboception GenICam Convenience Layer package combines Roboception convenience layer images GenICam reference implementation GigE Vision transport layer. contained package permits configuration image streaming GenICam GigE Vision compatible cameras Roboception rc_visard. API based compiled Linux Windows. package tools called command line discovering cameras, changing configuration streaming images. Prebuilt binaries downloaded releases page. name: rc_genicam_driver
description: The rc_genicam_driver provides data from a Roboception rc_visard 3D sensor or rc_cube on several ROS topics.
readme: rc_genicam_driver Nodelet/node providing ROS interface configure Roboception rc_visard rc_cube receive images. consult manuals details: https://doc.rc-visard.com https://doc.rc-cube.com Installation Debian/Ubuntu add ROS sources sudo apt-get install ros-${ROS_DISTRO}-rc-genicam-driver Source rc_genicam_driver depends rc_genicam_api dependencies installed rosdep. rosdep install --from-paths rc_genicam_driver --ignore-src rc_genicam_driver Building installing package typical ROS catkin workflow. alternative, cmake build-flow mkdir build build cmake -DCATKIN_BUILD_BINARY_PACKAGE="1" -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX="/opt/ros/$ROS_DISTRO" -DCMAKE_PREFIX_PATH="/opt/ros/$ROS_DISTRO" -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release install Alternatively, final install, package sudo dpkg install ros-melodic-rc-genicam-driver_*.deb. GenICam GenTL Transport Layer rc_genicam_driver rc_genicam_api interfacing rc_visard rc_cube GenICam/GigE Vision requires transport layer called GenTL producer (shared library suffix .cti). convenience rc_genicam_api producers Baumer common architectures. path producer set GENICAM_GENTL64_PATH environment variable GENICAM_GENTL32_PATH bit systems). set, rc_genicam_driver fall searching Baumer producer rc_genicam_api installed. producer .cti error message [ERROR] transport layers path /opt/ros/melodic/lib/rc_genicam_api case install rc_genicam_api properly set environment variable running export: GENICAM_GENTL64_PATH=/path/to/rc_genicam_api/baumer/Ubuntu-14.04/x86_64 Configuration Parameters Parameters set ROS param server run-time. device: device, Roboception rc_visard sensor rc_cube. serial number, IMPORTANT: preceed colon passing commandline, setting rosparam launch file https://github.com/ros/ros_comm/issues/1339). user defined (factory default rc_visard’s model), unique reachable sensors internal generated GenTL producer. MAC address change implementation transport layer. https://github.com/roboception/rc_genicam_api#device-id details. default parameter set works compatible device network. gev_access: gev_access mode, ‘control’ Configuration streaming possibility clients read GenICam parameters. default. ‘exclusive’ Exclusive access sensor. prevents clients read GenICam parameters. Dynamic-reconfigure Parameters parameters changed runtime dynamic reconfigure: ptp_enabled: Enable PTP slave (PrecisionTimeProtocol, IEEE1588) camera_fps: Frames published nodelet. Publishing frames slowed depending setting. Setting higher real framerate specific device effect. camera_exp_auto: parameter removed. camera_exp_control camera_exp_control: Expose control mode “Manual” setting exposure time gain camera_exp_value camera_gain_value, “Auto” auto exposure “HDR” high dynamic range mode. Default: Auto. camera_exp_auto_mode Auto-exposure mode “Normal”, “Out1High” “AdaptiveOut1”. Default: Normal. camera_exp_max: Maximum exposure time seconds exp_auto true. camera_exp_auto_average_max: auto exposure set exposure time gain factor average image intensity upper bound. parameter defines upper bound. reached saturation due reflections). camera_exp_auto_average_min: camera_exp_auto_average_max. parameter defines bound. average image intensity reduced reduce avoid saturation due reflections). camera_exp_value: Exposure time seconds exp_auto false. camera_gain_value: Gain factor decibel exp_auto false. camera_gamma: Gamma factor. Default: Auto exposure region: Definition region left image, region size image, full left image determine auto exposure. camera_exp_width: Width auto exposure region. image. camera_exp_height: Height auto exposure region. image. camera_exp_offset_x: column auto exposure region camera_exp_offset_y: row auto exposure region depth_acquisition_mode: SingleFrame Continuous. letter checked, giving sufficient. depth_quality: Quality “Medium”, “High” “Full”. letter checked, specification sufficient. quality setting effectively downscales image downscale factor Full downscale image, factor NOTE: mode requires ‘stereo_plus’ license rc_visard. High downscales factor Medium downscales factor downscales factor depth_static_scene: parameter set true scene camera static. effect quality High Full. active, input images accumulated averaged reduce noise. limits frame rate maximum timestamp disparity image image accumulation. depth_fill: Higher numbers fill gaps measurments potentielly higher errors. depth_seg: Maximum size isolated disparity regions invalidated, related full resolution. depth_smooth: Switching smoothing disparities NOTE: Smoothing requires ‘stereo_plus’ license rc_visard. depth_minconf: Minimal confidence. disparities confidence set invalid. depth_mindepth: Minimum depth meter. disparities depth set invalid. depth_maxdepth: Maximum depth meter. disparities higher depth set invalid. depth_maxdeptherr: Maximum depth error meter. disparities higher depth error set invalid. depth_exposure_adapt_timeout: Maximum time seconds wait triggering SingleFrame modes auto exposure finished adjustments. out1_mode: Mode digital GPIO values switching permanently High switching permanently ExposureActive switching exposure time image. ExposureAlternateActive switching exposure time image. changed rc_visard Control license. default out2_mode: Mode digital GPIO functionality out1_mode. default color sensors, dynamic-reconfigure parameters additionally camera_wb_auto: true, white balancing automatically. false, red blue green ratios chosen manually. camera_wb_ratio_red: Red green ratio color balancing camera_wb_auto false. camera_wb_ratio_blue: Blue green ratio color balancing camera_wb_auto false. Topics topics nodelet request data images, poses) sensor subscriber topic. Images, Stereo Data, Point Clouds /stereo/left/camera_info (sensor_msgs::CameraInfo) /stereo/right/camera_info (sensor_msgs::CameraInfo) /stereo/left/camera_param (rc_common_msgs::CameraParam) /stereo/right/camera_param (rc_common_msgs::CameraParam) /stereo/left/image_rect (sensor_msgs::Image, MONO8) /stereo/right/image_rect (sensor_msgs::Image, MONO8) /stereo/disparity (stereo_msgs::DisparityImage) /stereo/disparity_color (sensor_msgs::Image, RGB8, visually pleasing) /stereo/depth (sensor_msgs::Image, TYPE_32FC1) /stereo/confidence (sensor_msgs::Image, TYPE_32FC1, values /stereo/error_disparity (sensor_msgs::Image, TYPE_32FC1) /stereo/error_depth (sensor_msgs::Image, TYPE_32FC1) /stereo/points2 (sensor_msgs::PointCloud2) proprietary CameraParam messages image information exposure time, gain values digital inputs outputs time image capture. color sensors, topics additionally /stereo/left/image_rect_color (sensor_msgs::Image, format: RGB8) /stereo/right/image_rect_color (sensor_msgs::Image, format: RGB8) connected rc_visard Control license, topics additionally images GPIO high. topics out1_mode set special mode ExposureAlternateActive. /stereo/left/image_rect_out1_low (sensor_msgs::Image, MONO8) /stereo/left/image_rect_out1_high (sensor_msgs::Image, MONO8) /stereo/right/image_rect_out1_low (sensor_msgs::Image, MONO8) /stereo/right/image_rect_out1_high (sensor_msgs::Image, MONO8) color sensors Control license, topics additionally /stereo/left/image_rect_color_out1_low (sensor_msgs::Image, format: RGB8) /stereo/left/image_rect_color_out1_high (sensor_msgs::Image, format: RGB8) /stereo/right/image_rect_color_out1_low (sensor_msgs::Image, format: RGB8) /stereo/right/image_rect_color_out1_high (sensor_msgs::Image, format: RGB8) Running multiple rc_visard’s ros environment operating multiple rc_visard’s ros environment, ros node started separate namespaces, my_visard. result, frame_ids ros messages prefixed, my_visard_world my_visard_camera. name: rc_hand_eye_calibration_client
description: The rc_hand_eye_calibration_client package
readme: ROS Hand-eye calibration client Roboception’s rc_visard package developed anymore. client rc_reason_clients_ros repository. node ROS service calls topics calibrate rc_visard robot (aka hand-eye calibration). pre-existing calibration /tf_static. default behavior request existing calibration rc_visard startup, broadcast (latched) /tf_static broadcast advertised ROS services calibrate get_calibration called. calibration routine consists steps: Setting calibration parameters, grid size mounting, dynamic reconfigure. user-defined number robot calibration poses repeat Move robot pose (calibration grid visible rc_visard’s view). Send robot pose rc_visard (set_pose) Trigger calibration tranformation calculated (calibrate). calibration transform calculated tested, saved rc_visard (save_calibration). detailed instructions calibration routine consult rc_visard manual: https://doc.rc-visard.com. Installation Debian/Ubuntu add ROS sources sudo apt-get install ros-${ROS_DISTRO}-rc-hand-eye-calibration-client Source package relies git submodules cpr library initialized building source. git submodule update --init --recursive Configuration Parameters device: device, Roboception rc_visard sensor. serial number, IMPORTANT: preceed colon passing commandline setting rosparam https://github.com/ros/ros_comm/issues/1339). neccessary string launch file. user defined (factory default rc_visard’s model), unique reachable sensors. host: device address hostname rc_visard calibrated. rc_visard_frame_id: frame rc_visard calibrating. Default: “camera” end_effector_frame_id: frame calibrated robot_mounted rc_visard. Default: “end_effector”. base_frame_id: frame calibrated statically (externally) mounted rc_visard (robot_mounted false). Default: “base_link” calibration_request_period: Decimal number, controls requesting calibration rc_visard. Default: positive: Interval seconds automated requests latest calibration. request startup (default). negative, calibration requested. case, results broadcast services calibrate get_calibration node called. calibration_publication_period: Decimal number, controls broadcasting calibration /tf_static. Default: positive: Interval seconds. negative calibration broadcast /tf_static changed manual periodic calibration request (default). Dynamic reconfigure parameters grid_width: width calibration pattern meters. grid_height: height calibration pattern meters. robot_mounted: camera mounted robot tcp_rotation_axis:, TCP rotation axis DOF robot calibration general robot) tcp_offset: Offset TCP DOF robot calibration Services services offered follow calibration routine: reset_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Trigger]: Deletes previously poses images. saved calibration result reloaded. service (re-)start hand-eye calibration scratch. set_pose [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/SetCalibrationPose]: robot pose calibration pose hand-eye calibration routine. calibrate [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Calibration]: Calculates returns hand-eye calibration transformation robot poses configured set_pose service. Broadcasts result /tf_static. get_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Calibration]: Returns existing hand-eye calibration transformation. Broadcasts result /tf_static. save_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Trigger]: Persistently saves result hand-eye calibration rc_visard overwrites existing stored result retrieved time get_calibration service. remove_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Trigger]: Removes stored hand-eye calibration rc_visard. call get_calibration service reports hand-eye calibration Periodic broadcasting stopped. set_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/SetCalibration]: Sets hand-eye calibration transformation. calibration transformation expected format returned calibrate get_calibration calls. save_calibration called calibration transformation persistent. Launching command line parameters: rosrun rc_hand_eye_calibration_client rc_hand_eye_calibration_client_node _device:=:<serial_number>

name: realtime_tools
description:
Contains a set of tools that can be used from a hard
    realtime thread, without breaking the realtime behavior.  The
    tools currently only provides the realtime publisher, which makes
    it possible to publish messages to a ROS topic from a realtime
    thread. We plan to add a basic implementation of a realtime
    buffer, to make it possible to get data from a (non-realtime)
    topic callback into the realtime loop. Once the lockfree buffer is
    created, the realtime publisher will start using it, which will
    result in major API changes for the realtime publisher (removal of
    all lock methods).
readme: realtime_tools ros_control realtime_tools documentation ros.org Build Status Publication find work give credits authors citing: Chitta, Marder-Eppstein, Meeussen, Pradeep, Rodríguez Tsouroukdissian, Bohren, Coleman, Magyar, Raiola, Lüdtke Fernandez Perdomo “ros_control: generic simple control framework ROS”, Journal Open Source Software, (PDF) @article{ros_control, author {Chitta, Sachin Marder-Eppstein, Eitan Meeussen, Wim Pradeep, Vijay Rodr{\'i}guez Tsouroukdissian, Adolfo Bohren, Jonathan Coleman, David Magyar, Bence Raiola, Gennaro L{\"u}dtke, Mathias Fern{\'a}ndez Perdomo, Enrique}, title {ros\_control: generic simple control framework ROS}, journal Journal Open Source Software}, year doi {10.21105/joss.00456}, URL {http://www.theoj.org/joss-papers/joss.00456/10.21105.joss.00456.pdf}

name: resource_retriever
description:
This package retrieves data from url-format files such as http://,
   ftp://, package:// file://, etc., and loads the data into memory.
   The package:// url for ros packages is translated into a local
   file:// url.  The resourse retriever was initially designed to load
   mesh files into memory, but it can be used for any type of
   data. The resource retriever is based on the the libcurl library.

name: locomotor
description: Locomotor is an extensible path planning coordination engine that replaces move_base. The goal is to provide a mechanism for controlling what happens when the global and local planners succeed and fail. It leverages ROS callback queues to coordinate multiple threads.

name: roboticsgroup_upatras_gazebo_plugins

readme: roboticsgroup_upatras_gazebo_plugins Collection small gazebo plugins MimicJointPlugin simple (Model) plugin Gazebo order add Gazebo mimic joint functionality exists URDF (ROS). Inspired code Goncalo Cabrita. XML Parameters joint (Required) string joint mimic-ed. mimicJoint (Required) string mimic joint. multiplier double multiplier parameter mimic joint. Defaults offset double offset parameter mimic joint. Defaults maxEffort double max effort mimic joint generate. Defaults effort limit sdf model. sensitiveness double sensitiveness mimic joint. Defaults basically threshold difference angles (joint’s mimic’s) applying “mimicness”. robotNamespace string namespace robot Defaults hasPID Determines joint PID order controlled PID position/effort controller. <hasPID/> means mimic joint controlled PID. Omit mimic joint controlled setAngle. Optionally, set pid namespace. DisableLinkPlugin simple (Model) plugin Gazebo disable link Gazebo’s physics engine. XML Parameters link (Required) string link disabled. valid sdf urdf) link. Hoping add plugins…. Usage Standard Gazebo plugin import xacro/urdf. libroboticsgroup_upatras_gazebo_ prefix. import MimicJointPlugin: libroboticsgroup_upatras_gazebo_mimic_joint_plugin.so Notes issue I’ll add functionality/plugin. name: topic_tools
description:
Tools for directing, throttling, selecting, and otherwise messing with
    ROS topics at a meta level. None of the programs in this package actually
    know about the topics whose streams they are altering; instead, these
    tools deal with messages as generic binary blobs. This means they can be
    applied to any ROS topic.

authors: Morgan Quigley, Brian Gerkey, Dirk Thomas

name: lockfree
description:
The lockfree package contains lock-free data structures for use in multithreaded programming.  These
     kinds of data structures are generally not as easy to use as single-threaded equivalents, and are not
     always faster.  If you don't know you need to use one, try another structure with a lock around it
     first.

name: rosatomic

     conditionally use those instead.

name: rt_usb_9axisimu_driver
description: The rt_usb_9axisimu_driver package
readme: English rt_usb_9axisimu_driver rt_usb_9axisimu_driverは株式会社アールティが販売しているUSB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュール用のROSパッケージです。 License: 3-Clause BSD License Source: https://github.com/rt-net/rt_usb_9axisimu_driver.git (branch: master) 現在、以下のROSのディストリビューションに対応しております。 Kinetic (kinetic-devel) Melodic (melodic-devel) Noetic (noetic-devel) Dashing (dashing-devel) Foxy (foxy-devel) industrial_ci source build amd64 binary arm64 binary main develop(master) ROS develop(ros2-devel) Xenial Kinetic(kinetic-devel) Bionic Melodic(melodic-devel) Focal Noetic(noetic-devel) Bionic Dashing(dashing-devel) Focal Foxy(foxy-devel) このパッケージは、USB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールをROSから使用するためのドライバを提供するものです。 USB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールは、センサとしてInvenSense社のMPU9250を使用しております。 このセンサの磁気センサの座標系はNED座標系(x-north, y-east, z-down)ですが、 モジュール内のマイコン(LPC1343)においてENU座標系(x-east, y-north, z-up)に変換され、 これはROSで使われる座標系のルールにも適合しています。詳しくは、REP-0103をご覧ください。 USB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールはオープンハード・オープンソースのため、モジュール内のマイコンのファームウェアの変更が可能です。 このROSパッケージはデフォルトのファームウェアにのみ対応しております。ファームウェアを変更された場合、正常な動作ができなくなる恐れがございますので、ご了承ください。 ver2.0でのご利用について 2018年10月現在、販売されているUSB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールはver2.0となります。 このバージョンのデフォルトのファームウェアには、ASCII出力とBinary出力の２つのデータ出力形式があります。 センサ出荷時点ではASCII出力に設定されています。出力形式の切り替え方法は、以下のリポジトリにあるマニュアルをご参照ください。 https://github.com/rt-net/RT-USB-9AXIS-00 ROS 2のパッケージについてはros2-develのREADMEをご覧ください。 ROS Kineticの場合 sudo apt install ros-kinetic-rt-usb-9axisimu-driver ROS Melodicの場合 sudo apt install ros-melodic-rt-usb-9axisimu-driver catkinワークスペースを~/catkin_wsとすると、以下のような手順になります。 ~/catkin_ws/src git clone $ROS_DISTRO-devel https://github.com/rt-net/rt_usb_9axisimu_driver.git ~/catkin_ws catkin build 2のようにインストールを行った場合、ドライバを起動しようとする端末(Terminal)で、 source ~/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash roscore rosrun rt_usb_9axisimu_driver rt_usb_9axisimu_driver roslaunch rt_usb_9axisimu_driver rt_usb_9axisimu_driver.launch [ERROR] Error opening sensor device, re-check devices. sudo chmod /dev/ttyACM0 rt_usb_9axisimu_driver rt_usb_9axisimu_driverはUSB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールの出力を受信し、角速度と並進加速度・磁束密度をパブリッシュします。 /imu/data_raw(sensor_msgs/Imu) /imu/mag(sensor_msgs/MagneticField) /imu/temperature(std_msgs/Float64) ~frame_id (string, default: imu_link) IMUデータのヘッダーにセットされるフレーム名 ~port (string, default: /dev/ttyACM0) ~linear_acceleration_stddev (double, default: ~angular_velocity_stddev (double, default: 角速度の共分散行列の対角成分の平方根(rad/s) ~magnetic_field_stddev (double, default:

Simple usecase #+begin_src python sba_cpp import SPA2d, Node2d import numpy SPA2d() Test prep start yaw start yaw start yaw yaw, node_id s.add_node(0, s.add_node(1.1, s.add_node(0.1, inverse covariance precision_matrix np.identity(3) from_node_id, to_node_id, xdiff, ydiff, yawdiff, precision matrix s.add_constraint(0, precision_matrix.tolist()) s.add_constraint(1, precision_matrix.tolist()) s.add_constraint(2, precision_matrix.tolist()) parameters max iterations, diagonal augmentation CSparse (set True fast version) initial tolerance max iterations step s.compute(100, True, values defaults original code work s.nodes: print(n.x, n.yaw) #+end_src Building ROS Missing deps: openblas-dev, libsuitesparse-dev Change tag pybind11 python versions support ros1 work latest pybind

name: sbg_driver
description: ROS driver package for communication with the SBG navigation systems.
readme: sbg_driver ROS driver package SBG Systems IMU, AHRS INS. driver package sbgECom binary protocol read data configure SBG Systems devices. Initial work ENSTA Bretagne. Author: SBG Systems Maintainer: SBG Systems Contact: support@sbg-systems.com Features driver supports features: Configure ELLIPSE products yaml files note Parse IMU/AHRS/INS/GNSS sbgECom protocol Publish standard ROS messages detailed specific SBG Systems topics Subscribe RTCM data support DGPS/RTK mode centimeters-level accuracy Calibrate magnetic field on-board ELLIPSE algorithms [!NOTE] ELLIPSE devices configured ROS driver. High Performance INS EKINOX, APOGEE QUANTA, sbgInsRestApi Installation Installation Packages User install sbg_ros_driver standard ROS installation system. Noetic sudo apt-get install ros-noetic-sbg-driver Building sources Dependencies Robot Operating System (ROS) sbgECom Library (embeds v1.11.920-stable compatible ELLIPSE firmware Boost Library Building Clone repository Release version) catkin_ws/src git clone master https://github.com/SBG-Systems/sbg_ros_driver.git Install dependencies rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src Build normal ROS catkin build system source /opt/ros/${ROS_DISTRO}/setup.bash catkin_make Usage Source generated setup environment source devel/setup.bash run default Ros node default configuration roslaunch sbg_driver sbg_device.launch run magnetic calibration node roslaunch sbg_driver sbg_device_mag_calibration.launch Config files Default config files configuration file defined structure. sbg_device_uart_default.yaml config file default UART connection device. configure device ROS node, previously configured (manually ROS node). defines outputs device: /sbg/imu_data, /sbg/ekf_quat ROS standard outputs /imu/data, /imu/velocity, /imu/temp /sbg/status, /sbg/utc_time /imu/utc_ref sbg_device_udp_default.yaml config file default Udp connection device. configure device ROS node, previously configured (manually ROS node). defines outputs device: /sbg/imu_data, /sbg/ekf_quat ROS standard outputs /imu/data, /imu/velocity, /imu/temp /sbg/status, /sbg/utc_time /imu/utc_ref config files ellipse_A_default.yaml Default config file Ellipse-A. ellipse_E_default.yaml Default config file Ellipse-E external NMEA GNSS. ellipse_N_default.yaml Default config file Ellipse-N internal GNSS. ellipse_D_default.yaml Default config file Ellipse-D internal GNSS. Launch files Default launch files sbg_device_launch.py Launch sbg_device node handle received data, load sbg_device_uart_default.yaml configuration. sbg_device_mag_calibration_launch.py Launch sbg_device_mag node calibrate magnetometers, load ellipse_E_default.yaml configuration. Nodes sbg_device node sbg_device node handles communication connected device, publishes SBG output ROS environment subscribes topics RTCM data streams. Published Topics SBG Systems specific topics SBG Systems defined proprietary ROS messages report detailed information AHRS/INS. messages match sbgECom logs output device. /sbg/status sbg_driver/SbgStatus information general status (Communication, Aiding, /sbg/utc_time sbg_driver/SbgUtcTime UTC time reference. /sbg/imu_data sbg_driver/SbgImuData IMU status, sensors values. /sbg/ekf_euler sbg_driver/SbgEkfEuler Computed orientation Euler angles. /sbg/ekf_quat sbg_driver/SbgEkfQuat Computed orientation Quaternion. /sbg/ekf_nav sbg_driver/SbgEkfNav Computed navigation data. /sbg/mag sbg_driver/SbgMag Calibrated magnetic field measurement. /sbg/mag_calib sbg_driver/SbgMagCalib Magnetometer calibration data. /sbg/ship_motion sbg_driver/SbgShipMotion Heave, surge sway data. /sbg/gps_vel sbg_driver/SbgGpsVel GPS velocities GPS receiver. /sbg/gps_pos sbg_driver/SbgGpsPos GPS positions GPS receiver. /sbg/gps_hdt sbg_driver/SbgGpsHdt GPS true heading dual antenna system. /sbg/gps_raw sbg_driver/SbgGpsRaw GPS raw data post processing. /sbg/odo_vel sbg_driver/SbgOdoVel Odometer velocity. /sbg/event[ABCDE] sbg_driver/SbgEvent Event sync pin. /sbg/air_data sbg_driver/SbgAirData Air data. ROS standard topics [!NOTE] update driver configuration enable standard ROS messages publication output.ros_standard. driver publish standard ROS messages driver setup ENU frame convention output.use_enu. order define ROS standard topics, requires SBG messages, merged. ROS standard, activate needed SBG outputs. /imu/data sensor_msgs/Imu IMU data. Requires /sbg/imu_data /sbg/ekf_quat. /imu/temp sensor_msgs/Temperature IMU temperature data. Requires /sbg/imu_data. /imu/velocity geometry_msgs/TwistStamped IMU velocity data. Requires /sbg/imu_data. /imu/mag sensor_msgs/MagneticField IMU magnetic field. Requires /sbg/mag. /imu/pres sensor_msgs/FluidPressure IMU pressure data. Requires /sbg/air_data. /imu/pos_ecef geometry_msgs/PointStamped Earth-Centered Earth-Fixed position. Requires /sbg/ekf_nav. /imu/utc_ref sensor_msgs/TimeReference UTC time reference. Requires /sbg/utc_time. /imu/nav_sat_fix sensor_msgs/NavSatFix Navigation satellite fix Global Navigation Satellite System. Requires /sbg/gps_pos. /imu/odometry nav_msgs/Odometry UTM projected position relative valid INS position. Requires /sbg/imu_data /sbg/ekv_nav /sbg/ekf_euler /sbg/ekf_quat. Disabled default, set odometry.enable configuration file. NMEA topics driver publish NMEA GGA messages internal GNSS receiver. party NTRIP client modules support VRS networks providers. Disabled default, set nmea.publish true .yaml config file feature. /ntrip_client/nmea nmea_msgs/Sentence Data /sbg/gps_pos serialized NMEA GGA format. Requires /sbg/gps_pos. Namespace ntrip_client topic_name nmea customized .yaml config files. Subscribed Topics RTCM topics sbg_device node subscribe RTCM topics published party ROS modules. Incoming RTCM data forwarded INS internal GNSS receiver enable DGPS/RTK solutions. Disabled default, set rtcm.subscribe true .yaml config file feature. /ntrip_client/rtcm rtcm_msgs/Message RTCM data /ntrip_client/rtcm forwarded internal INS GNSS receiver. Namespace ntrip_client topic_name rtcm customized .yaml config files. sbg_device_mag node sbg_device_mag node execute board in-situ magnetic field calibration. planning magnetic based heading, mandatory perform magnetic field calibration clean magnetic environnement. ELLIPSE products support magnetic based heading feature on-board magnetic field calibration process. Services /sbg/mag_calibration std_srvs/Trigger Service start/stop magnetic calibration. /sbg/mag_calibration_save std_srvs/Trigger Service save FLASH memory latest computed magnetic field calibration. HowTo Configure SBG device SBG Ros driver user configure device starting data parsing. set parameter config file. Configuration device ROS. confWithRos: true modify desired parameters config file, Firmware Reference Manual, features configurable, parameter values Configure RTK/DGPS sbg_device node subscribe rtcm_msgs/Message topics differential corrections INS internal GNSS receiver. RTCM data stream serial/ethernet interface ROS communicate INS. enables simple efficient RTK operations requiring additional hardware wiring. combined party NTRIP client, offers turnkey solution access local VRS providers centimeter-level accuracy solutions. driver device properly setup: Configure INS accept RTCM corrections interface ROS driver: ELLIPSE, simply sbgCenter Assignment panel, RTCM set Port High Performance INS, configuration web interface sbgInsRestApi. Install configure party node broadcast RTCM corrections NTRIP client Update node config yaml file set rtcm.subscribe nmea.publish true node broadcast RTCM topics, update config yaml file update topics namespaces. Calibrate magnetometers ELLIPSE products magnetometers determine heading. calibration required compensate soft hard iron distortions due vehicle product installed magnetic calibration procedure held clean magnetic environnement buildings). read information magnetic field calibration procedure SBG Systems Support Center. ROS driver dedicated node easily ELLIPSE board magnetic field calibration algorithms. ELLIPSE offers magnetic field calibration mode. selected desired magnetic field calibration mode (calibration.mode configuration yaml file). Start magnetic calibration session ready map magnetic field: roslaunch sbg_driver sbg_device_mag_calibration.launch rosservice call /sbg/mag_calibration success: True message: “Magnetometer calibration process started.” Rotate unit map surrounding magnetic field (ideally, perform axis pointing downward). covered orientations, compute magnetic field calibration: rosservice call /sbg/mag_calibration success: True message: “Magnetometer calibration finished. output console calibration information.” happy results (Quality, Confidence), apply save magnetic calibration parameters. continue rotate product perform computation repeat step rosservice call /sbg/mag_calibration_save success: True message: “Magnetometer calibration uploaded device.” Reset/Power Cycle device accurate magnetic based heading. Enable communication SBG device able communicate device, user part dialout group. added, restart machine save apply sudo adduser $USER dialout Create udev rules Udev rules defined communication port, order avoid modifying port configuration changed. name: schunk_simulated_tactile_sensors
description:
This package provides simulated tactile sensors for the Schunk Dextrous
     Hand (SDH) which is mounted on the Care-O-bot arm. The node subscribes to
     the Gazebo bumper topics of the SDH. It transforms the Gazebo feedback to
     the "tactile_data" topic to provide the same tactile sensor interface as
     the schunk_sdh package.

     The following parameters can be set:
     * cells_x: The number of patches on the tactile sensor in the direction
                perpendicular to the finger. Defaults to 6.
     * cells_y: The number of patches on the tactile sensor along the direction
                of the finger. Defaults to 14.
     * output_range: The maximum output value of one patch. Defaults to 3500.
     * sensitivity: The change of output in one patch per Newton. Defaults to
                    350. The sensitivity can be approximated by the following
                    formula: S = output_range / (measurement_range * cell_area)
                    - The measurement range of the tactile pads is 250 kPa (from
                      the data sheet).
                    - The output range can be determined by experiment from the
                      real SDH. It is about 3500.
                    - The cell area is the size of one patch. Length and width
                      of the area are determined by dividing the length/width
                      of the collision surface by the number of cells in the
                      respective direction.
                      Important: In most cases this is NOT the cell area that is
                                 given in the data sheet!
     * filter_length: The length of the moving average filter which smoothes
                      the values from simulation. Defaults to 10.

     The node subscribes to the following topics to receive data from the
     simulation:
     * thumb_2/state
     * thumb_3/state
     * finger_12/state
     * finger_13/state
     * finger_22/state
     * finger_23/state

     The node publishes the processed data on the following topic:
     * tactile_data

     The simulated bumper must obtain the collision data in the link that the
     sensor is attached to. This is achieved by setting the "frameName" property
     in the gazebo_ros_bumper controller.

readme: seed_smartactuator_sdk ROS上のパッケージから本ライブラリをインクルードし、SEED-Driverへコマンドを送信するための一連サンプルを示す。 なお、PCからSEED-Driverへ直接指令を行う際は通信モジュール(CM4U/CMSU)が必要となる。 多軸ロボットへ指令する際は、aero3_command.hを用いる。詳細は seed_r7_ros_controllerを参考にされたい #include <ros/ros.h> #include "seed_smartactuator_sdk/seed3_command.h" int main(int argc, char **argv) ros::init(argc,argv,"cpp_sample_node"); ros::NodeHandle seed::controller::SeedCommand seed; seed.openPort("/dev/ttyACM0",115200); seed.openCom(); int std::array<int,3> motor_state; seed.runScript(id,1); seed.waitForScriptEnd(1); (ros::ok()) motor_state seed.getPosition(id); if(motor_state[0]) std::cout motor_state[2] std::endl; return Python #!/usr/bin/env python import rospy seed_smartactuator_sdk.seed3_command import SeedCommand main "__main__": rospy.init_node('python_sample_node') seed=SeedCommand() seed.open_port("/dev/ttyACM0",115200) seed.open_com() motor_state seed.run_script(id,1) seed.wait_for_script_end(1) rospy.is_shutdown(): motor_state seed.get_position(id) if(motor_state[0]): print motor_state[2]

name: gmapping

Shadow hand arm Simulation start simulation hand arm, run: roslaunch sr_robot_launch sr_right_ur10arm_hand.launch left hand arm roslaunch sr_robot_launch sr_left_ur10arm_hand.launch Real Robots start real robots, roslaunch sr_robot_launch sr_right_ur10arm_hand.launch sim:=false hand_serial:=1178 left hand arm roslaunch sr_robot_launch sr_left_ur10arm_hand.launch sim:=false hand_serial:=1178 find hand serial launch command hand_serial argument check program output. read mapping /hand/mapping/1178 case serial number hand. change hand mapping, set mapping_path argument. adding: mapping_path:=`rospack find sr_edc_launch`/mappings/default_mappings/rh_E_v3.yaml change ethernet port hand, add eth_port argument, eth_port:=eth6 Real Robots, normal limited) joint range default URDF arm limited range joints, helps moveit find planning solution. restricts robot movements, user start robots full joint range. roslaunch sr_robot_launch sr_right_ur10arm_hand.launch sim:=false robot_description:=`rospack find sr_multi_description`/urdf/right_srhand_ur10.urdf.xacro hand_serial:=1178 left hand arm roslaunch sr_robot_launch sr_left_ur10arm_hand.launch sim:=false robot_description:=`rospack find sr_multi_description`/urdf/left_srhand_ur10.urdf.xacro hand_serial:=1178 Hand tactile sensors hand biotacs sensors, simply append _biotacs robot_description:= robot_config_file:= robot_description:=`rospack find sr_multi_description`/urdf/right_srhand_ur10_joint_limited_biotacs.urdf.xacro robot_config_file:=`rospack find sr_multi_moveit_config`/config/robot_configs/right_sh_ur10_biotac.yaml Bimanual system Simulation start simulation bimanual system, run: roslaunch sr_robot_launch sr_bimanual.launch use_moveit:=true Real Robots start real robots, roslaunch sr_robot_launch sr_bimanual.launch sim:=false rh_serial:=1290 lh_serial:=1338

name: teb_local_planner
description:
The teb_local_planner package implements a plugin
    to the base_local_planner of the 2D navigation stack.
    The underlying method called Timed Elastic Band locally optimizes
    the robot's trajectory with respect to trajectory execution time,
    separation from obstacles and compliance with kinodynamic constraints at runtime.
