name: timed_roslaunch
description: Script to delay the launch of a roslaunch file
readme: timed_roslaunch script delay launch roslaunch file. Install apt sudo apt install ros-kinetic-timed-roslaunch source /opt/ros/kinetic/setup.bash source ~/<catkin_ws>/src git clone https://github.com/MoriKen254/timed_roslaunch.git ~/<catkin_ws> catkin_make source ~/<catkin_ws>/devel/setup.bash Usage script delay launch roslaunch file. file executable (chmod timed_roslaunch.sh) Run command line rosrun timed_roslaunch timed_roslaunch.sh [number seconds delay] [rospkg] [roslaunch file] [arguments (optional)] roslaunch timed_roslaunch timed_roslaunch.launch time:=[number delay] pkg:=[rospkg] file:=[roslaunch file] value:=[arguments (optional)] rosrun timed_roslaunch timed_roslaunch.sh turtlebot_navigation amcl_demo.launch initial_pose_x:=17.0 initial_pose_y:=17.0" Run roslaunch file <launch> <include file="$(find timed_roslaunch)/launch/timed_roslaunch.launch"> <arg name="time" <arg name="pkg" value="turtlebot_navigation" <arg name="file" value="amcl_demo.launch" <arg name="value" value="initial_pose_x:=17.0 initial_pose_y:=17.0" <arg name="node_name" value="timed_roslaunch" optional argument </include> </launch> <launch> <node pkg="timed_roslaunch" type="timed_roslaunch.sh" args="2 turtlebot_navigation amcl_demo.launch initial_pose_x:=17.0 initial_pose_y:=17.0"  name="timed_roslaunch" output="screen" </launch> LICENSE software released BSD 3-Clause License, LICENSE.txt.

name: towr
description:
A light-weight, Eigen-based
    C++ library for trajectory optimization for legged robots. 
    This library provides implementations for variables, costs and constraints
    that can be used to represent a legged locomotion problem. 
    The resulting Nonlinear Programming Problem (NLP) can then be solved with
    off-the-shelf solvers, e.g. Ipopt using the generic optimizer interface

name: trac_ik
description:
The ROS packages in this repository were created to provide an improved
    alternative Inverse Kinematics solver to the popular inverse Jacobian
    methods in KDL.  TRAC-IK handles joint-limited chains better than KDL
    without increasing solve time.

name: trac_ik_lib
description:
TRAC-IK is a faster, significantly more reliable drop-in replacement for
    KDL's pseudoinverse Jacobian solver.

    The TRAC-IK library has a very similar API to KDL's IK solver calls,
    except that the user passes a maximum time instead of a maximum number of
    search iterations.  Additionally, TRAC-IK allows for error tolerances to
    be set independently for each Cartesian dimension (x,y,z,roll,pitch.yaw).
readme: TRAC-IK kinematics solver built trac_ik_lib library tested ROS Indigo Catkin). headers shared objects package linked user programs. package part ROS Noetic binaries: sudo apt-get install ros-noetic-trac-ik package part ROS Indigo/Jade binaries: sudo apt-get install ros-jade-trac-ik requires Ubuntu packages NLOpt Libraries installed ros-indigo-nlopt packages proper headers). running sudo apt-get install libnlopt-dev trusty standard Ubuntu distros. Alternatively, run ```rosdep update rosdep install trac_ik_lib KDL KDL::ChainFkSolverPos_recursive fk_solver(chain); KDL::ChainIkSolverVel_pinv vik_solver(chain); KDL::ChainJntToJacSolver jac_solver(chain); KDL::ChainIkSolverPos_NR_JL ik_solver(KDL::Chain chain, KDL::JntArray lower_joint_limits, KDL::JntArray upper_joint_limits, fk_solver, vik_solver, int num_iterations, double error); int ik_solver.CartToJnt(KDL::JntArray joint_seed, KDL::Frame desired_end_effector_pose, KDL::JntArray& return_joints); NOTE: CartToJnt succeeded NOTE: timeout seconds iterations set called loop timer. NOTE: error acceptable purposes TRAC-IK: #include <trac_ik/trac_ik.hpp> TRAC_IK::TRAC_IK ik_solver(KDL::Chain chain, KDL::JntArray lower_joint_limits, KDL::JntArray upper_joint_limits, double timeout_in_secs=0.005, double error=1e-5, TRAC_IK::SolveType type=TRAC_IK::Speed); TRAC_IK::TRAC_IK ik_solver(string base_link, string tip_link, string URDF_param="/robot_description", double timeout_in_secs=0.005, double error=1e-5, TRAC_IK::SolveType type=TRAC_IK::Speed); NOTE: arguments constructors optional. type Speed: returns quickly solution Distance: runs full timeout_in_secs, returns solution minimizes SSE seed Manip1: runs full timeout, returns solution maximizes sqrt(det(J*J^T)) product singular values Jacobian) Manip2: runs full timeout, returns solution minimizes ratio min max singular values Jacobian. int ik_solver.CartToJnt(KDL::JntArray joint_seed, KDL::Frame desired_end_effector_pose, KDL::JntArray& return_joints, KDL::Twist tolerances); NOTE: CartToJnt succeeded NOTE: tolerances effector pose optional, default ABS() values set tolerances -tol..0..+tol Cartesian dimensions effector pose.

description:
The trac_ik_python package contains a python wrapper using SWIG

readme: trac_ik_python Python wrapper TRAC library SWIG generate bindings. send questions Sam Pfeiffer usage Upload robot param server: roslaunch pr2_description upload_pr2.launch IK’s: #!/usr/bin/env python trac_ik_python.trac_ik import ik_solver IK("torso_lift_link", "r_wrist_roll_link") seed_state ik_solver.number_of_joints ik_solver.get_ik(seed_state, Returns: play bounds call: #!/usr/bin/env python trac_ik_python.trac_ik import ik_solver IK("torso_lift_link", "r_wrist_roll_link") seed_state ik_solver.number_of_joints ik_solver.get_ik(seed_state, bounds Rotation bounds Returns: coordinate frame poses base frame, check links/joints #!/usr/bin/env python trac_ik_python.trac_ik import ik_solver IK("torso_lift_link", "r_wrist_roll_link") ik_solver.base_link 'torso_lift_link' ik_solver.tip_link 'r_wrist_roll_link' ik_solver.joint_names ('r_shoulder_pan_joint', 'r_shoulder_lift_joint', 'r_upper_arm_roll_joint', 'r_elbow_flex_joint', 'r_forearm_roll_joint', 'r_wrist_flex_joint', 'r_wrist_roll_joint') ik_solver.link_names ('r_shoulder_pan_link', 'r_shoulder_lift_link', 'r_upper_arm_roll_link', 'r_upper_arm_link', 'r_elbow_flex_link', 'r_forearm_roll_link', 'r_forearm_link', 'r_wrist_flex_link', 'r_wrist_roll_link') initialize string URDF default takes /robot_description parameter param server): #!/usr/bin/env python trac_ik_python.trac_ik import URDF urdf_str rospy.get_param('/robot_description') ik_solver IK("torso_lift_link", "r_wrist_roll_link", urdf_string=urdf_str) check modify joint limits: #!/usr/bin/env python trac_ik_python.trac_ik import ik_solver IK("torso_lift_link", "r_wrist_roll_link") lower_bound, upper_bound ik_solver.get_joint_limits() lower_bound: upper_bound: ik_solver.set_joint_limits([0.0]* ik_solver.number_of_joints, upper_bound) Extra notes Python wrapper SWIG extended languages. You’ll extra output instantiating class WARN] root link base_footprint inertia URDF, KDL support root link inertia. workaround, add extra dummy link URDF. finishing program you’ll error apparently): terminate called throwing instance 'boost::exception_detail::clone_impl<boost::exception_detail::error_info_injector<boost::lock_error> boost: mutex lock failed pthread_mutex_lock: Invalid argument Aborted (core dumped) final note didn’t simplify letting Pose/PoseStamped messages input, transforming coordinate frames, adding kinematics, simple.

name: turtlebot3_example
description: This package provides four TurtleBot3 basic example include move using interactive marker, move and stop using LDS, move to goal position, move to custom routes. The interactions node is that you can control the TurtleBot3 front and back side or rotate to goal position. The obstacle node is that when the robot meets an obstacle, it stops. The patrol node is that TurtleBot3 move to custom route. There are 3 route(square, triangle, circle) in this package. You can add your route and move the TurtleBot3. The pointop node is that you can insert goal position include distance x-axis, y-axis and angluar z-axis.

The tuw_checkerboard package is designed to detect one 
    checkerboard and to estimate the pose of the checkerboard relative to the camera.
    The detection itself is based on the opencv functions for checkerboards.

name: tuw_ellipses
description:
The tuw_ellipses package contains a computer vision library which is able to detect ellipses within images.  
    The package is able to estimate the pose of the circle related to the ellipse the circle diameter as well as the camera parameter are known.
    A dynamic reconfigure interface allows the user to tune the parameter of the system to ones needs.
    But be aware that the pose of a projected circle within a image (ellipse) has two solutions and only one is published as TF.

name: tuw_multi_robot
description: This repository includes ros packages to plan routes for multiple robots on a search graph. It creates a search graph out of a pixel map and tries to find a path for multiple robots using an extended approach for prioritized planning. The inputs are the tuw_multi_robot_msgs/RobotInfo messages which include the robots pose, the map and the desired goal poses. The output are multiple synchronized routes given to the individual robots.
maintainers: Benjamin Binder, Markus Bader, Florian Beck

name: ubiquity_motor
description: Provides a ROS interface to Ubiquity Robotics Magni motor controllers
readme: ubiquity_motor Introduction Package ROS interface motors UbiquityRobotics robots. communicates motor controller serial port. Installation package installed binaries ARM architectures. package installed sudo apt-get install ros-kinetic-ubiquity-motor Configuration launch files Magni robot package magni_robot. ROS API Subscribed topics cmd_vel geometry_msgs/Twist command input. Published topics odom nav_msgs/Odometry Odometry computed motor controller messages. tf/tfMessage transform odom base_link battery_state sensor_msgs/BatteryState Charge state robot’s batteries. publish_cmd geometry_msgs/TwistStamped cmd_vel limits applied. publish_cmd paramater set. Parameters Comms paramaters serial_port (string, default: “/dev/ttyS0”) device communicate motor controller hardware. baud_rate (int, default: Baud rate serial communication motor controller hardware. Firmware parameters pid_proportional (int, default: paramater motor controller’s PID controller. pid_integral (int, default: paramater motor controller’s PID controller. pid_derivative (int, default: paramater motor controller’s PID controller. pid_denominator (int, default: Divisor PID paramaters. pid_moving_buffer_size (int, default: Size moving buffer control loop. deadman_timer (int, default: message received interval MCU clock ticks), motor controller assume error condition stop. battery_voltage_multiplier (float, default: calculate battery voltage motor controller messages. battery_voltage_offset (float, default: calculate battery voltage motor controller messages. Node parameters controller_loop_rate (double, default: Rate send commands motor controller hardware. diff_drive_controller paramaters left_wheel (string string[…]) Left wheel joint list joint names. right_wheel (string string[…]) wheel joint list joint names. pose_covariance_diagonal (double[6]) Diagonal covariance matrix odometry pose publishing. twist_covariance_diagonal (double[6]) Diagonal covariance matrix odometry twist publishing. publish_rate (double, default: Frequency odometry published. odom. wheel_separation_multiplier (double, default: Multiplier applied wheel separation parameter. account difference robot model real robot. wheel_radius_multiplier (double, default: Multiplier applied wheel radius parameter. account difference robot model real robot. cmd_vel_timeout (double, default: Allowed period seconds) allowed successive velocity commands. delay, speed command wheels. base_frame_id (string, default: base_link) Base frame_id, fill child_frame_id Odometry messages linear/x/has_velocity_limits (bool, default: false) controller limit linear speed. linear/x/max_velocity (double) Maximum linear velocity linear/x/min_velocity (double) Minimum linear velocity Setting disable motion. unspecified, -max_velocity linear/x/has_acceleration_limits (bool, default: false) controller limit linear acceleration. linear/x/max_acceleration (double) Maximum linear acceleration linear/x/min_acceleration (double) Minimum linear acceleration unspecified, -max_acceleration linear/x/has_jerk_limits (bool, default: false) controller limit linear jerk. linear/x/max_jerk (double) Maximum linear jerk angular/z/has_velocity_limits (bool, default: false) controller limit angular velocity. angular/z/max_velocity (double) Maximum angular velocity rad/s). angular/z/min_velocity (double) Minimum angular velocity rad/s). Setting disable counter-clockwise rotation. unspecified, -max_velocity angular/z/has_acceleration_limits (bool, default: false) controller limit angular acceleration. angular/z/max_acceleration (double) Maximum angular acceleration rad/s^2). angular/z/min_acceleration (double) Minimum angular acceleration rad/s^2). unspecified, -max_acceleration angular/z/has_jerk_limits (bool, default: false) controller limit angular jerk. angular/z/max_jerk (double) Maximum angular jerk enable_odom_tf (bool, default: true) publish wheel_separation (double) distance left wheel(s). diff_drive_controller attempt read URDF parameter wheel_radius (double) Radius wheels. expected size. diff_drive_controller attempt read URDF parameter odom_frame_id (string, default: “/odom”) frame publish odometry. publish_cmd (bool, default: false) Publish velocity command executed. monitor effect limiters controller input. allow_multiple_cmd_vel_publishers (bool, default: false) enabled controller brake publishers input topic, ~/cmd_vel.

name: urdf
description:
This package contains a C++ parser for the Unified Robot Description
    Format (URDF), which is an XML format for representing a robot model.
    The code API of the parser has been through our review process and will remain
    backwards compatible in future releases.

name: urg_stamped
description: Precisely stamped URG driver for ROS
readme: urg_stamped Precisely accurately stamped URG driver ROS Background Algorithm 2D-LIDAR URG series resolution timestamp exclusive clock synchronization mode. hard compensate clock drift measurement resolution timestamp high-speed motion sensor. urg_stamped estimates sub-millisecond algorithm: Select algorithms based sensor model UTM: UTM-30LX-EW UST(UUST1): UST-*LX, UST-*LC firmware version UST(UUST2-Unfixed): UST-*LX, UST-*LC firmware version UST(UUST2-Fixed): UST-*LX, UST-*LC firmware version Determine sensor internal clock state (clock offset gain) command (Triggered timer default) UTM/UST(UUST1)/UST(UUST2-Fixed): models respond command expected SCIP2 protocol) Observe sub-millisecond clock offset finding increment millisecond resolution sensor timestamp Observe clock gain multiple observations clock offset UST(UUST2-Unfixed): model responds command frame breaks SCIP2’s time synchronization logic) Request command times timing Filter responses large delay Collect sensor response timings synchronized sensor timestamp increment Observe sub-millisecond clock offset based sensor response timings sensor timestamps Observe clock gain multiple observations clock offset Determine scan origin time interval UTM: (UTM sends scan data scan finished) Observe scan timing based scan data arrival time UST: (UST sends scan data frame scan finished) Find sensor scan timestamp jitter Observe scan origin time scan interval scan timestamp jitter Calculate sub-millisecond scan timestamp based observed scan origin time scan interval LaserScan data stopped sensor internal clock estimation takes UTM/UST(UUST1)/UST(UUST2-Fixed) UST(UUST2-Unfixed). avoid stopping sensors multi-sensor configuration, urg_stamped automatically adjusts timing sensor internal clock estimation based messages urg_stamped_sync_start topic. Usages Topics major parameters designed compatible urg_node. Published Topics scan (sensor_msgs::LaserScan) Parameters urg_node compatible parameters ip_address (string): device address ip_port (int): device TCP/IP port frame_id (string): frame_id published scans publish_intensity (bool): fill intensity field true error_limit (int): reset sensor exit errors occur count urg_stamped specific parameters clock_estim_interval (double): sensor internal clock state estimation interval seconds (dropping scans estimation) fallback_on_continuous_scan_drop (int): fallback naive accuracy timestamp failed estimate sub-millisecond timestamp Limitations Timestamp estimation designed sensors connected ethernet interface. Tested sensor models: UTM-30LX-EW UST-05LX UST-20LX UST-30LC UUST2 model UST series (firmware version takes longer time perform time synchronization due sensor’s behavior. scans dropped due clock synchronization delay estimation. Comparison urg_node Configurations UTM-30LX-EWs mounted velocity controlled turntable, shown reconstruct point cloud scans. accuracy timestamp affects offset precision timestamp affects distribution pointcloud. Results [!NOTE] results based previous algorithm (urg_stamped<0.2.0). updated image shows point cloud rad/s turntable assumed reference. name: video_stream_opencv
description:
The video_stream_opencv package contains a node to publish a video stream (the protocols that
    opencv supports are supported, including rtsp, webcams on /dev/video and video files) in ROS image topics, it supports camera info and basic image flipping (horizontal, vertical or both) capabilities, also adjusting publishing rate.
readme: video_stream_opencv package view video streams based OpenCV VideoCapture module, easy publish ROS Image topic (including camera info) usb cams, ethernet cameras, video streams video files. supports flipping images fps throttling. usages launch folder argument video_stream_provider mandatory): <launch> launch video stream <include file="$(find video_stream_opencv)/launch/camera.launch" node ros graph <arg name="camera_name" value="webcam" means video device /dev/video0 <arg name="video_stream_provider" set camera fps device <arg name="set_camera_fps" set buffer queue size frame capturing <arg name="buffer_queue_size" throttling publishing frames <arg name="fps" setting frame_id <arg name="frame_id" value="webcam" camera info loading, care "file:///" start "file:///$(find your_camera_package)/config/your_camera.yaml" <arg name="camera_info_url" flip image horizontally (mirror <arg name="flip_horizontal" value="false" flip image vertically <arg name="flip_vertical" value="false" force width height, means forcing <arg name="width" <arg name="height" visualize image_view window stream generated <arg name="visualize" value="true" </include> </launch> Based ROS tutorial convert opencv images ROS messages. name: visp_auto_tracker
description:
Online automated pattern-based object tracker relying on visual servoing.

    visp_auto_tracker wraps model-based trackers provided by ViSP visual
    servoing library into a ROS package. The tracked object should have a
    QRcode of Flash code pattern. Based on the pattern, the object is
    automaticaly detected. The detection allows then to initialise the
    model-based trackers. When lost of tracking achieves a new detection
    is performed that will be used to re-initialize the tracker.

    This computer vision algorithm computes the pose (i.e. position and
    orientation) of an object in an image. It is fast enough to allow
    object online tracking using a camera.
readme: visp_auto_tracker visp_auto_tracker wraps model-based trackers ViSP visual servoing library ROS package. tracked object QRcode Flash code pattern. Based pattern, object automaticaly detected. detection initialise model-based trackers. lost tracking achieves detection performed re-initialize tracker. computer vision algorithm computes pose position orientation) object image. fast object online tracking camera. package composed node called ‘visp_auto_tracker’. node detect QRcode Flash code object. detection performed, node tracks object. lost tracking occurs node detect object restart tracking. viewer comming visp_tracker package monitor tracking result. Project webpage ros.org: tutorial API reference Project webpage: source code download, bug report Setup package submodules. compiled ROS package catkin_make. Prerequisities visp_auto_tracker depends visp_bridge visp_tracker packages https://github.com/lagadic/vision_visp (indigo-devel branches). visp_auto_tracker depends libdmtx-dev libzbar-dev system dependencies. install run: sudo apt-get install libdmtx-dev libzbar-dev build visp_tracker Supposed catkin work space run: ~/catkin_ws/src git clone indigo-devel https://github.com/lagadic/vision_visp.git catkin_make --pkg visp_auto_tracker Documentation documentation project ROS homepage. information, refer ROS tutorial.

name: yocs_cmd_vel_mux
description:
A multiplexer for command velocity inputs. Arbitrates incoming cmd_vel messages from several topics,
     allowing one topic at a time to command the robot, based on priorities. It also deallocates current
     allowed topic if no messages are received after a configured timeout. All topics, together with their
     priority and timeout are configured through a YAML file, that can be reload at runtime.

name: apriltag_ros
description:
A ROS wrapper of the AprilTag 3 visual fiducial detection
    algorithm. Provides full access to the core AprilTag 3 algorithm's
    customizations and makes the tag detection image and detected tags' poses
    available over ROS topics (including tf). The core AprilTag 3 algorithm is
    extended to allow the detection of tag bundles and a bundle calibration
    script is provided (bundle detection is more accurate than single tag
    detection). Continuous (camera image stream) and single image detector nodes
    are available.

name: arduino_daq
description: AVR8 firmware, a host standalone C++ library, and a ROS node for UAL eCAR's Arduino-based DAQ system, but it is generic enough for use in many other applications
readme: arduino_daq package AVR8 firmware, host standalone library, ROS node UAL eCAR’s Arduino-based DAQ system, generic applications. DAQ features: analog outputs optional MAX5500 chip), ADC inputs, GPIO PWM outputs, channels quadrature encoder decoding support, absolute encoder (EMS22A) input. Outputs feature optional watchdog timer resets output updated periodically (default period precompiled firmware atmega328P, recompiled larger versions pins. Valid pin numbers function pin configured fly host software. Naturally, microcontroller hardware limits special functions: atmega328P GPIO inputs/outputs: ADC inputs: PWM outputs: pins Quadrature encoders: Encoder Channel pin Channels free GPIO pin. Encoder Channel pin Channels free GPIO pin. Compiling GNU/Linux Windows, CMake generate Makefiles project files usual. Set CMake variables BUILD_ROS BUILD_STANDALONE_LIB ON/OFF depending compile ROS node standalone library. compilation ROS catkin environment, simple clone repository ~/catkin_ws/src/ run catkin_make. Prerequisites: sudo apt install libmrpt-dev ROS examples Note: Numering pins Arduino pin number convention Arduino ProMini,ATmega328, pin: chip pin number: Arduino pin number: Launch node roscore terminal rosrun arduino_daq arduino_daq_node _SERIAL_PORT:=/dev/ttyUSB0 terminal terminal Setting DAC analog (volts): rostopic pub /arduino_daq_dac0 std_msgs/Float64 Setting GPIO digital output pin: rostopic pub /arduino_daq_GPIO_output7 std_msgs/Bool Setting PWM output maps duty cycle): rostopic pub /arduino_daq_pwm3 std_msgs/UInt8 Automatically starting ADC sampling reference voltage, channels pins ADC0 ADC1 (Requires killing arduino_daq launch parameters). rosrun arduino_daq arduino_daq_node _SERIAL_PORT:=/dev/ttyUSB0 _ADC_INTERNAL_REFVOLT:=0 _ADC_MEASURE_PERIOD_MS:=100 _ADC_CHANNEL0:=0 _ADC_CHANNEL1:=1 Automatically starting reading ENCODER sampling channel Channel A=pin Channel B=pin Channel Z=pin (set channel wired). (Requires killing arduino_daq launch parameters). rosrun arduino_daq arduino_daq_node _SERIAL_PORT:=/dev/ttyUSB0 _ENC0_PIN_A:=2 _ENC0_PIN_B:=8 _ENC0_PIN_Z:=9 EMS22A absolute encoder: pin_cs); rosrun arduino_daq arduino_daq_node _SERIAL_PORT:=/dev/ttyUSB0 _ENCABS0_PIN_CS:=1 _ENCABS0_PIN_CLK:=2 _ENCABS0_PIN_DO:=2 _ENCABS_MEASURE_PERIOD_MS:=100 ROS graph Source code directories arduino_firmware: AtmelStudio project firmware. Written Arduino library. include libarduinodaq: Standalone library. src: ROS node Frame format Communication arduino based data frames follow: START_FLAG OPCODE DATA_LEN DATA CHECKSUM END_FLAG byte byte bytes =sum(data) complete list OPCODES payload DATA fields defined header file arduinodaq2pc-structs.h, #included host libraries embedded firmware. Programming microcontroller Atmega328p (“Arduino Mini Pro”), command line root directory repository: avrdude atmega328p arduino /dev/ttyUSB0 flash:w:"arduino_firmware/arduino_daq/arduino_daq/Release/arduino_daq.hex":i

name: axcli

readme: axcli simple actionlib CLI client Add package catkin workspace, don’t forget source devel/setup.bash running catkin_make axcli [--nowait] [--timeout TIMEOUT] <action namespace> <goal> Bash provide TAB-completion action servers goals. default axcli wait goal terminate, pressing Ctrl+c send goal cancel request action server. optional arguments change axcli’s behavior: --nowait don’t wait result, fire forget. --timeout timeout seconds wait action server (default: infinite timeout). --verbose verbose output.

name: bhand_controller
description: The bhand_controller package is intended to control the Barrett Hand
readme: bhand_controller controller Barrett Hand python node based original library pyHand. controller able set position velocity motorized joints hand read sensors (fingertip torque tactile sensors, temperature) joint positions. Installation package depends library pcan_python, python wrapper library peak-can. download install library, visit: pcan_python follow installation instructions. bhand_node ROS wrapper pyHand library. Parameters ~port (string, default: ‘/dev/pcan32’): port ~desired_freq (double, default control loop frequency. Recommended ~tactile_sensors (bool, default: True): true hand tactile sensors array. ~ft_sensor (bool, default: False): true hand sensor. ~joint_ids (string[], default: ‘F1_TIP’, ‘F2_TIP’, ‘F3_TIP’, ‘SPREAD_1’, ‘SPREAD_2’]): ids identify joints internally. param modified. ~joint_names (string[], default: [‘bh_j12_joint’, ‘bh_j13_joint’, ‘bh_j22_joint’, ‘bh_j23_joint’, ‘bh_j32_joint’, ‘bh_j31_joint’, ‘bh_j11_joint’, ‘bh_j21_joint’]): joint names hand joints. ~ft_sensor_frame_id (string, default: ‘bhand_ft_sensor_link’): frame sensor. Subscribed Topics ~command (sensor_msgs/JointState) Receives commands move hand Sets position/velocity hand joints depending current control mode. Joints set positions/velocities: bh_j32_joint F3-Base bh_j11_joint F1-Spread bh_j12_joint F1-Base bh_j21_joint F2-Spread bh_j22_joint F2-Base bh_j11_joint bh_j21_joint set motor publish rostopic pub /bhand_node/command sensor_msgs/JointState "header: seq: stamp: {secs: nsecs: frame_id: ['bh_j11_joint', 'bh_j32_joint', 'bh_j12_joint', 'bh_j22_joint'] position: velocity: effort: Published Topics ~joint_states (sensor_msgs/JointState) Publishes current joint states ~state(bhand_controller/State) Current state hand. state: control_mode: VELOCITY hand_initialized: True desired_freq: real_freq: state_description: READY_STATE temp_f1: temp_f2: temp_f3: temp_spread: ~tact_array(bhand_controller/TactArray) Values Barrett Hand tactile array. Units: N/cm² header: seq: stamp: secs: nsecs: frame_id: finger1: finger2: finger3: palm: ~force_torque(bhand_controller/ForceTorque) Values Barrett Hand Force Torque sensor Services ~actions (bhand_controller/Actions) Performs specific actions movements actions: INIT_HAND CLOSE_GRASP OPEN_GRASP SET_GRASP_1 SET_GRASP_2 CLOSE_HALF_GRASP TARE_FTS rosservice call /bhand_node/actions "action: (Inits hand) ~set_control_mode(std_msgs/String) Sets hand control mode rosservice call /bhand_node/set_control_mode "mode: 'VELOCITY'" Services Called Action server Action clients called Required Transforms Transforms Bringup run controller. roslaunch bhand_controller bhand_controller.launch action hand initialized calling service ~actions INIT_HAND.

name: choreo
description: The choreo metapackage

name: choreo_core
description:
this package is the central monitoring node that connects
    Choreo's specialized packages together. It receives requests from UI
    package and dispatch / receive computation request to responsible packages.

name: choreo_descartes_planner
description:
This package extends descartes planning package's ability to search joint path on a much larger ladder graph,
    by using sparse representation sparse ladder graph and run RRT* on this sparse graph.
    Then the sparse graph is extended back to ladder graph and run shortest path on it.
    This package is a part of the choreo assembly planning framework and contains
    several assembly application-dependent helper functions.

name: choreo_execution_gatekeeper
description:
This package is responsible for the execution of process plans generated
    from core service clients.
    Currently it can stream generated trajectory from choreo to down stream trajectory simulation packages and
    at the moment vendor- and dof- agnostic.

    It has potential to be further connected to online control system, but might need more vendor-specific settings.

name: choreo_geometry_conversion_helpers
description:
Helper functions for converting geometry data for Choreo,
    e.g. eigen composition or msg conversion
readme: choreo_geometry_conversion_helpers TODO: introduction. Developed Yijiang Huang Digital Structures Research Group, MIT.

name: choreo_gui
description:
The choreo_gui package presents an alternative front end for the choreo
    project that is focused on providing an easy to use, ATM-like interface for operating the system end to end.

name: choreo_msgs
description:
This package contains all the messages, services, and actions used in the Choreo
  framework to enable communications between nodes.

name: choreo_output_processor
description:
This package converts computed plans to a customized json format that enables micro-path modifications
    and IO commands synchronization on any preferred programming platform downstream.

name: choreo_param_helpers
description:
This package implements routines for loading, saving, and checking for the
    existence of user parameters. This package should work with only "include".

name: choreo_planning_capability
description: This package provides a unified assembly_type (capability) naming across various choreo packages.

name: choreo_task_sequence_processor

This package convert paths generated by choreo_task_sequence_planning into ros format for further process planning

readme: Dialogflow_ros package Google Text-To-Speech (TTS) API send results Dialogflow, Google’s NLP platform. information ROS wiki. Reproduced Installation install.sh script git directory steps one-by-one Installing package requires main steps: cloning dialogflow repo, setting Google cloud project, setting Dialogflow. install PortAudio PyAudio mic data. sudo apt-get install portaudio19-dev Cloning Repo Install requirements pip cloning Github repo installing packages requirements.txt. ~/catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/piraka9011/dialogflow_ros.git dialogflow_ros pip install requirements.txt Google Cloud Setup Follow instructions configuring Google Cloud project installing SDK authentication. google/gmail account. Usage Google Cloud SDK requires authentication. means require API key activated service account utilize APIs. Setup service account Download service account key JSON. Check GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS environment. path keys. export GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS='/path/to/key' Run authentication command: gcloud auth activate-service-account --key-file GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS Dialogflow Setup Follow steps setup authentication Dialogflow. Note project-id change config/params.yaml. Usage Follow steps setup package properly. Configure topics config directory change parameters params.yaml file: results_topic: (Optional) topic results published. project_id: project Google Speech node. Google Cloud project Google Cloud setup. Launching nodes start Dialogflow nodes, run command: roslaunch dialogflow_ros dialogflow.launch ROS Nodes mic_client ROS node receives text Google Cloud Speech API publishes text_topic config/params.yaml). dialogflow_client node. Published Topics text_topic (std_msgs/String) Acquired text Google Cloud Speech API. dialogflow_client ROS node takes text mic_client node sends Dialogflow parsing. Published Topics results_topic (dialogflow_msgs/DialogflowResult) Publishes message actions, parameters (python dictionary), fulfillment text detected intent std_msgs/String.

name: dynamixel_controllers
description:
This package contains a configurable node, services and a spawner script
        to start, stop and restart one or more controller plugins. Reusable
        controller types are defined for common Dynamixel motor joints. Both speed and
        torque can be set for each joint. This python package can be used by more
        specific robot controllers and all configurable parameters can be loaded
        via a yaml file.
authors: Antons Rebguns, Cody Jorgensen, Cara Slutter

name: dynamixel_driver
description:
This package provides low level IO for Robotis Dynamixel servos.
    Fully supports and was tested with AX-12, AX-18, RX-24, RX-28,
    MX-28, RX-64, EX-106 models. Hardware specific constants are
    defined for reading and writing information from/to Dynamixel
    servos. This low level package won't be used directly by most
    ROS users. The higher level dynamixel_controllers and specific
    robot joint controllers make use of this package.

name: dynamixel_motor
description:
This stack contains packages that are used to interface with Robotis
    Dynamixel line of servo motors. This stack was tested with and fully
    supports AX-12, AX-18, RX-24, RX-28, MX-28, RX-64, MX-64, EX-106 and
    MX-106 models.

name: dynamixel_msgs
description: Common messages used throughout dynamixel_motor stack.

name: dynamixel_tutorials
description: Example configuration and launch file for dynamixel_motor stack.

Orientation set identity quaternion Rosservice services piksi/settings_write: writes specific setting Piksi Multi; piksi/settings_read_req: requests parameter Piksi Multi; piksi/settings_read_resp: reads latest response “settings_read_req” request; piksi/settings_save: saves current settings Piksi Multi flash memory.

name: flask_cors
description: Cross Origin Resource Sharing ( CORS ) support for Flask
readme: Flask-CORS Flask extension handling Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS), making cross-origin AJAX package simple philosophy: enable CORS, enable cases domain. means mucking allowed headers, methods, default, submission cookies domains disabled due security implications. documentation enable credential'ed requests, add sort CSRF protection Installation Install extension pip, easy_install. {.sourceCode .bash} pip install flask-cors Usage package exposes Flask extension default enables CORS support routes, origins methods. parameterization CORS headers per-resource level. package decorator, prefer approach. Simple Usage simplest case, initialize Flask-Cors extension default arguments order CORS domains routes. full list options [documentation](https://flask-cors.corydolphin.com/en/latest/api.html#extension). {.sourceCode .python} flask import Flask flask_cors import CORS app Flask(__name__) CORS(app) @app.route("/") def helloWorld(): return cross-origin-world!" Resource specific CORS Alternatively, CORS options resource origin level granularity passing dictionary [resources]{.title-ref} option, mapping paths set options. full list options documentation. {.sourceCode .python} app Flask(name) cors CORS(app, resources={r”/api/”: {“origins”: @app.route(“/api/v1/users”) def list_users(): return “user Route specific CORS decorator extension exposes simple decorator decorate flask routes Simply add `@cross_origin()` call Flask\'s `@app.route(..)` CORS route. full list options [decorator documentation](https://flask-cors.corydolphin.com/en/latest/api.html#decorator). {.sourceCode .python} @app.route("/") @cross_origin() def helloWorld(): return cross-origin-world!" Documentation full list options, full documentation Troubleshooting working expect, enable logging understand hood, {.sourceCode .python} logging.getLogger(‘flask_cors’).level logging.DEBUG Tests simple set tests included test/. run, install nose, simply invoke nosetests python setup.py test exercise tests. nosetests work due longer working newer python versions. pytest run tests Contributing Questions, comments improvements? create issue Github, tweet \@corydolphin send email. include contribution proposed Credits Flask extension based Decorator HTTP Access Control written Armin Ronacher.

name: geonav_transform
description: The geonav_transform package
readme: geonav_transform goal package simplify integration accurate/precise geographic navigation information (typically sensor) ROS localization navigation workflows. ends, geonav_tranform_node perform functions: Takes incoming Odometry messages, typically sensor, geographic position sensor-frame orientation velocities. (Note, nice message specifies type input Odometry bit hack.) Tranforms message Odometry message express information frames utm odom Broadcasts tranforms utm->odom odom->base_link use-case motivated project integrating sensors provide GPS-aided INS solution microstrain, advanced navigation, Xsens, situation analogous ekf/ukf node robot_localization package fuse IMU GPS information, case processing sensor. purpose package integration type sensor ROS navigation stack. Parameters ~datum: origin local “odom” frame element array [Latitude, Logitude, Altitude]. Lat/Lon decimal degrees; altitude meters. Default ~frequency: frequency broadcasting tranforms. Odometry messages published rate incoming Odometry messages. Default ~broadcast_utm2odom_transform: broadcast utm->odom tranform. Default True. ~broadcast_odom2base_transform: broadcast odom->base_link tranform. Default True. ~zero_altitude ~base_link_frame_id: Default “base_link” ~odom_frame_id: Default “odom” ~utm_frame_id: Default “utm” Subscribed Topics /odometry/nav: nav_msgs/Odometry message geographic position velocity data. message organized header.frame_id child_frame_id values pose.pose.position Latitude [dec. degrees] Longitude [dec. degrees] Altitude pose.pose.orientation base_link relative fixed ENU coordinate frame ~orientation_ned parameter set true, node convert orientation NED ENU. assuming orientation true magnetic). Typically magnetic declination set internal sensor providing information. pose.covariance expressed meters position radians orientation (REP-103) twist.twist.linear/angular velocity base_link frame twist.covariance expressed rad/s. Published Topics message published rate incoming /odometry/nav messages. Consistent navigation stack robot_localization, Odometry messages position pose information relative fixed frame (utm odom, reported header.frame_id). velocity (twist) information relative mobile frame (base_link, reported .child_frame). /odometry/odom: nav_msgs/Odometry message local odom frame (relative datum) /odometry/utm: nav_msgs/Odometry message UTM frame published static tranform: http://wiki.ros.org/tf2_ros http://wiki.ros.org/tf2/Tutorials/Writing%20a%20tf2%20static%20broadcaster%20(C%2B%2B) Published Transforms utm->odom odom->base_link Frames utm: global UTM coordinate frame. origin frame UTM zone determined datum parameter odom: local, fixed odom frame orgin datum parameter. assumed orientation UTM odom frame. general simplifies implementation, usage interpretation. base_link: mobile frame typically coincides sensor frame.

name: hector_object_tracker
description:
hector_object_tracker is the core package of hector_worldmodel. It listens to percept message from
     detectors (e.g. heat signatures of persons or recognitions of other objects of interest in the
     scene) and fuses all information to a single worldmodel state. Objects will be tracked over time
     and their states can be influenced by a couple of services.

     The percept to object association problem is solved either automatically based on the Mahalanobis
     distance, or a unique object_id can be given in the percept message.

     If a hector_nav_msgs/GetDistanceToObstacle service is available, the object_tracker can optionally
     deduce the depth of objects in the scene by projection to the nearest obstacle (wall).

name: hector_worldmodel_msgs
description:
hector_worldmodel_msgs is a message package to comes with the hector_worldmodel stack.
     The messages can be used to send percepts from images (hector_worldmodel_msgs/ImagePercept) or other sources
     (hector_worldmodel_msgs/PosePercept) to the hector_object_tracker node. The tracker publishes model updates as
     hector_worldmodel_msgs/Object messages and latches the whole model state as a hector_worldmodel_msgs/ObjectModel message.

name: hls_lfcd_lds2_driver

HLDS(Hitachi-LG Data Storage) is developing the technology for the moving platform sensor such as Robot Vacuum Cleaners, Home Robot, Robotics Lawn Mower Sensor, etc.

Full documentation wiki: http://wiki.ros.org/image_overlay_scale_and_compass Quick Start Install: sudo apt-get install ros-kinetic-image-overlay-compass-and-scale Launch node: roslaunch image_overlay_scale_and_compass overlay.launch Publish heading scale values rostopic pub /heading std_msgs/Float32 rostopic pub /scale std_msgs/Float32 View resulting image rqt_image_view /science/overlay/compressed Command Line Interface (CLI) Invoke Command Line Interface (CLI) save disk pulishing ROS. roscd image_overlay_scale_and_compass ./src/image_overlay_scale_and_compass/image_overlay.py --input-image ~/mars.png --heading --scale-text --output-file output.png CLI Options Usage: image_overlay.py [OPTIONS] Options: --input-image TEXT Path input image file [required] --heading FLOAT Current heading relative north degrees [required] --scale-text FLOAT displayed scale bar centemeters [required] --output-file TEXT Output filename save result [default='output.png'] Show message exit. Full documentation wiki: http://wiki.ros.org/image_overlay_scale_and_compass

$OPENPOSE_INSTALL_PATH $OPENPOSE_INSTALL_PATH/ubuntu/install_cuda.sh $OPENPOSE_INSTALL_PATH/ubuntu/install_cudnn.sh wget https://github.com/CMU-Perceptual-Computing-Lab/caffe/archive/1.0.tar.gz /tmp/1.0.tar.gz tar -xvf /tmp/1.0.tar.gz /tmp/ /tmp/caffe-1.0/* $OPENPOSE_INSTALL_PATH/3rdparty/caffe $OPENPOSE_INSTALL_PATH mkdir build build cmake -DBUILD_PYTHON=1 name: indoor_positioning
description:
This is a ROS package for the metraTec Indoor Positioning System.
    You can use the regular IPS for zone location of your robot
    or the IPS+ products for 3D-position-estimation using UWB ranging.
readme: indoor_positioning ROS package metraTec Indoor Positioning System. regular IPS zone location robot IPS+ products 3D-position-estimation UWB ranging. image shows indoor positioning functionality. beacons environment localize receiver vicinity. IPS displays current (user-defined) zone receiver (green rectangle). Additionally, IPS+ position estimation, actual 3D-position receiver displayed (pink sphere). Installation ROS binaries (preferred method): Simply run command change distribution sudo apt-get install ros-ROSDISTRO-indoor-positioning source: binary release, older version ROS, manually build package clone repository source folder catkin workspace: git clone https://github.com/metratec/indoor_positioning.git YOUR_CATKIN_WS/src/indoor_positioning Install python dependencies pip install requirements.txt able package (catkin) package workspace. III. Usage ```receiver.py node handles communication receiver publishes incoming messages. run node order IPS IPS+ node. run ```positioning.py node IPS ```positioning_plus.py node IPS+ theoretically). Setup able nodes zone location (IPS) UWB positioning (IPS+) setup environment YAML configuration file. ```config/zones_template.yml information structure content config file. Launch files launch files test package default parameters. ```ips.launch file starts receiver IPS node zone location. ```ips_plus.launch file starts receiver IPS+ node UWB position estimation. TCP USB receiver connected. launching IPS node metraTec USB receiver roslaunch indoor_positioning ips.launch rec_name:=port rec_value:=/dev/ttyUSB0 launching IPS+ node TCP receiver roslaunch indoor_positioning ips_plus.launch rec_name:=host rec_value:=192.168.2.223 non-default parameters nodes set ROS parameter server write launch files set respective parameters launch files mentioned _Note:_ setting ```do_vis parameter launch files ```True simultaneously start rviz predefined settings display defined beacon positions, current zone and/or estimated receiver position. Nodes receiver.py node establish connection metraTec IPS receiver USB stick. pass USB port stick connected address regular receiver command line argument private parameter launch file. Running command line: rosrun indoor_positioning receiver.py TYPE REQUIRED OPTIONAL rosrun indoor_positioning receiver.py usb /dev/ttyUSB0 rosrun indoor_positioning receiver.py tcp Subscribed topics: ips/receiver/send (std_msgs/String): Message TCP serial connection IPS receiver USB stick Published topics: ips/receiver/raw (indoor_positioning/StringStamped): Raw messages received receiver USB stick Parameters: ~type (string, default=’tcp’): ‘tcp’ TCP receiver ‘usb’ metraTec USB stick ~host (string, default=’192.168.2.223): address connected receiver ~port type=’tcp’: int, default=10001; type=’usb’: string, default=’/dev/ttyUSB0): Port TCP connection ‘~type’ parameter set ‘tcp’, specifies USB port stick ~baudrate (int, default=115200): Baudrate serial communication USB receiver positioning.py (IPS) node perform indoor zone location metraTec IPS tracking system. Prerequisites node running receiver-node handles communication receiver beacons vicinity. defined zones correctly YAML config file. Subscribed topics: ips/receiver/raw (indoor_positioning/StringStamped): Raw messages received UWB receiver Published topics: ips/receiver/current_zone/name (indoor_positioning/StringStamped): zone receiver ips/receiver/current_zone/polygon (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped): Polygon comprising current zone ips/receiver/zone_leave (indoor_positioning/StringStamped): zone receiver left. published moment zone-leave occurs ips/receiver/zone_enter (indoor_positioning/StringStamped): zone receiver entered. published moment zone-enter occurs Parameters: ~config_file (string, default=’config/zones.yml’): Path configuration file zones beacons relative package directory ~rate (double, default=1): publishing rate messages ~bcn_len (int, default=2*number_of_beacons): Buffer length BCN messages positioning_plus.py (IPS+) node perform indoor positioning metraTec IPS+ tracking system. Creating config YAML file Create tra1_jog_config/config/tra1_jog.yaml file set parameters node. jog_joint_node: joint_names: joint1 joint2 joint3 joint4 joint5 joint6 jog_frame_node: group_names: manipulator link_names: link_j6 ‘joint_names:’ joint names MoveIt! config file. joints list jog control don’t unused joints. joints MoveIt! config file. ‘group_names’ list group MoveIt! config file. ‘link_names:’ list target frame control jog_frame_node. Creating launch file Create launch file tra1_jog_config/launch/tra1_jog.launch <launch> <arg name="use_joy" default="false"/> Jog controllers <rosparam command="load" file="$(find tra1_jog_config)/config/tra1_jog.yaml"/> <node name="jog_joint_node" pkg="jog_controller" type="jog_joint_node"/> <node name="jog_frame_node" pkg="jog_controller" type="jog_frame_node"/> Launch joypad <include if="$(arg use_joy)" file="$(find jog_controller)/launch/joypad.launch"> <arg name="group_name" value="manipulator"/> <arg name="frame_id" value="base_link"/> <arg name="link_name" value="link_j6"/> </include> </launch> joypad jog, set use_jog argument true. Choose push button, add panels left side rviz window. notice drag panels adjust size position rviz pane. joypad joypad.launch launch file bringup joypad driver (joy node) converter node (joy_to_jog_frame.py). launch file arguments. joy_dev (default: /dev/input/js0) device file joypad. defice file joypad check device /dev/input command. joy_config (default: xbox_wireless) Configure file setup button assignments. find config/xbox_wireless.config.yaml. Notice modify config file fit joypad device. group_name (default: manipulator) MoveIt! group jog. identical group link_name (default: tool0) link_name jog. valid frame group. frame_id Reference frame frame jogging. valid frame base_link, tool0 check joypad launching joypad.launch. roslaunch jog_controller joypad.launch error [ERROR] open joystick /dev/input/js0. retry check joypad connection device file /dev/input/. note joypad powered (XBox wireless silver power button shaped front joypad.) error, check jog_frame topic published. rostopic echo /jog_frame push ‘Enable’ button set enable_button parameter config file. message header: seq: stamp: secs: nsecs: frame_id: "base_link" group_name: "manipulator" link_name: "tool0" linear_delta: angular_delta: avoid_collisions: True check sticks produce desired message. name: micros_swarm_framework
description: This is a programming framework to facilitate application development involving robot swarms. It makes coding for swarms much easier by providing an adequate swarm-level abstraction, as well as tools for swarm management, various communication mechanisms and so on. It also provides essential data structures, such as Neighbor, Swarm, and Virtual Stigmergy, to the user. Most importantly, it is completely compatible with ROS Indigo and presented in the form of a C++ library, which means that all resources in the ROS ecosystem are still available to the user. It is currently  extensible to Opensplice DDS.

name: msp
description: Implementation of the MultiWii Serial Protocol (MSP)
readme: MultiWii Cleanflight Baseflight Communication Library library implements MultiWii Serial Protocol (MSP) communicating MultiWii Cleanflight flight controller serial device. defines low-level API sending+encoding receiving+decoding MSP messages, high-level API subscriber pattern periodically request data call callback functions message received. communication tested MultiWii Arduino Nano achieve update rate approximately cycle time Betaflight Naze32 Rev6 update rates max. Installation Test Linux (Ubuntu Debian) install asio ninja: sudo apt install --no-install-recommends ninja-build libasio-dev check source code cmake compile: cmake build -GNinja -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release cmake --build build run program path serial device: ./msp_read_test /dev/ttyUSB0 Windows Requirements CMake asio: download asio-1.20.0.zip extract header files, C:\asio-1.20.0 Visual Build Tools: https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/de/downloads/ Build Test open Developer Command Prompt Visual Studio change directory checked msp run: cmake build -GNinja -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DASIO_ROOT=C:\asio-1.20.0 cmake --build build test serial device: msp_read_test.exe Hardware Setup connect Arduino Naze32 boards built-in USB-to-Serial connector direct serial connection (RX->TX, TX->RX, GND->GND, VCC->VCC). option preferable high transfer rates, direct connection exposed transmission errors. MultiWii MSP update rate determined variable LOOP_TIME config.h #define LOOP_TIME sets loop time update rate Arduino boards integrated USB-to-serial converter auto-reset serial connection established. send messages opening connection request send response. prevent delay opening connection sending messages, disabling Data Terminal Ready (DTR) line, stty /dev/ttyUSB0 -hupcl. Cleanflight Betaflight change update rate serial task range set serial_update_rate_hz=2000, save increase serial baudrate Ports configuration tab Sending Receiving commands activate feature RX_MSP GUI: Configuration Receiver Receiver Mode MSP input CLI: execute feature RX_MSP Beginning Cleanflight Betaflight MSP_SET_RAW_RC messages targets insufficient flash memory naze32). verify MSP_RC messages return empty list channels. activate MSP_SET_RAW_RC messages targets, add #define USE_RX_MSP target.h, src/main/target/NAZE/target.h. FlightContoller API FlightContoller API user send/receive messages flight controller. Messages queried periodically, demand. Instantiation Instantiate setup FlightController class: #include <FlightController.hpp> fcu::FlightController fcu; connection setup fcu.connect("/dev/ttyUSB0", Periodic request messages Messages queried periodically handled automatically subscription. class method stand-alone function. Class method pattern Define class holds callback functions process information received message. function signature void <callback_name>(const <child_of_msp::Message>& class App public: void onStatus(const msp::Status& status) std::cout<<status; void onImu(const msp::Imu& imu) std::cout<<imu; Instantiate class callbacks register FCU desired update rate seconds: App app; fcu.subscribe(&App::onStatus, &app, fcu.subscribe(&App::onImu, &app, Requests processed flight controller fast important note MultiWii FCU processed single message cycle. subscribed messages share effective bandwidth messages Lambda pattern FlightController::subscribe method restricted callbacks return void argument const msp::Message&. order call method doesn’t match signature additional information), wrap non-compliant method lambda matches expected signature. auto callback [](const msp::msg::RawImu& imu){ ImuSI subclass msp::Message, suitable callback argument std::cout msp::msg::ImuSI(imu, fcu.subscribe<msp::msg::RawImu>(callback, Manual sending messages Additional messages periodically dispatched method bool sendMessage(msp::Message &message, const double timeout instantiate instance object matching message send provide method optional timeout. timeout paramter method wait (possibly response received flight controller. strictly positive limit waiting interval. msp::Status status; (fcu.sendMessage(status) status values returned flight controller msp::SetCalibrationData calibration; calibration.acc_zero_x calibration.acc_zero_y calibration.acc_zero_x calibration.acc_gain_x calibration.acc_gain_y calibration.acc_gain_z (fcu.sendMessage(calibration) calibration data message return data, values unchanged message type data response flight controller, instance message sendMessage call values unpacked flight controller’s response.

name: multipoint_navigation_receiver
description:
It is a function table that receives multipoint information,
  then controls the time of sending the target point and serves the Move_base
readme: multipoint_navigation_receiver 基于ROS的多点导航功能包 catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/SuperAruba/multipoint_navigation_receiver.git catkin_make 多点导航需要用到multipoint_navigation_receiver节点，由于考虑到目标点可能出现长时间存在障碍物的情况，故增加了一个判断地图中某点的障碍物信息的节点obstacle_server节点。 obstacle_server节点订阅了/move_base/global_costmap/costmap话题，使用服务机制，服务名称为/obstacle_information，请求服务时参数为地图某点的X坐标和Y坐标。该节点可以直接用于其他节点请求地图中的障碍物数据。该节点依赖于global_costmap，所以需要在global_costmap中加入obstacle layer。 multipoint_navigation_receiver节点使用时需要先开启obstacle_server节点，也使用服务机制，传入的参数为位置和姿态一一对应的两个数组（考虑到某些其他语言无法直接得到move_base_msgs::MoveBaseGoal数据类型）分别为geometry_msgs/Vector3[]和geometry_msgs/Quaternion[]。 BSD

name: mvsim

License: 3-clause BSD License Copyright Jose Luis Blanco jlblanco@ual.es (University Almeria) collaborators refer MVSim SoftwareX paper ArXiV preprint) gentle introduction project architecture. cite MVSim work, @article{blanco2023mvsim, title {MultiVehicle Simulator (MVSim): Lightweight dynamics simulator multiagents mobile robotics research}, journal {SoftwareX}, volume pages year issn doi {https://doi.org/10.1016/j.softx.2023.101443}, url {https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2352711023001395}, author {José-Luis Blanco-Claraco Borys Tymchenko Francisco José Mañas-Alvarez Fernando Cañadas-Aránega Ángel López-Gázquez José Carlos Moreno} ROSCon talk Spanish talk English slides subtitles (slides Installation installation documentation details options. easiest install ROS ROS sudo apt install ros-$ROS_DISTRO-mvsim jump steps launch demo worlds. Demo videos Build matrix status Distro Build dev Build releases Stable version ROS Noetic amd64 arm64 armhf ROS Humble amd64 arm64 ROS Jazzy amd64 arm64 ROS Rolling amd64 arm64 EOL distro Stable version ROS Melodic ROS Foxy ROS Iron Docs Main documentation site clone repository, remember checkout git submodules git clone https://github.com/MRPT/mvsim.git --recursive Launch demos steps Standalone: mvsim launch mvsim_tutorial/demo_warehouse.world.xml mvsim launch mvsim_tutorial/demo_2robots.world.xml mvsim launch mvsim_tutorial/test_mesh.world.xml ROS roslaunch mvsim demo_depth_camera.launch ROS ros2 launch mvsim demo_warehouse.launch.py ros2 launch mvsim demo_depth_camera.launch.py Main features Lightweight memory, CPU library requirements. Vehicle models: Differential driven wheel drive). Ackermann steering (kinematic dynamic steering, mechanical drive models). Ackermann steering mechanical differentials full grade. name: myahrs_driver
description: myahrs_driver is a driver package for the WITHROBOT's myAHRS+. The myAHRS+ is a low cost high performance AHRS(Attitude Heading Reference System) with USB/UART/I2C interface. The myAHRS+ board contains a 3-axis 16-bit gyroscope, a 3-axis 16-bit accelerometer and a 3-axis 13-bit magnetometer. The driver should also work with USB port.
readme: myahrs_driver Overview driver package WITHROBOT’s myAHRS+ http://www.lilliputdirect.com/odroid-myahrs lilliputdirect http://www.hardkernel.com/main/products/prdt_info.php?g_code=G141464363369 myAHRS+ cost high performance AHRS(Attitude Heading Reference System) USB/UART/I2C interface. myAHRS+ board 3-axis 16-bit gyroscope, 3-axis 16-bit accelerometer 3-axis 13-bit magnetometer. driver work USB port. Axes Convention myAHRS+ board NED type. myahrs_driver contained package converts frame conventions ROS east north (ENU) convention hand rule) publishing msgs. driver coordinate frame http://www.ros.org/reps/rep-0103.html#axis-orientation information. left NED type IMU: x-north, y-east, z-down, relative magnetic north. ENU type IMU: x-east, y-north, z-up, relative magnetic north. Original Source original source support ROS) maintained github tutorials wiki page. visualization test 3D-box included original source. package myAHRS+ SDK https://github.com/withrobot/myAHRS_plus Video visualization demonstration RViz. Installation Install package: sudo apt-get install ros-indigo-myahrs-driver Install package github: ~/catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/robotpilot/myahrs_driver.git ~/catkin_ws catkin_make Run Run driver rosrun myahrs_driver myahrs_driver _port:=/dev/ttyACM0 roslaunch myahrs_driver myahrs_driver.launch Nodes Official ROS documentation ROS wiki http://wiki.ros.org/myahrs_driver Communication Protocol Manual Forum myAHRS+ protocol here(https://github.com/withrobot/myAHRS_plus/tree/master/tutorial). Forum myAHRS+ user here(http://forum.odroid.com/viewforum.php?f=109). References References myAHRS+ board http://www.withrobot.com/myahrs_plus_en/ http://www.withrobot.com/?wpdmact=process&did=MTE4LmhvdGxpbms= https://github.com/robotpilot/myAHRS_plus http://www.hardkernel.com/main/products/prdt_info.php?g_code=G141464363369 http://www.lilliputdirect.com/odroid-myahrs References convention axes unit http://www.ros.org/reps/rep-0003.html http://www.ros.org/reps/rep-0103.html https://github.com/paulbovbel/rep/blob/master/rep-0145.rst References similar IMU packages http://wiki.ros.org/um6 http://wiki.ros.org/razor_imu_9dof https://github.com/KristofRobot/razor_imu_9dof http://docs.ros.org/api/sensor_msgs/html/msg/Imu.html

name: pattern_manager
description: A ROS package for defining, configuring, and working with patterns in robotics applications. This allows a robotics application developer to easily define a pattern, or group of patterns, for batch processing of structured parts, e.g. palletizing operations.
maintainers: Mikkel Rath Hansen, Mads Vainoe Baatrup
authors: Mads Vainoe Baatrup, Mikkel Rath Hansen
readme: Pattern Manager package implements ROS package defining, configuring, working patterns robotics applications. robotics application developer easily define pattern, group patterns, batch processing structured parts, palletizing operations. Status implemented ROS package purely Python, key components working patterns rest FTP: Structure Behaviour patterns transforms represented single class: XForm. XForm transform) class implemented tree node. transform contained XForm node. XForm class functions manage configure transform tree structure. Patterns transforms consist parent XForm object child-XForm objects tree structure) positioned respective pattern shape, relative parent. XForm nodes attribute describing node “active”, Setting property choosing transforms iterated affords functionality iterating transforms sequentially. Pattern classes implemented plugins, allowing easy extensibility set pattern types. standard set pattern plugins implemented generating primitive pattern types linear, rectangular, circular, scatter). group transforms and/or patterns creating XForm object assigning parent patterns sub-groups grp1 grp2 XForm tree stored .yaml file, addition allowing users persistently store configuration, editing tree hand text editor. Saved manually constructed) .yaml files loaded generate reflective XForm tree. required functionality creating managing patterns transforms enabled ROS services. Geometric information position orientation transform published broadcasted frame coordinate management tool, data broadcasted transforms node tracked visualized time tools ROS visualization tool, RViz. node publishes Marker Messages draw primitive, color-coded, markers RViz transform position. markers transform active transform iteration. Graphical User Interface Additionally, GUI (rqt_pattern_manager) developed enable simple intuitive interaction Pattern Manager node. GUI designed RQT plugin easy integrate existing ROS software, good sense case RViz, accepts RQT plugins extended program functionality. means GUI opened RViz part editor’s environment. functionalities interacting Pattern Manager ROS node, RQT plugin. GUI additionally drag-and-drop functionality reordering re-parenting transforms (incl. patterns). Documentation, testing, integration source code documented reStructuredText docstrings. API documentation generated rosdoc_lite (utilizing Sphinx). Unit test created source code modules (Python unittest library). Package added ROS build farm. Documentation added ROS documentation index. Tutorials added ROS wiki page. tree structure Patterns transforms grouped patterns. Additionally, groupings sub-grouped. enables user combine multiple (groups patterns, turn seamless iteration multiple patterns, pattern. structure: root <transform-name> [<transform-number>] grp1 transform pattern group/container lin1 linear pattern transforms lin1_1 lin1_2 lin1_3 lin2 lin2_1 lin2_2 lin2_3 grp2 grp3 rect1 rectangular pattern transforms rect1_1 rect1_2 Acknowledgement Supported ROSIN ROS-Industrial Quality-Assured Robot Software Components project received funding European Union’s Horizon research innovation programme grant agreement

name: pepper_moveit_config
description: An automatically generated package with all the configuration and launch files for using the Pepper robot with the MoveIt Motion Planning Framework
readme: pepper_moveit_config MoveIt! config package generated MoveIt! wizard. requires Pepper model https://github.com/ros-naoqi/pepper_robot/tree/master/pepper_description binary package ros-indigo-pepper-description moveit package run remote computer robot. Compile package pepper_moveit_config package compilation running rospack profile ready play Pepper Run MoveIt robot run moveit package unconnected robot attached robot (real simulated): standalone execution {.sourceCode .bash} roslaunch pepper_moveit_config demo.launch launching demo, robot MoveIt! GUI ![image](tuto/moveit_launch.png){width="100.0%"} Check box Approximate Solutions optionally, choose Planning library), ready control robot. real robot MoveIt! real robot, instanciate ROS controllers robot. [pepper\_dcm\_bringup](https://github.com/ros-naoqi/pepper_dcm_robot) [pepper\_control](https://github.com/ros-naoqi/pepper_virtual) set NAO\_IP environment variable robot\'s address: {.sourceCode .bash} export NAO_IP=<YOUR_ROBOT_IP> Launch DCM bringup: {.sourceCode .bash} roslaunch pepper_dcm_bringup pepper_bringup.launch Wait ready, run: {.sourceCode .bash} roslaunch pepper_moveit_config moveit_planner.launch visual data robot's cameras integrate visual data robot's cameras MoveIt obstacle detection. ways acquire visual data: naoqi_driver {.sourceCode .bash} roslaunch naoqi_driver naoqi_driver.launch pepper\_sensors\_py data single camera) {.sourceCode .bash} roslaunch pepper_sensors_py camera.launch obstacle detection based Octomap library integrated MoveIt, change active camera depth camera rqt\_reconfigure. tune acquisition parameters needed: {.sourceCode .bash} rosrun rqt_reconfigure rqt_reconfigure depth data configure Octomap represents robot\'s space set occupied free voxels. ![image](tuto/moveit_octomap.png){width="100.0%"} simulated robot Gazebo) MoveIt! Gazebo simulation, [pepper\_dcm\_bringup](https://github.com/ros-naoqi/pepper_dcm_robot) [pepper\_control](https://github.com/ros-naoqi/pepper_virtual) [pepper\_gazebo\_plugin](https://github.com/ros-naoqi/pepper_virtual) Launch Gazebo, {.sourceCode .bash} roslaunch pepper_gazebo_plugin pepper_gazebo_plugin_Y20.launch Wait ready, click Play button Gazebo. Finally, start MoveIt!: {.sourceCode .bash} roslaunch pepper_moveit_config moveit_planner.launch Moveit: MoveIt GUI, check box approximate Solutions" bottom left control panel. Planning tab. Select part robot move: plugin list, MotionPlanning/Planning Request/Planning Group choose roobot's part control. interactive marker chosen robot's part. control robot drag dropping interactive markers. compute trajectory reach position, clicking 'Planning' button Planning tab. satisfied planned trajectory, real robot 'Execute' and/or 'Plan execute' buttons. NOTE: start state updated automatically, 'Select Start State' select 'Current' click 'Update'.

readme: Pionner_bringup ROS package providing roslaunch functionalities starting Adept MobileRobots Pioneer Pioneer-compatible robots (Including Pioneer Pioneer Pioneer AmigoBot, PeopleBot, PatrolBot, PowerBot, Seekur Seekur Compatible RosAria P2OS hardware controllers. Installation Depending install dependencies: ROSARIA P2OS Urg_node: Hokuyo lidars LMS1XX: Sick LMS100 lidar Sicktoolbox_wrapper: Sick LMS2xx lidar Sick_scan: Sick lidars list bellow) Usbcam: usb cameras webcams Freenect: Kinect Pionner_dashboard: GUI dashboard window Usage starting robot default parameters: roslaunch pioneer_bringup pioneer_bringup.launch activate features arguments: Argument values Default Description controller rosaria, p2os rosaria Hardware driver controller controller_port /dev/ttySx /dev/ttyUSBx /dev/ttyS0 Serial port communicate controller sonar true, false false Activate sonars laser hokuyo, lms_1xx, lms_2xx, lms_1xxx, lms_4xxx, lms_5xx, mrs_1xxx, mrs_6xxx, rms_3xx, tim_5xx, tim_7xx, tim_7xxS false Laser reference code ‘false’ laser usbcam true, false false launch usbcam node kinect true, false false launch kinect nodes dashboard true, false false launch GUI window rqt visualize sensors readings control motors roslaunch pioneer_bringup pioneer_bringup.launch controller:=p2os laser:=lms_5xx usbcam:=true controls Laser node: arguments lidar parameters: Argument values Default Description laser_hostname Address Laser address hostname applicable) laser_serial_port /dev/ttySx /dev/ttyUSBx /dev/ttyUSB0 Laser Port applicable) laser_baselink_distance roll pitch yaw Distance baselink lidar meters setting broadcast) roslaunch pioneer_bringup pioneer_bringup.launch laser:=lms_1xx laser_hostname:=192.168.0.4 laser_baselink_distance:='0.20 controls Camera node: arguments customize camera parameters: Argument Values Default Description camera_port /dev/videox device number) /dev/video0 USB Camera port camera_display true, false true Display video feed screen stop parameters: customize parameters config/camera.yaml file, image_width, image_height, pixel_format, camera_frame_id io_method. roslaunch pioneer_bringup pioneer_bringup.launch usbcam:=true camera_display:=false camera_port:=/dev/video2 controls Kinect node: arguments customize kinect parameters: Argument Values Default Description kinect_data_skip Number frames skip kinect data kinect_id kinect device number roslaunch pioneer_bringup pioneer_bringup.launch kinect:=true kinect_data_skip:=5 issues open /dev/ttyUSB0: Permission denied frequent issue USB-Serial adapter connect robot hardware lidar. Basically give correct autorizations: sudo chmod /dev/ttyUSB0 issues report problem issues section

name: pioneer_mrs
description: The pioneer_mrs ROS package for Pioneer 3-AT Multi-Robot Systems
readme: pioneer_mrs Introduction ROS package Pioneer Multi-Robot Systems. flexible experimental platform multi-robot systems problem, feel free contact maintainer @hanzheteng open issue repository. ROS package Description (Features) Platform pioneer_mrs Support scientific computing library (numpy, scipy, algorithms Support kinds localization methods (odometry, VICON) Support kinds robot-level simulators (MobileSim, Gazebo) Visualization developing Pioneer Ubuntu LTS Robot Operating System (kinetic) ROSARIA package (git-commit-aa8d5f7) ARIA lib (2.9.1a-ubuntu16-i386) Usage Running Robots System Configuration package drive robots, configure platform steps: machines robots laptop) installed Ubuntu ROS Kinetic robots (onboard computer, installed catkin_make-ed ARIA lib ROSARIA package machines static address able automatically connect local WiFi Write hostname information /etc/hosts file machines commander (laptop) able SSH robots public key password) Onboard computers able access microcontroller serial post (add user dialout group) detailed information set system, refer wiki page. suggestions system development. install teamviewer software laptop robots remote login onboard computers. IDE, recommend RoboWare Studio, designed ROS developers. alternative KDevelop, cross-platform IDE. Package Dependencies git clone repository, install ARIA lib git clone ROSARIA package catkin_make ROSARIA package ARIA library. clone repository git clone https://github.com/hanzheteng/pioneer_mrs.git catkin_make package workspace. Notice: catkin_make ROSARIA package clone package, report errors. catkin_make pioneer_mrs_generate_messages catkin_make package, compiler able find header file messages. Optional package: VICON motion capture system, git clone catkin_make vicon_bridge package. Sample Usage Basically steps: launch nodes robots roslaunch pioneer_mrs multi-robot.launch launch nodes commander roslaunch pioneer_mrs commander.launch Vicon: launch nodes robots roslaunch pioneer_mrs multi-robot.launch pose:=vicon launch nodes commander roslaunch pioneer_mrs commander.launch Running MobileSim Simulator ROSARIA package connect MobileSim simulator. install MobileSim laptop. Basically steps: run MobileSim simulator spawn robots roslaunch pioneer_mrs mobilesim.launch launch nodes robots roslaunch pioneer_mrs multi-robot.launch machine:=mobilesim launch nodes commander roslaunch pioneer_mrs commander.launch Running Gazebo Simulator ROSARIA package Gazebo charge publishing odom information dealing cmd_vel commands. Gazebo installed default ROS choosing desktop-full install. simulations Pioneer robots, models URDF files supplied Mobile Robots git clone amr-ros-config meta-package. Basically steps: run Gazebo simulator spawn robots roslaunch pioneer_mrs gazebo.launch launch nodes robots roslaunch pioneer_mrs multi-robot.launch pose:=gazebo machine:=gazebo launch nodes commander roslaunch pioneer_mrs commander.launch Notice: real robots, movement commands execute WatchDog timeout mechanism; gazebo simulator, robots moving direction. press space bar (stop command) stop Single Robot Recoverary Optional launch file robot1): launch robot roslaunch pioneer_mrs single-robot.launch hostname:=robot1 launch robot Vicon roslaunch pioneer_mrs single-robot.launch hostname:=robot1 pose:=vicon launch robot MobileSim simulator roslaunch pioneer_mrs single-robot.launch hostname:=robot1 machine:=mobilesim launch robot Gazebo simulator roslaunch pioneer_mrs single-robot.launch hostname:=robot1 pose:=gazebo machine:=gazebo Robot Operating System ROS Node Info Note: robot# represents robot label Node Topic Srv Client Pub Topic Srv Server Description /vicon (vicon_bridge package) /vicon/robot#/robot# publish translation rotation info /robot#/RosAria (rosaria package) /robot#/RosAria/cmd_vel /robot#/RosAria/pose SDK bridge communicate microcontrollers /robot#/pioneer_server /robot#/cmd_vel_hp /robot#/RosAria/pose /robot#/gazebo/odom /vicon/robot#/robot# /robot#/RosAria/cmd_vel /robot#/get_pose pose server pioneer robots /robot#/algorithm_node name: pioneer_teleop
description: The pioneer_teleop package provides teleoperation using keyboard, sockets or command line for the Adept MobileRobots Pioneer and Pioneer-compatible robots (Including Pioneer 2, Pioneer 3, Pioneer LX, AmigoBot, PeopleBot, PatrolBot, PowerBot, Seekur and Seekur Jr.).
readme: Pionner_teleop ROS package providing scripts teleoperation keyboard, sockets command line. package compatible robot ROS ecosystem, originally implemented Adept MobileRobots Pioneer Pioneer-compatible robots (Including Pioneer Pioneer Pioneer AmigoBot, PeopleBot, PatrolBot, PowerBot, Seekur Seekur case robot, read robot Pionner-compatible?” section Note: package tested ROS Kinetic Kame Installation Dependencies install pioneer_bringup package, https://github.com/amine_horseman/pionner_bringup.git Source Download clone repository caktin workspace: ~/catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/amineHorseman/pioneer_teleop.git rosdep install pioneer_teleop Build catkin packages source ~/catkin_ws catkin_make python scripts executable ~/catkin_ws/src/pioneer_teleop/nodes sudo chmod Usage ##Teleop Modes package, ways teleoperation: Keyboard teleoperation: roslaunch pioneer_teleop keyboard_teleop.launch control robot motors keyboard arrows, increase decrease speed, stop version keyboard teleoperation command: roslaunch pioneer_teleop discrete_keyboard_teleop.launch case, robot moves small period time seconds default) stops. Sockets teleoperation: Controls robot remotely throught socket commands move robot web interface internet/LAN). roslaunch pionner_teleop socket_teleop.launch expected commands “left” default, script listens port robot moves seconds. change parameters, extra arguments mentionned bellow: roslaunch pionner_teleop socket_teleop.launch _port:=12345 _speed:=0.3 _move_time:=2.0 Command line teleoperation move robot command line throught terminal ssh roslaunch pionner_teleop socket_commandline.launch _direction:=forward expected commands (_direction argument) “left” default, robot moves seconds speed. change parameters, extra arguments mentionned bellow: roslaunch pionner_teleop socket_teleop.launch _direction:=backward _speed:=0.3 _move_time:=2.0 robot Pionner-compatible? package compatible robot ROS long velocity commands published /cmd_vel topic section). modify .launch scripts remove pionner_bringup call, execute python scripts located /nodes folder. issues Velocity command topic default, scripts publish velocity commands /cmd_vel topic. case velocity commands topic Pionner-compatible robots, remap velocity topic /cmd_vel change topic python scripts /nodes folder issues report problem issues panel TODO Feel free contribute repository Test package Ros Kinetic Kame Add joystick_teleop mode Add xbox360_teleop mode Add web page socket_teleop
