description: Package to access the Raspberry Pi Camera from ROS.
readme: raspicam_node ROS node Raspberry Camera Module. Works versions module. recommend cameras auto gain, general image quality Installation binary https://packages.ubiquityrobotics.com/ follow instructions add repository. run sudo apt install ros-kinetic-raspicam-node Build Intructions build source binary follow section. node primarily supported ROS Kinetic, Ubuntu instuctions presume. catkin_ws ~/catkin_ws/src. Download source node running git clone https://github.com/UbiquityRobotics/raspicam_node.git dependencies recognized ros, create file /etc/ros/rosdep/sources.list.d/30-ubiquity.list add yaml https://raw.githubusercontent.com/UbiquityRobotics/rosdep/master/raspberry-pi.yaml run rosdep update. Install ros dependencies, ~/catkin_ws rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src --rosdistro=kinetic Compile code catkin_make. Running Node node built, run launch file. camera, run roslaunch raspicam_node camerav2_1280x960.launch camera, run roslaunch raspicam_node camerav1_1280x720.launch rqt_image_view connected computer view published image. Configuring node dynamic reconfigure raspicam_node supports dynamically reconfiguring camera parameters. Run dynamic reconfigure node connected computer: rosrun rqt_reconfigure rqt_reconfigure bring user interface Paramaters dynamically adjusted interface. Troubleshooting user video group running groups|grep video. error Failed create camera component, camera cable properly seated ends, cable missing pins. publish rate image network expected, resolution reduce amount bandwidth required. Node Information Topics: ~/image/compressed: Publishes sensor_msgs/CompressedImage jpeg camera module. ~/image: Publishes sensor_msgs/Image camera module parameter enable_raw set). ~/motion_vectors: Publishes raspicam_node/MotionVectors camera module parameter enable_imv set). ~/camera_info: Publishes sensor_msgs/CameraInfo camera info frame. Services: ~/set_camera_info: update calibration info camera. Parameters: ~private_topics (bool): default topics private, meaning node added front topic don’t topics private, set parameter “true”. parameter present order compatibility. ~camera_frame_id frame): frame identifier associate camera. ~camera_info_url: URL camera calibration .yaml file. ~camera_name (string): camera, match camera_info file. ~framerate (fps): Framerate capture Maximum 90fps ~height (pixels): Height capture images ~width (pixels): Width capture images ~quality Quality captured images. ~enable_raw (bool): Publish raw image (takes CPU memory) ~enable_imv (bool): Publish inline motion vectors computed GPU ~camera_id (int): camera supported Compute Module) Calibration raspicam_node package calibration file raspberry camera versions tutorial Monocular Camera Calibration tutorial shows calibrate single camera. checkerboard checkerboard large require specialized printers print full scale. printed common printer sizes auto scaling turned carefully measure square size millimeters convert meters dividing Running calibration requires raw publishing enabled. Add enable_raw:=true camera roslaunch command. launch file camerav2_1280x960_10fps.launch roslaunch raspicam_node camerav2_1280x960_10fps.launch enable_raw:=true workstation: rosrun camera_calibration cameracalibrator.py --size --square image:=/raspicam_node/image camera:=/raspicam_node Motion vectors raspicam_node able output motion vectors calculated Raspberry Pi’s hardware video encoder. motion vectors applications motion detection. add enable_imv:=true camera roslaunch command: roslaunch raspicam_node camerav2_410x308_30fps.launch enable_imv:=true workstation, build raspicam_node MotionVectors ROS message recognized Python: ~/catkin_ws catkin_make source ~/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash Finally, run script imv_view.py visualize motion vectors: rosrun raspicam_node imv_view.py

name: raspicat

readme: raspicat Raspberry Raspberry model Raspberry model Ubuntu Ubuntu Ubuntu MATE ROS ROS Kinetic Kame Device Driver rt-net/RaspberryPiMouse 最新版のROSをインストール ROS WiKiを参照するか、以下のスクリプトを使用してROSのインストールを完了させてください。 ryuichiueda/ros_setup_scripts_Ubuntu16.04_server rt-net/RaspberryPiMouseのREADMEを参照してください。 ROSインストール後、以下のようにワークスペースを作成します。 mkdir ~/catkin_ws/src ~/catkin_ws/src catkin_init_worksapce ~/catkin_ws catkin_make ~/.bashrc source /opt/ros/kinetic/setup.bash ~/.bashrc source ~/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash ~/catkin_ws/src ~/catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/rt-net/raspicat_ros.git ~/catkin_ws rosdep install --from-paths src それでも解決しない場合はIssuesにてお問い合わせください。 ~/catkin_ws catkin_make source ~/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash roslaunch raspicat raspicat.launch Raspberry Catに搭載されているブザーが鳴ります。 rostopic pub /buzzer std_msgs/UInt16 このリポジトリはApache ライセンスについてはLICENSEを参照ください。

	    This package contains tools for flashing the roch's FTDI chip (usually done at the factory).
	    The special firmware for the FTDI chip (USB to serial converter) enables it to appear as 
	    /dev/roch on the user's PC.
readme: roch ftdi Documentation Official Web Page home page, sales, specifications hardware howto. Important Scripts create_udev_rules creates /dev/roch link get_serial_number flasher Trouble Shooting check roch bring properly roch stream data? cat /dev/roch check data stream roch USB device? lsusb “10c4:ea60 Future Technology Devices International, USB-Serial (UART) dmesg happen roch usb plugged. /dev/roch? rosrun roch_ftdi create_udev_rules roch serial number correct? sudo ./get_serial_number directory roch_ftdi scripts Check Device Manufacturer SawYer Robot Product iClebo roch Serial Number: roch_A601D86G sudo ./flasher directory roch_ftdi scripts check serial

name: roch_safety_controller
description:
A controller ensuring the safe operation of roch.

    The SafetyController keeps track of ult, psd and cliff events. In case of the first three,
    roch is commanded to move back. 
    This controller can be enabled/disabled.
    The safety states (cliff, psd and ult etc.) can be reset. WARNING: Dangerous!
    Refrence with yujinrobot' kobuki.

name: roch_sensorpc
description:
Ult/psd/cliff to pointcloud:
    Publish Ult, cliff, and psd sensors events as points in a pointcloud, so navistack can use them
    for poor-man navigation. Implemented intended to run together with roch_base.

GUI {width=”100.0%”} real ROMEO MoveIt! real robot, instanciate ROS controllers Romeo. romeo_dcm_bringup (https://github.com/ros-aldebaran/romeo_dcm_robot) romeo_control (https://github.com/ros-aldebaran/romeo_virtual) set NAO_IP environment variable robot's address: {.sourceCode .bash} export NAO_IP= Launch DCM bringup: {.sourceCode .bash} roslaunch romeo_dcm_bringup romeo_dcm_bringup_remote.launch Wait ready, run: {.sourceCode .bash} roslaunch romeo_moveit_config moveit_planner.launch simulated robot Gazebo) MoveIt! name: ros_opcua_impl_freeopcua
description: The ros_opcua_impl_freeopcua package implements bindings for freeopcua - Open Source C++ OPC-UA Server and Client Library.
readme: ros_opcua_impl_freeopcua Ths package OPC client implementation ROS. client connect OPC server access simple data started ros_opcua_impl_freeopcua start OPC client implementation repository OPC server started. testing server freopcua libraray. start commands: roscd ./build/ros_opcua_impl_freeopcua/freeopcua/bin/example_server start client roslaunch ros_opcua_impl_freeopcua client.launch NOTE: server run computer server. running diffrent computers, localhost insert DNS address computer server running. recommended cross check values server UaExpert OPC client. examples provide nodeId service calls qualifiedName required left Reading Writing Variables services connect server, read write data: rosservice call /opcua/opcua_client/connect "endpoint: 'opc.tcp://localhost:4840'" rosservice call /opcua/opcua_client/read "node: nodeId: 'ns=2;i=2001' qualifiedName: 'MyVariable'" rosservice call /opcua/opcua_client/write "node: nodeId: 'ns=2;i=2001' qualifiedName: 'MyVariable' data: {type: 'uint32', bool_d: false, int8_d: uint8_d: int16_d: uint16_d: int32_d: uint32_d: int64_d: uint64_d: float_d: double_d: string_d: lines OPC server variable Objects->NewObject->MyVariable (ATTENTION: fast check server seconds!) Subscribe Variable rosservice call /opcua/opcua_client/connect "endpoint: 'opc.tcp://localhost:4840'" rosservice call /opcua/opcua_client/subscribe "node: nodeId: 'ns=2;i=2001' qualifiedName: 'MyVariable' callback_topic: 'topic'" Start echo created callback topic topic: rostopic echo /opcuopcua_client/topic output seconds. Exmple: Method Call rosservice call /opcua/opcua_client/call_method "node: nodeId: 'ns=2;i=99' qualifiedName: 'NewObject' method: nodeId: 'ns=2;i=2003' qualifiedName: 'MyMethod' data: {type: bool_d: false, int8_d: uint8_d: int16_d: uint16_d: int32_d: uint32_d: int64_d: uint64_d: float_d: double_d: string_d: result “MyMethod called!” output terminal server started. Compiling freeopcua libraray needed automatically) package implements bindings freeopcua Open Source OPC-UA Server Client Library. build FreeOpcUa commands (asume FreeOpcUa folder): mkdir build build cmake change FreeOpcUa_LIBRARIES variable compatible environment. Enjoy!

Command line Echo multiple topics rosrun ros_wild echo ".*_sensor" topic: /ultrasound_sensor type: sensor_msgs/Range header: seq: stamp: secs: nsecs: frame_id: ultrasound_sensor radiation_type: field_of_view: min_range: max_range: range: topic: /infrared_sensor type: sensor_msgs/Range header: seq: stamp: secs: nsecs: frame_id: infrared_sensor radiation_type: field_of_view: min_range: max_range: range: Python Wildcard Subscriber ros_wild import Subscriber subscribe Log topics Subscriber(r"/rosout.*") sub.subscribed_topics ['/rosout', '/rosout_agg'] unsubscribe topics sub.unregister_all() sub.subscribed_topics resubscribe topics sub.reload_topics() sub.subscribed_topics ['/rosout', '/rosout_agg'] Register Callback ros_wild import Subscriber std_msgs.msg import Bool, String def callback_bool(msg): print("bool {}".format(msg.data)) def callback_string(msg): print("string {}".format(msg.data)) subscribe callback Subscriber(r".+") print("Subscribed topics {}".format(sub.subscribed_topics)) Subscribed topics ['/bool/01', '/rosout', 'rosout_agg', '/string/01'] register callback sub.register_callback(Bool, callback_bool) sub.register_callback(String, callback_string) print("Topics callback {}".format(sub.topics_with_callback)) Topics callaback ['/bool/01', '/strings/01'] Wildcard Publisher code publish Log message /rosout /rosout_agg topics. import rospy ros_wild import Publisher rosgraph_msgs.msg import Log publish topics rospy.init_node("test") pub Publisher(".*", queue_size=1) pub.published_topics ['/rosout', '/rosout_agg', '/tf', pub.publish(Log(msg="this test message")) Links Author: Yuma Mihira Documentation ROS

name: rtt_rosclock
description: This package provides an RTT plugin to access different time measurements on a realtime host.
readme: RTT ROSClock package helpful functions measuring time sources realtime operating system. running Orocos components ROS network, important time synchronized machines. standard RTT::TimeService calls query time, tens seconds NTP-corrected time timestamped messages published ROS dramatically delayed. avoid problems staying realtime-safe portable), rtt_rosclock::host_now() function ROS time structure CLOCK_HOST_REALTIME realtime time source Xenomai system. gnu/linux system, call return result standard ros::Time::now() function. host_now() broadcasting ROS Header Stamps rtt_rosclock::host_now() function time source ROS header timestamps time broadcast ROS messages machines processes. assumed /clock topic active, ROS messages broadcasted based time source. compiled Xenomai running simulation mode, function return NTP-synchronized clock time CLOCK_HOST_REALTIME clock source. Note supported Xenomai compiled Xenomai running simulation mode, pass-through ros::Time::now(). running simulation mode, simulation clock, based ROS /clock topic. pass-through rtt_rosclock::rtt_now(). RTT Services Clock Service “rosclock” RTT service sub-service global “ros” service called “ros.clock” operations: ros::Time ros.clock.host_now(void) ROS time structure NTP-adjusted realtime clock sim clock. ros::Time ros.clock.host_wall_now(void) ROS time structure NTP-adjusted realtime clock wall clock. ros::Time ros.clock.rtt_now(void) ROS time structure RTT clock sim clock. ros::Time ros.clock.rtt_wall_now(void) ROS time structure RTT wall clock. RTT::Seconds ros.clock.host_offset_from_rtt(void) differences RTT wall clock source NTP-adjusted realtime clock source. API calls rtt_rosclock/rtt_rosclock.h header: ros::Time rtt_rosclock::host_now(void) ros::Time rtt_rosclock::host_wall_now(void) ros::Time rtt_rosclock::rtt_now(void) ros::Time rtt_rosclock::rtt_wall_now(void) RTT::Seconds rtt_rosclock::host_offset_from_rtt(void) Simulation Clock Activity package RTT SimClockActivity periodically execute task subject progression simulated clock source. clock source ROS /clock topic exists /use_sim_time parameter set true, manual time update in-process source. simulation clock activity implemented classes: SimClockActivity actual activity executes task. SimClockActivityManager singleton class responsible coordinating execution SimClockActivity periodically (subject minimum desired periods). SimClockThread singleton thread responsible acutal updates RTT::os::TimeService optinally subscribing ROS messages /clock topic. default, SimClockThread running, started, override normal RTT TimeService update based update rate SimClockThread clock source. Usage SimClockActivity similarly RTT::PeriodicActivity. rtt_rosclock service plugin loaded, set number tasks’ activities SimClockActivities, set clock source, enable SimClockThread. import("rtt_ros"); ros.import("rtt_rosclock"); create components my_component_1.setPeriod(0.01); my_component_2.setPeriod(1.0); Set simulation clock activities ros.clock.setSimClockActivity(my_component_1); ros.clock.setSimClockActivity(my_component_2); Set SimClockThread ROS /clock topic time ros.clock.useROSClockTopic(); Start simulation clock thread override RTT clock ros.clock.enableSim();

name: rtt_roscomm
description:

    automatic rtt typekit and transport generation from ROS msg files
readme: RTT ROS Communications Nomenclature Warning: “ROS Service” remote procedure call hapens ROS communication protocols “Orocos/RTT Service” grouping properties, functions, data ports. “ROS Services” satisfy similar role “Orocos/RTT Operations”. Contents package serves purposes. Orocos RTT Service publishing subscribing ROS topics Orocos RTT Services calling serving ROS services Orocos typekits ROS message primitive types template generating wrapper packages ROS .msg .srv files typekits .msg files transport plugin .msg files ros service proxy factories .srv files Plugins ROS Topics package global RTT service creating real-time-safe connections ROS topics Orocos RTT data ports. package Orocos connection policies: buffered unbuffered connections ROS topics. Publishing subscribing command, topic type inferred Orocos port type. Connection policies created operations: rostopic.connection(TOPIC_NAME): Creates connection buffer length rostopic.bufferedConnection(TOPIC_NAME, BUFFER_LENGTH): Creates connection user-supplied buffer length. rostopic.unbufferedConnection(TOPIC_NAME): Creates unbuffered connection, writing thread immediately publishs message (publishing real-time safe. Note TOPIC_NAME prefixed tilde resolved process’s private namespace, similarly topic names resolved rospy. ROS Services package global RTT service task-scoped service facilitating communication ROS services. global service, rosservice_registry register factories creating proxies ROS service clients servers. task-scoped service ued bind RTT operations operation callers ROS services. general, users task-scoped RTT service, similarly rtt_rosparam service task-scoped RTT service rosservice operations: rosservice.connect(RTT_OPERATION_NAME, ROS_SERVICE_NAME, ROS_SERVICE_TYPE) Connect RTT operation ROS service. Note function RTT operation operation operation caller. RTT_OPERATION_NAME: task-scoped operation/operation caller provided/required services separated dots foo.bar.baz.op) ROS_SERVICE_NAME: service client/server ROS graph /some/ros/ns/my_service) ROS_SERVICE_TYPE: full typename service std_srvs/Empty) rosservice.disconnect(ROS_SERVICE_NAME) Disconnects RTT operation operation caller ROS service server client.. ROS_SERVICE_NAME: service client/server ROS graph /some/ros/ns/my_service) rosservice.disconnectAll() Disconnects RTT operations operation callers ROS service servers clients. global RTT service rosservice_registry operations: rosservice_registry.registerServiceFactory(FACTORY): Register ROS service factory rosservice_registry.hasServiceFactory(TYPENAME): Check ROS service type registered rosservice_registry.geServiceFactory(TYPENAME): ROS service client/server factory Code Generation package facilities generating typekits ROS service types defined .srv files generating plugins register ROS service types rosservice_registry service. Usage Connecting Orocos Port ROS Topic Imports import("rtt_roscomm") Publish stream("my_component.my_output", ros.comm.topic("my_ros_output")) Subscribe stream("my_component.my_input", ros.comm.topic("my_ros_input")) set connections code: #include <rtt_roscomm/rostopic.h> Add port stream ROS topic this->ports()->addPort("my_port", my_port_); my_port_.createStream(rtt_roscomm::topic("my_ros_topic")); create privately-scoped component-scoped topic, Privately-scoped (resolves NODE_NAME/TOPIC_NAME) my_port_.createStream(rtt_roscomm::topic("~my_private_ros_topic")); Component-scoped (resolves NODE_NAME/COMPONENT_NAME/TOPIC_NAME) my_port_.createStream(rtt_roscomm::topic("~" this->getName() "/my_component_scoped_ros_topic")); Connecting RTT Operations ROS Services connect Orocos operation ROS service .ops script Orocos DeploymentComponent: Imports import("rtt_roscomm") import("rtt_std_srvs") Load application-specific component loadComponent("some_component_name","some_component_package::SomeComponent") Load rosservice RTT service components loadService("some_component_name","rosservice") Expose operation component ROS service some_component_name.rosservice.connect( "some_provided_service.some_operation", "/some/ros/namespace/empty", "std_srvs/Empty") Expose ROS service component some_component_name.rosservice.connect( "some_Required_service.some_operation_caller", "/some/ros/namespace/empty", "std_srvs/Empty") Making Package’s ROS .msg .srv Types Generally, create catkin package simply create_rtt_msgs script running: rosrun rtt_roscomm create_rtt_msgs my_msgs create package CMakeLists.txt package.xml: project(rtt_my_msgs) find_package(catkin REQUIRED COMPONENTS rtt_roscomm) Generate typekits ros .msg files ros_generate_rtt_typekit(my_msgs) Generate plugin services my_msgs ros_generate_rtt_service_proxies(my_msgs) Call orocos_generate_package() export proper targets orocos_generate_package( DEPENDS my_msgs DEPENDS_TARGETS rtt_roscomm ros_generate_rtt_service_proxies() cmake function generate RTT plugin registers factories services named package plugin loaded. Design ROS Services rosservice_registry RTT service list ROS service clients servers RTT operations operationcallers, rosservice.connect operation, inspects argument Operation OperationCaller. name: rtt_rosparam
description: This package provides an RTT service and service-requester for associating RTT component properties with ROS parameters
readme: RTT ROSParam package RTT service service-requester associating RTT component properties ROS parameters. Design RTT service enables setting/getting individual RTT properties RTT component to/from ROS parameter server, setting/getting properties component simultaneously. operations map operations roscpp library, store retrieved values RTT properties component. service requires RTT properties component match names ROS parameters, modulo namespace resolution policy Usage Service Interface Constants resolution policies): RELATIVE Relative resolution: ABSOLUTE Absolute resolution: PRIVATE Private resolution: COMPONENT Component resolution: "~COMPONENT_NAME/name" Operations (getting/setting params) Operations properties getAll() getAllComponentPrivate() Attempt properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server COMPONENT namespace. getAllRelative() Attempt properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server relative namespace. getAllAbsolute() Attempt properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server absolute namespace. getAllPrivate() Attempt properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server node’s private namespace. Operations setting properties setAll() setAllComponentPrivate() Stores properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server similarly-named property COMPONENT’s private namespace. setAllRelative() Stores properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server similarly-named property relative namespace. setAllAbsolute() Stores properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server similarly-named property absolute namespace. setAllPrivate() Stores properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server similarly-named property node’s private namespace. Operations single properties getParam(ros_name, rtt_name) ROS param ros_name store RTT property rtt_name. leaders private absolute resolution. get(name ,policy) Attempt property named populates properties named RTT sub-service) ROS parameter namespace policy. getRelative(name) getAbsolute(name) getPrivate(name) getComponentPrivate(name) Operations setting single properties setParam(ros_name, rtt_name) Set ROS param ros_name RTT property rtt_name*. leaders private absolute resolution. set(name, policy) Attempt set property named stores properties named RTT sub-service) ROS parameter namespace policy. setRelative(name) setAbsolute(name) setPrivate(name) setComponentPrivate(name) Operations create properties linked ROS param operations rosparam service create properties owner component link ROS parameters. properties evaluate()-d set(), ROS parameter server queried. IMPORTANT Setting ROS parameter property real-time safe operation shouldn’t real-time component. addRosParamProperty_type_(name) Adds property type _type_ owning task context linked ROS parameter Relative namespace resolution context. addRosParamProperty_type_Relative(name) Adds property type _type_ owning task context linked ROS parameter Relative namespace resolution context. addRosParamProperty_type_Absolute(name) Adds property type _type_ owning task context linked ROS parameter Absolute namespace resolution context. addRosParamProperty_type_Private(name) Adds property type _type_ owning task context linked ROS parameter Private namespace resolution context. addRosParamProperty_type_ComponentPrivate(name) Adds property type _type_ owning task context linked ROS parameter ComponentPrivate namespace resolution context. addRosParamProperty_type_ComponentAbsolute(name) Adds property type _type_ owning task context linked ROS parameter ComponentAbsolute namespace resolution context. addRosParamProperty_type_ComponentRelative(name) Adds property type _type_ owning task context linked ROS parameter ComponentRelative namespace resolution context. Scripting Interface Components meant ROS parameters properties set ROS parameter server scripting interface: Import packages import("rtt_ros") ros.import("rtt_rosparam") Create components laodService("my_component","rosparam") parameters component namespace "~my_component/..." my_component.rosparam.getAll() "robot_description" property absolute namespace "/robot_description" my_component.rosparam.getAbsolute("robot_description") Alternatively: my_component.rosparam.get("robot_description",my_component.rosparam.ABSOLUTE) Create property linked ROS parameter my_component.rosparam.addRosParamPropertyDouble("parameter_double") my_component.rosparam.addRosParamPropertyStringAbsolute("robot_name") Change ROS parameter Property my_component.parameter_double my_component.parameter_double other_component.other_double var double my_double my_component.parameter_double my_double Interface component designed ROS parameters, easily access rosparam service RTT ServiceRequester. simple component "robot_description" ROS param global namespace ("/robot_description") "publish_period" ROS param node’s private namespace ("~publish_period") Include service requester interface #include <rtt_rosparam/rosparam.h> class MyComponent public RTT::TaskContext private: Storage RTT properties std::string robot_description_; double publish_period_; public: MyComponent::MyComponent(std::string RTT::TaskContext(name) Add properties this->addProperty("robot_description",robot_description_); this->addProperty("publish_period",publish_period_); rosparam service requester boost::shared_ptr<rtt_rosparam::ROSParam> rosparam this->getProvider<rtt_rosparam::ROSParam>("rosparam"); if(rosparam) Add properties linked ROS parameters rosparam->addRosParamPropertyDouble("parameter_double"); bool configureHook() bool all_params_found true; rosparam service requester boost::shared_ptr<rtt_rosparam::ROSParam> rosparam this->getProvider<rtt_rosparam::ROSParam>("rosparam"); parameters if(rosparam) ROS parameter "/robot_description" all_params_found rosparam->getAbsolute("robot_description"); ROS parameter "~publish_period" all_params_found rosparam->getPrivate("publish_priod"); Evaluate parameter (updates this->getProperty("parameter_double")->value(); return all_params_found; API documentation information.

Keyboard-Based Control Switch “KeyTool” top Rviz window keyboard commands: continue break stop API Basic Publishing Functions rviz_visual_tools.h details documentation functions: publishSphere publishSpheres publishArrow/publishXArrow publishYArrow publishZArrow publishCuboid publishCone publishXYPlane publishXZPlane publishYZPlane publishLine publishPath publishPolygon publishBlock publishWireframeCuboid publishWireframeRectangle publishAxis publishAxisLabeled publishCylinder publishMesh publishMesh publishText publishTest Helper Functions Reset function deleteAllMarkers() tells Rviz clear current markers displayed. markers triggered published, calling trigger() function. batch publishing achieved calling markers created, greatly increasing speed application. explicitly rviz_visual_tools publish triggerEvery(NUM_MARKERS) command: trigger() triggerEvery(20) Conversion functions convertPose convertPoint32ToPose convertPoseToPoint convertPoint convertPoint32 convertFromXYZRPY convertToXYZRPY Convenience functions generateRandomPose generateEmptyPose dRand fRand iRand getCenterPoint getVectorBetweenPoints Frame locking markers automatically updated base frame moves republish. enable enableFrameLocking() enabled default). name: slam_constructor
description:
The package provides implementation of several 2D laser-based simultaneous
    localization and mapping (SLAM) algorithms (tinySLAM, vinySLAM, GMapping)
    created with the SLAM constructor framework. The framework provides common
    SLAM components that may help to develop custom SLAM algorithms and can be
    accessed by provided links.
readme: SLAM Constructor Framework SLAM constructor framework common functionality classes create custom SLAM algorithms laser scan-based methods supported). includes implementation SLAM algorithms: tinySLAM, vinySLAM GMapping. show framework usage base SLAM algorithm. Hardware Requirements Current implementation requires odometry data laser scans ROS topics Subscribed topics). supposes laser scanner fixed robot mounted horizontally. Examples algorithms usage algorithm supplied launch-files MIT Stata Center Willow Garage datasets give idea launch algorithms. _mit_ launch-files general case data dataset require preprocessing. tinySLAM launched roslaunch slam_constructor tiny_mit_run.launch path:=[path dataset] order run algorithm data received real time remove dataset player node launch file, sensor data subscribed topics. Single-hypothesis SLAM workflow diagram shows expected workflow general single-hypothesis SLAM algorithm: Parameters parameters common SLAM algorithms: ROS-specific parameters ~in/lscan2D/ros/topic/name (string, default: /base_scan) laser scan topic ~in/odometry/ros/tf/odom_frame_id (string, default: odom_combined) odometry frame ~ros/tf/map_frame_id (string, default: map) map frame ~ros/tf/robot_pose_frame_id (string, default: robot_pose) output robot pose frame ~ros/tf/async_correction (bool, default: false) set true publish map odom transformation asynchronous thread) listener parameters (refer tf::MessageFilter documentation): ~ros/subscribers_queue_size (int, default: queue size message_filters::Subscriber ~ros/tf/buffer_duration (double, default: buffer size seconds tf::TranformListner ~ros/filter_queue_size (int, default: filter queue size ~ros/rviz/map_publishing_rate (double, default: map publishing interval seconds ~ros/skip_exceeding_lsr_vals (bool, default: false) set true skip laser scan outliers false insert measurements map free space General SLAM parameters ~slam/map/height_in_meters (double, default: map height meters ~slam/map/width_in_meters (double, default: map width meters ~slam/map/meters_per_cell (double, default: map resolution meters ~slam/performance/use_trig_cache (bool, default: false) trigonometry cache speed scan point operations cell occupancy estimator parameters: ~slam/occupancy_estimator/type (string, default: const) type cell occupancy estimator: const cell considered occupied laser scan point cell area cell occupancy estimation based laser beam splits cell ~slam/occupancy_estimator/base_occupied/prob (double, default: cell’s probability occupied scan point cell ~slam/occupancy_estimator/base_occupied/qual (double, default: ~slam/occupancy_estimator/base_empty/prob (double, default: cell’s probability empty laser beam passes cell ~slam/occupancy_estimator/base_empty/qual (double, default: ~slam/mapping/blur (double, default: blur obstacles direction laser beam range meters) ~slam/mapping/max_range (double, default: <infinity>) maximum valid range laser scan measurements updating map laser scan Scan-matcher parameters ~slam/scmtch/type (string, default: <undefined>) scan matcher scan matchers supported: Monte-Carlo scan matcher. Parameters: ~slam/scmtch/MC/attempts_limit (unsigned int, default: maximum number iterations ~slam/scmtch/MC/seed (int, default: <random>) seed RNG ~slam/scmtch/MC/dispersion/translation (double, default: ~slam/scmtch/MC/dispersion/rotation (double, default: ~slam/scmtch/MC/dispersion/failed_attempts_limit (unsigned int, default: hill climbing scan matcher. Parameters: ~slam/scmtch/HC/distortion/translation (double, default: initial step size direction meters ~slam/scmtch/HC/distortion/rotation (double, default: initial step size direction radians ~slam/scmtch/HC/distortion/failed_attempts_limit (unsigned int, default: brute-force scan matcher. Searches scan position initial guess. Parameters set search ranges search steps coordinate meters translation, radians rotation): ~slam/scmtch/BF/[x, t]/from (double, default: ~slam/scmtch/BF/[x, t]/to (double, default: ~slam/scmtch/BF/[x, t]/step (double, default: ~slam/scmtch/spe/type (string, default: <undefined>) scan probability estimator type. wmpp (weighted point probability) supported. Parameters: ~slam/scmtch/spe/wmpp/weighting/type (string, default: <undefined>) point scan equal weight viny weighting scheme vinySLAM paper) ahr weighting scheme based angle histograms ~slam/scmtch/spe/wmpp/sp_skip_rate (unsigned int, default: skip n-th point scan ~slam/scmtch/spe/wmpp/sp_max_usable_range (double, default: max valid scan measurement range scan probability estimation ~slam/scmtch/oope/type (string, default: obstacle) occupancy observation probability estimator type. types supported: obstacle max overlap tinySLAM parameters ~slam/cell/type (string, default: base) cell model type. Accepted values: base cell model original tinySLAM avg cell model proposed paper GMapping parameters GMapping additional parameters (note ~slam/scmtch/oope/type shouldn’t custom): ~slam/particles/number (unsigned int, default: ~slam/particles/sample/xy/mean (double, default: ~slam/particles/sample/xy/sigma (double, default: ~slam/particles/sample/theta/mean (double, default: ~slam/particles/sample/theta/sigma (double, default: ~slam/particles/sm_delta_lim/xy/min (double, default: ~slam/particles/sm_delta_lim/xy/max (double, default: ~slam/particles/sm_delta_lim/theta/min (double, default: ~slam/particles/sm_delta_lim/theta/max (double, default: ~slam/scmtch/oope/custom/fullness_threshold (double, default: ~slam/scmtch/oope/custom/window_size (unsigned int, default: Subscribed topics (tf/tfMessage) provide required transforms. /base_scan (sensor_msgs/LaserScan) laser scans create map Published topics map (nav_msgs/OccupancyGrid) map data periodically published topic Required transforms frame attached incoming scans> (extracted scan messages) odom_frame_id (configurable, default: odom_combined) odometry system transforms current estimate robot’s pose map frame. Frame ids configured. map_frame_id odom_frame_id map_frame_id robot_pose_frame_id Offline mode package utility lslam2d_bag_runner launch algorithms offline mode process datasets BAG format. Usage lslam2d_bag_runner <slam type> <bag file> <traj file>] <map file>] <properties file>] Parameters <slam type> viny, tiny gmapping <bag file> path dataset enable verbose output <traj file> save robot trajectory traj file TUM format <map file> save output map map file PGM format <properties file> path SLAM configuration file key=value format. configurations Contributors Artur Huletski Dmitriy Kartashov Kirill Krinkin Copyright JetBrains Research, Mobile Robot Algorithms Laboratory

name: smacha
description: SMACHA (short for "State Machine Assembler", pronounced "smasha") aims at distilling the task-level simplicity of SMACH into compact YAML-based scripts in the foreground, while retaining all of its power and flexibility in Jinja2-based templates and a custom code generation engine in the background.
readme: SMACHA SMACHA YAML Jinja2-based meta-scripting, templating, code generation engine SMACH. SMACH exceptionally comprehensive task-level architecture state machine construction ROS-based robot control systems. terms power flexibility, task-level simplicity obfuscated script-level boilerplate code, intricate structure lack code reuse state machine prototypes. SMACHA (short “State Machine Assembler”, pronounced “smasha”) aims distilling task-level simplicity SMACH compact YAML-based scripts foreground, retaining power flexibility Jinja2-based templates custom code generation engine background. Overview SMACHA Scripts SMACHA scripts YAML files describe SMACHA generate SMACH code. “Nesting State Machines” SMACH Tutorials SMACHA script: Nesting State Machines Tutorial SMACHA script. sm_top template: Base manifest: smach_tutorials node_name: smach_example_state_machine outcomes: [outcome5] states: BAS: template: Bas transitions: {outcome3: template: StateMachine outcomes: [outcome4] transitions: {outcome4: outcome5} states: FOO: template: Foo transitions: {outcome1: BAR, outcome2: outcome4} BAR: template: Bar transitions: {outcome1: FOO} demonstrates reduction lines raw SMACH code lines SMACHA script. Nice. Base Variables base script specifies variables: state machine, template: base template, manifest (optional): optional ROS manifest, node_name: ROS node, outcomes: list outcomes, states: list constituent states. states base script turn, similar variables discussed sub-sections. States state, including base, template respective code generated. States lists transition order nested SMACH tutorials combinations template state specifications Outcomes state outcomes list base state machine container state. Transitions state transitions associative array (hash/dictionary) state. Remappings Input output remappings user data shown SMACH User Data Tutorial details) associative array state. Jinja2 Templating Jinja2 powerful template engine Python. Jinja2 templates code generated SMACHA scripts. Base template Base.tpl file "Utils.tpl" import render_userdata set defined_headers block base_header #!/usr/bin/env python base_header endblock base_header block imports import roslib; manifest defined %}roslib.load_manifest('{{ manifest endif import rospy import smach import smach_ros imports endblock imports block defs defs endblock defs block class_defs class_defs endblock class_defs defined set sm_name set sm_name 'sm' endif block main_def def function_name defined function_name %}main{% endif rospy.init_node('{% node_name defined node_name endif main_def indent(4) endblock main_def block body sm_name smach.StateMachine(outcomes=[{% outcome outcomes outcome loop.last endif endfor userdata defined render_userdata(name userdata) indent(4) endif header header[name] indent(4, true) endif sm_name body indent(8) endblock body block footer footer indent(8) endblock footer block introspection_server sis smach_ros.IntrospectionServer('{% node_name defined node_name endif sis.start() endblock introspection_server block execute execute indent(4) outcome sm_name }}.execute() endblock execute block spin rospy.spin() endblock spin block base_footer base_footer indent(4) endblock base_footer block main '__main__': indent(4, true) function_name defined function_name %}main{% endif endblock main Core Templates SMACHA default core templates SMACH states containers, constructs. core templates present functional: Base.tpl: core base template bare bones Python SMACH state machine script. State.tpl: functionality common states, userdata specification. StateMachine.tpl: core template inserting StateMachine container. Concurrence.tpl: core template inserting Concurrence container. ServiceState.tpl: core template inserting ServiceState. SimpleActionState.tpl: core template inserting SimpleActionState. TF2ListenerState.tpl: reading transforms. Core Code Generation Variables Code Blocks number core code generation variables code blocks present core templates enable code generation engine produce code places. base_header block: code top program script. defs block: position function definitions. class_defs block: position class definitions. main_def block: position main function definition. header block: header block state template rendered header variable parent template base template depending nesting depth. body block: body block state template rendered body variable parent state base template depending nesting depth. footer block: footer block state template rendered footer variable parent template base template depending nesting depth. execute block: position code executing state machine. base_footer block: code bottom program script. main block: code execute main function. additional blocks optionally included, introspection_server ROS spin blocks, introspection server required SMACH viewer, comment blocks, decorate generated source code. Note code generation variables code blocks removed, modified arbitrarily customized API use-cases. code insertion order API, code prepended appended variable. Overriding Core Templates, Variables Blocks Template Inheritance Jinja2 powerful template functionality, including ability extend templates template inheritance, constituent code blocks overridden extended required. SMACHA aims incorporate functionality core templates overridden augmented custom user-specified templates usual Jinja2 template inheritance mechanisms, caveats. works usual Jinja2 variables expressions: extends "<template_name>" expression appears top template, template inherit code blocks parent template <template_name>. super() expression appears block, code block parent template extends rendered position. include "<template_name>" expression appears top template, template include code template <template_name>. Caveats: state template blocks, extends expression top template, implied code blocks rendered variables blocks names blocks state template dictated SMACHA script defined base template container templates. behaviour specific SMACHA present Jinja2. current implementation, base templates extends inheritance mechanism, state container templates include mechanism inherit code templates. Core Code Generation Variables Code Blocks Section examples behaviour works core code generation variables blocks. Usage Section custom templates included generating code command-line practice. Code Generation Engine SMACHA code generator Python API recursively processes SMACHA scripts, manages Python code templates, renders Python SMACH code. API imported Python projects invoked command-line script rosrun. ROS wrapper eventually written generator launched ROS node exposes services generates SMACH code ROS topics, implemented. Installation Dependencies SMACHA relies ruamel.yaml YAML parsing, ruamel system package Ubuntu Trusty rosdep install package ROS Jade Indigo. install ruamel Trusty/Jade Trusty/Indigo pip, tested. Kinetic Lunar installations work fine, Install Simply clone src directory catkin workspace run catkin_make catkin build root workspace. update dependencies rosdep rosdep update rosdep install smacha Usage simplest case, default core templates, SMACHA invoked my_script.yml SMACHA script file rosrun smacha generate my_script.yml generated code output file called smacha_output.py usage “Nesting State Machines” tutorial: roscd smacha/test rosrun smacha generate smacha_scripts/executive_smach_tutorials/state_machine_nesting2.yml smacha_templates/executive_smach_tutorials state_machine_nesting2.py argument specifies custom template directories tutorial, templates override core templates. argument specifies custom generated output file. argument tells SMACHA print verbose processing output terminal. arguments options explored running rosrun smacha generate rosrun smacha generate Testing Tests performed running: roscd smacha/test nosetests executive_smach_tutorials.py python executive_smach_tutorials.py Passing argument bring list options: python executive_smach_tutorials.py Passing argument write generated output files disk, subsequently run state_machine_nesting2.py rosrun smacha state_machine_nesting2.yml.py run original file script, command: rosrun smacha state_machine_nesting2.py Contributors SMACHA developed maintained Barry Ridge. Acknowledgements SMACHA developed ReconCell Project. Minija Tamošiūnaitė design contributions.

name: sns_ik_lib
description:
Saturation in the Null Space (SNS) Inverse Kinematic Library.
   SNS is a real-time Cartesian IK solver for uses the redundancy
   in the supplied kinematic chain for collision avoidance,
   joint motion optimization, or additional task objectives or
   soft constraints.
authors: Fabrizio Flacco, Ian McMahon, Forrest Rogers-Marcovitz

readme: master: dev: spinnaker_sdk_camera_driver ros drivers running Grey (FLIR) cameras Spinnaker SDK. code tested Point Grey Blackfly (BFS) cameras. Compatibility Matrix Spinnaker Architecture Ubnuntu Xenial +ROS Kinetic Ubnuntu Bionic +ROS Melodic Ubnuntu Focal +ROS Noetic AMD64 :heavy_check_mark: :heavy_minus_sign: :heavy_minus_sign: ARM64 :heavy_check_mark: :heavy_minus_sign: :heavy_minus_sign: AMD64 :heavy_check_mark: :heavy_minus_sign: :heavy_minus_sign: ARM64 :heavy_check_mark: :heavy_minus_sign: :heavy_minus_sign: AMD64 :heavy_check_mark: :white_check_mark: :heavy_minus_sign: ARM64 :heavy_check_mark: :heavy_minus_sign: AMD64 :heavy_minus_sign: :heavy_check_mark: :white_check_mark: ARM64 :heavy_minus_sign: AMD64 :heavy_minus_sign: ARM64 :heavy_minus_sign: :heavy_check_mark: Tested :heavy_minus_sign: Applicable :white_check_mark: Reported work compatibility Issue Started instructions copy project running local machine development testing purposes. Prerequisites pre-requisites repo include: ros-kinetic-desktop-full spinnaker (download Grey’s website) ros-kinetic-cv-bridge ros-kinetic-image-transport installing spinnaker, Verify run cameras SpinView. architecture, install libunwind-dev installing ros, install pre-requisites sudo apt install libunwind-dev ros-kinetic-cv-bridge ros-kinetic-image-transport arm64 (aarch64) architecture, install pre-requisites sudo apt install ros-kinetic-cv-bridge ros-kinetic-image-transport Installing install spinnaker_sdk_camera_driver mkdir ~/spinnaker_ws/src spinnaker_ws/src git clone https://github.com/neufieldrobotics/spinnaker_camera_driver.git ~/spinnaker_ws/ catkin_make source ~/spinnaker_ws/devel/setup.bash add ~/.bashrc permanent Running drivers Modify params/test_params.yaml file replacing cam-ids master cam serial number match camera’s serial number. run code launch nodelet verison driver, roslaunch spinnaker_sdk_camera_driver acquisition.launch launch node version driver, roslaunch spinnaker_sdk_camera_driver node_acquisition.launch Test images published running rqt_image_view Parmeters parameters set launch file yaml config_file. good practice ‘task’ specific parameters launch file ‘system configuration’ specific parameters config_file. Task Specific Parameters ~binning (int, default: Binning cameras, changing cameras unplugged replugged ~color (bool, default: false) color images works models support color images) ~exposure_time (int, default: 0:auto) Exposure setting cameras, dynamic reconfiguarble parameter. ~external_trigger (bool, default: false) Camera triggering setting external trigger. mode, cameras set master camera. cameras setup external trigger. mode main loop runs rate set soft_framerate, external trigger rate higher soft_framerate, buffer filled images lag. mode, getnextimage timeout set infinite node dosen’t die trigger received ~target_grey_value (double, default: 0:Continous/auto) Setting target_grey_value (min:4 max:99) turn AutoExposureTargetGreyValueAuto set AutoExposureTargetGreyValue target_grey_value. dynamic reconfigurable parameter. Dynamic reconfigurable parameter section. ~frames (int, default: Number frames save/view ~live (bool, default: false) Show images screen GUI ~live_grid (bool, default: false) Show images screen GUI grid ~save (bool, default: false) Flag images saved opencv mat objects disk) ~save_path (string, default: “~/projects/data”) Location save image data ~save_type (string, default: “bmp”) Type file type save saving images locally: binary, tiff, bmp, jpeg ~soft_framerate (int, default: hybrid software triggering controls FPS, fast ~time (bool, default=false) Show time/FPS output ~to_ros (bool, default: true) Flag images published ROS. manually selecting frames send rosbag, set False. case, frames ‘space bar’ pressed ~utstamps (bool, default:false) Flag image Unique timestamps master cams time stamp ~max_rate_save (bool, default: false) Flag max rate mode master triggers slaves saves images maximum rate multithreaded mode ~flip_horizontal (bool, default: false) Flag flip image horizontally camera rotate mirror image. setting enumeration: “reverseX”. cameras left unspecified cameras default behaviour. ~flip_vertical (bool, default: false) Flag flip image vertically camera rotate mirror image. setting enumeration: “reverseY”. cameras left unspecified cameras default behaviour. horizontal vertical flags true, equivalent rotating 180deg. ~region_of_interest (dict, default width: height: x_offset: y_offset: region interest camera image dict width, height, x_offset y_offset. Width height size final image smaller sensor size). offsets image origin. offset image size smaller sensor size. node/nodelet Specific Parameters ~tf_prefix (string, default: tf_prefix pre-fixed (existing cam_*_optical_frame) frame ros Image msg cameraInfo msg. default option doesn’t add prefix frame uas1/cam_0_optical_frame tf_prefix:= uas1 System configuration parameters ~cam_ids (yaml sequence array) list camera serial numbers order organized. convention start left ~cam_aliases (yaml squence array) names cameras filenames rostopics order cam0, cam1 ~master_cam (int, default: serial number camera master triggering cameras. ~skip (int) Number frames skipped initially flush buffer ~delay (float) Secs wait deinit/init sequence. Dynamic Reconfigure parameters ~target_grey_value (double) Target Grey parameter helps compensate lighting conditions adjusting brightness achieve optimal imaging results. linear percentage maximum pixel Explained detail FLIR webpage. Setting target_grey_value invokes setting AutoExposureTargetGreyValueAuto AutoExposureTargetGreyValue set target_grey_value. ~exposure_time (int, default= 0:auto) Exposure time sensor. Exposure time set minimum maximum exposure time limits(varies camera model), ExposureAuto set ExposureTime set exposure_time param exposure_time set ExposureAuto set ‘Continuous’, enabling auto exposure. nodelet details ~manager (string, default: vision_nodelet_manager) nodelet_manager nodelet launched. ~external_manager (bool, default: false) set False(default), nodelet_manager $(arg manager) launched. set True, acquisition/Capture waits nodelet_manager $(arg manager). Camera info message details ~image_width (int) ~image_height (int) ~distortion_model (string) Distortion model camera calibration. Typical ‘plumb_bob’ ~distortion_coeffs (array arrays) Distortion coefficients cameras array. match number cam_ids ~intrinsic_coeff (array arrays) Intrinsic coefficients cameras array. match number cam_ids ~projection_coeffs (array arrays) Projection coefficients cameras array. match number cam_ids case Monocular camera, projection_coeffs set, intrinsic_coeff set, ~rectification_coeffs (array arrays) Rectification coefficients cameras array. match number cam_ids Multicamera Master-Slave Setup multiple cameras, images cameras synched master-slave setup GPIO connector. support multicamera operation code. general guide multi camera setup https://www.ptgrey.com/tan/11052, note slightly setup package. Refer params/multi-cam_example.yaml setup configuration. master_cam cameras cam_ids list. master camera camera explicitly software triggered code triggered internally counter frame rate. cameras triggered externally master camera triggers. order work, wiring external signal master camera Line2 connected Line3 slave cameras. connect cameras Connect primary (master) camera’s pin (red wire, GPIO) secondary (slave) camera’s pin (green wire, GPI). Nodes area_division area_division divides environment area multiple CPSs. node running, listens area division requests CPSs perform area division. division triggered CPS join leave swarm behavior state CPS area division starts, CPSs participate area division, stop moving, synchronize order achieve area division result. division algorithm based DARP algorithm divide area optimally. CPS assigned equal share environment includes current position. name: swarmros
description: SwarmIO communications bridge for ROS
readme: CPSwarm Communication Library CPSwarm Communication Library unified interface tools swarm members interact started information, WIKI. Deployment Packages built continuously Bamboo. Build Building Linux Build work Linux distribution, packaging architecture detection work Debian Ubuntu. default, compiled host architecture, cross-compilation armhf arm64 supported required tools default compiler clang, GCC Builds tested Ubuntu latest updates installed. build modes choose DEVELOPMENT (default) Development mode build install components subdirectory build directory. IDE full include path debugging. default, products installed “devtree-" subdirectory. PACKAGE Package mode build .deb packages target set /opt/swarmio install prefix. Find packages “packages” subdirectory. INSTALL Install install outputs local machine, default /opt/swarmio. build, create directory source tree, issue commands cmake <PATH SOURCE> [optional parameters] cmake --build Optional parameters: -DSWARMIO_BUILD_MODE=<MODE> (DEVELOPMENT, PACKAGE INSTALL) Specifies build mode DEVELOPMENT build generate debug executables, PACKAGE INSTALL builds compile release flags debug symbols). -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=<TYPE> (Debug, Release, RelWithDebInfo) Override build type derrived build mode. CMake docs info. -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=<PATH> Overrides installation directory. DEVELOPMENT INSTALL build, override output directory. PACKAGE builds, override installation prefix generated packages. -DSWARMIO_TARGET_ARCHITECTURE=<ARCH> (armhf arm64) Enables cross-compilation required tools installed. Ubuntu systems, crossbuild-essential- packages trick. -DSWARMIO_BUILD_ROS_NODE=ON turned swarmros built. -DSWARMIO_MULTISTRAP_CONFIGURATION=<CONFIG> configured, full multistrap sysroot initialized cross-compilation. Requires multistrap bunch tools installed. supported configurations: xenial (Ubuntu xenial-ros (Ubuntu ROS Kinetic Kame) bionic (Ubuntu bionic-ros (Ubuntu ROS Melodic Morenia) -DSWARMIO_SYSROOT=<SYSROOT PATH> multistrap sysroot manually set cross-compilation work. Ensure system image absolute symbolic links -DSWARMIO_GCC_VERSION=<GCC VERSION> multistrap set GCC version present manually sysroot order compilers find correct libraries link -DSWARMIO_ROS_PREFIX=<PATH> Specifies location ROS installation building ROS node. script automatically detect default installation directory Kinetic Kame Melodic Morenia. -DSWARMIO_PREFER_GCC=ON GCC clang. note cross-compilation work operating system architecture) host machine. Requires GCC Building Windows Windows, DEVELOPMENT mode supported. Building ROS node multistrap environments supported. Basic build command Linux: cmake <PATH SOURCE> [optional parameters] cmake --build Optional parameters: -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=<TYPE> (Debug, Release, RelWithDebInfo) Overrides default build mode (Debug). -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=<PATH> Overrides output directory. -DSWARMIO_TARGET_ARCHITECTURE=<ARCH> Specifies generate bit builds. Quick generate packages armhf target, ROS support turned targeting Ubuntu Xenial: cmake <SOURCE_DIR> -DSWARMIO_BUILD_MODE=PACKAGE -DSWARMIO_TARGET_ARCHITECTURE=armhf -DSWARMIO_MULTISTRAP_CONFIGURATION=xenial-ros -DSWARMIO_BUILD_ROS_NODE=ON cmake --build output packages packages subdirectory. Contributing Contributions fork, submit pull request. major open issue discuss authors. Affiliation work supported European Commission CPSwarm project grant

ROS package build top unofficial TelloPy library. TelloPy library moment offers functionalities official Tello SDK unofficial library. name: thingmagic_usbpro
description:
ROS driver package for the thingmagic USB pro RFID reader.  The driver creates a node named /rfid_detector_node and 
publish all detected tags on the topic /RFID_detections as custom RFID_Detection messages.  The RFID_Detection messages have 4
fields: 
  epc:  A string containing the RFID tag identifier detected
  antenna:  8-bit integer containing the antenna number of the detection
  read_count:  8-bit integer containing the number of detections during the collection period
  rssi:  8-bit integer containing the RSSI value of the detection 

Uses the python wrapper for ThingMagic's Mercury API located here: https://github.com/gotthardp/python-mercuryapi
readme: ROS driver ThingMagic USB Pro RFID detector Install Package Dependencies (clone repo install): ~/catkin_ws/src git clone https://gitlab.com/chambana/thingmagic_usbpro.git ./thingmagic_usbpro/install_script.sh ~/catkin_ws catkin_make source devel/setup.bash Launch Package: roslaunch thingmagic_usbpro rfid_detector.launch Settings settings configured editing file thingmagic_usbpro/launch/rfid_detector.launch Device port default /dev/ttyACM0 Device Region code default North America Message Publish Rate (max) default Petr Gotthard maintaing Mercury API Python Wrapper

name: thormang3_imu_3dm_gx4
description:
Driver for Microstrain 3DM-GX4-25 IMU
    This package is modified by robotis.
    The original version is Kumar Robotics's imu_3dm_gx4 package.
readme: Notice thormang3_imu_3dm_gx4 package modified version imu_3dm_gx4 package (Apache License ROBOTIS Thormang3. document original README file existing imu_3dm_gx4 package. refer link details. https://github.com/KumarRobotics/imu_3dm_gx4 imu_3dm_gx4 imu_3dm_gx4 package support Lord Corporation Microstrain 3DM-GX4-25 series IMU. package employs MIP packet format, conceivably adapted support versions Microstrain products effort. present, series AHRS systems supported. package works Ubuntu Version History Fixed issue packets dropped header checksum broken multiple packets. Added verbose option. Replaced decimation options rate options. Decimation automatically calculated. Added file Kalibr. Added udev rule configure permissions. Units acceleration agreement ROS convention. Cleaned code base, replaced error codes exceptions. Status viewed rqt_runtime_monitor. Filter output single, custom message covariances important status flags. Added option enable/disable accelerometer update estimator. Removed broadcast option. Reformatted code base clang-llvm convention. release. Options imu_3dm_gx4 node supports base options: device: Path device /dev. Defaults /dev/ttyACM0. baudrate: Baudrate employ serial communication. Defaults frame_id: Frame headers. imu_rate: IMU rate Default verbose: true, packet reads mismatched checksums logged. additional options present leveraging 3DM’s onboard estimation filter: enable_filter: true, IMU estimation filter enabled. Default false. filter_rate: Filter rate Default enable_mag_update: true, IMU magnetometer correct heading angle estimate. Default false. enable_accel_update: true, IMU accelerometer correct roll/pitch angle estimates. Default true. order launch node (streaming IMU data execute: roslaunch imu_3dm_gx4 imu.launch Note rate parameters: _rate options command requested frequency calculating ‘decimation defined relation: frequency base_frequency decimation base frequency 1kHz decimation values integers, frequencies expressed relation. integer decimation rate. cases, receive frequency obtained rounding decimation nearest integer. requesting produce 1kHz. Output launch, node configure IMU parameters enable streaming node. topics published: /imu_3dm_gx4/imu: instance sensor_msgs/Imu. Orientation quaternion message part filter message. /imu_3dm_gx4/magnetic_field: instance sensor_msgs/MagneticField. /imu_3dm_gx4/pressure: instance sensor_msgs/FluidPressure. topics published synchronized timestamps. Additional topics published enable_filter true: /imu_3dm_gx4/filter: instance imu_3dm_gx4/FilterOuput. Custom message indicating onboard filter outputs. Issues enable_mag_update option set false device acknowledges setting positive ACK), quat_status field received fully debugged Specifications Specifications 3DM-GX4-25. provide YAML file work Kalibr, calib folder. FAQs data rates driver supports IMU data, filter data. high data rates, recommended baudrate driver open device, path correctly ownership device /dev, part dialout group.

name: tiny_slam
description: TinySLAM ROS implementation
readme: TinySLAM implementation ROS Description package ROS implentation tinySLAM (https://openslam.org/tinyslam.html) simpliest lightweight SLAM methods. Current implementation compatible ROS Jade adds improvements original method: alternative model grid map cell track average stored effective values (probability occupied scan’s quality account); option compute probability cell occupied based laser beam splits cell original implementation constant Hardware Requirements Current implementation supposes robot odometry data laser scan measurements horizontally mounted fixed laser scanner. data ROS topics Subscribed topics). launch-file run configure tinySLAM node: roslaunch tiny_slam tinyslam_run.launch path:=[path dataset] Dataset BAG format (http://wiki.ros.org/rosbag). comment rosbag node launch file real-time data replace dataset player. Nodes tiny_slam tiny_slam node takes sensor_msgs/LaserScan messages odometry data topic builds map (nav_msgs/OccupancyGrid) retrieved ROS topic /map. Subscribed Topics (tf/tfMessage) Transforms relate frames laser, base, odometry laser_scan (sensor_msgs/LaserScan) Laser scans create map Published Topics map (nav_msgs/OccupancyGrid) map data topic (updated periodically) Parameters ~cell_type (string, default: “avg”) Accepted values: “avg” modified cell model; “base” original cell model ~occupancy_estimator (string, default: “const”) Accepted values: “const” original occupancy estimator; “area” dynamic occupancy estimator ~skip_exceeding_lsr_vals (boolean, default: false) Determines handle range laser measurements: true ignore measurements, false process empty cells. ~base_occupied_prob (float, default: initial probability cell occupied probability laser scan data valid) ~base_empty_prob (float, default: initial probability cell empty probability range laser scan data valid) Required Transforms base_link odom_combined: odometry system driver mobile base) Transforms map robot_pose: current estimate robot’s pose map frame Contributors Artur Huletski Dmitriy Kartashov Kirill Krinkin Copyright JetBrains Research, Mobile Robot Algorithms Laboratory

name: wire
description:
The wire meta package is implements a framework that
     generates and maintains one consistent world state estimate based
     on object detections. It solves the data association problem by
     maintaining multiple hypotheses and facilitates tracking of various
     object attributes. The state estimators used for estimation and the
     probabilistic models used for association can be configured.

Planner Start Planner control node Moveit! Planner Rviz visual simulation roslaunch xarm_planner robot_planner_fake.launch robot_type:=xarm dof:=7 <optional parameters> robot_type: xarm/lite/uf850 dof: robot_type=xarm: dof=5/6/7 robot_type=lite/uf850: dof=6 Moveit! Planner Real Robotic Arm roslaunch xarm_planner robot_planner_realmove.launch robot_ip:=<your controller box LAN address> robot_type:=xarm dof:=7 <optional parameters> robot_type: xarm/lite/uf850 dof: robot_type=xarm: dof=5/6/7 robot_type=lite/uf850: dof=6 Call Service planning xarm7 joint-space planning request: rosservice call xarm_joint_plan 'target: target elements case correspond joint target angle radians, number elements DOF. Cartesian-space point-to-point planning request: rosservice call xarm_pose_plan 'target: separated fields Cartesian target correspond tool frame position ***meters*** orientation ***Quaternions*** Note motion point-to-point, meaning trajectory straight line. Cartesian straight line planning request: rosservice call xarm_straight_plan 'target: Command data units Cartesian pose command. planned succesfully, end-effector trajectory straight-line space. Note velocity change expected execution. refer MoveGroupInterface documentation modifications code calling planning services, boolean result named 'success' returned. Execution planned trajectory: Notice: Cartesian planning special care, trajectory Moveit (OMPL) planner highly random necessarily optimal (closest) solution, check Rviz befroe confirm move! solution exists user execute robot, service call (recommended) topic message. Execute service call (Blocking) Call ‘xarm_exec_plan’ service request data ‘true’, latest planned path executed, service return finishing execution: rosservice call xarm_exec_plan 'true' Execute topic (Non-blocking) publish message (type: std_msgs/Bool) topic “/xarm_planner_exec”, boolean data ‘true’ launch execution, returns immediately wait finish: rostopic pub /xarm_planner_exec std_msgs/Bool 'true' Optional Parameters General parameters robot_sn: manipulator load inertial parameters, generally precise inertial parameters required model1300: model, valid xarm series, model changed, robot_sn automatically override parameter limited: limit joint range default true attach_to: link robotic arm attach, default world create link default) attach_xyz: xyz offset arm relative link attach_to, default attach_rpy: rpy offset arm relative link attach_to, default add_realsense_d435i: load RealSense camera model add_d435i_links: load detailed linkage add_realsense_d435i true add_gripper: load gripper, default false, priority higher add_vacuum_gripper add_other_geometry add_vacuum_gripper: load vacuum gripper, default false, priority add_gripper, higher add_other_geometry add_other_geometry: add geometry models default false, priority add_gripper add_vacuum_gripper geometry_type: type geometric model loaded supports box/cylinder/sphere/mesh, parameters supported types geometry_mass: Geometric model quality, unit default geometry_height: Geometry model height, unit (meter), default valid geometry_type box/cylinder/sphere geometry_radius: Geometry model radius, unit (meter), default valid geometry_type cylinder/sphere geometry_length: Geometry model length, unit (meter), default valid geometry_type box geometry_width: Geometry model width, unit (meter), default valid geometry_type box geometry_mesh_filename: file geometric model, geometry_type valid mesh, file stored xarm_description/meshes/other/ directory, file directory file geometry_mesh_origin_xyz: datum reference system geometric model relative reference system flange. geometry_type mesh valid. Pay attention quotation marks geometry_mesh_origin_xyz:=’“0.0 geometry_mesh_origin_rpy: datum reference system geometric model relative reference system flange, geometry_type mesh. Pay attention quotation marks geometry_mesh_origin_rpy:=’“0.0 geometry_mesh_tcp_xyz: offset geometric model (TCP) relative reference frame geometric model, valid geometry_type mesh. Pay attention quotation marks geometry_mesh_tcp_xyz:=’“0.0 geometry_mesh_tcp_rpy: offset geometric model (TCP) relative reference frame geometric model, valid geometry_type mesh. Pay attention quotation marks geometry_mesh_tcp_rpy:=’“0.0 Load box model virtual xArm7 roslaunch uf_robot_moveit_config xarm7_moveit_fake.launch add_other_geometry:=true geometry_type:=box Load cylinder model virtual xArm7 roslaunch uf_robot_moveit_config xarm7_moveit_fake.launch add_other_geometry:=true geometry_type:=cylinder Load sphere model virtual xArm7 roslaunch uf_robot_moveit_config xarm7_moveit_fake.launch add_other_geometry:=true geometry_type:=sphere Load mesh models (load vacuum_gripper loaded model xarm_description/meshes/other, geometry_mesh_filename parameter pass file virtual xArm7 roslaunch uf_robot_moveit_config xarm7_moveit_fake.launch add_other_geometry:=true geometry_type:=mesh geometry_mesh_filename:=package://xarm_description/meshes/vacuum_gripper/xarm/visual/vacuum_gripper.stl geometry_mesh_tcp_xyz:='"0 jnt_stat_pub_rate: Publish frequency joint_state_publisher, default kinematics_suffix: joint Kinematics parameter file suffix xArm/UF850 produced August Generation Kinematics parameter file: src/xarm_ros/xarm_description/config/kinematics python gen_kinematics_params.py {robot_ip} {kinematics_suffix} Note robot_ip represents robot arm. connect robot arm obtain actual parameters. kinematics_suffix represents suffix generated parameter file. successful, configuration file generated xarm_description/config/kinematics/user directory. kinematics_suffix AAA, file xarm5: xarm_description/config/kinematics/user/xarm5_kinematics_AAA.yaml xarm6: xarm_description/config/kinematics/user/xarm6_kinematics_AAA.yaml xarm7: xarm_description/config/kinematics/user/xarm7_kinematics_AAA.yaml lite6: xarm_description/config/kinematics/user/lite6_kinematics_AAA.yaml xarm_description/config/kinematics/user/uf850_kinematics_AAA.yaml Kinematics parameter file: parameter starting launch file Note parameter, configuration file exists. exist, connect robot arm above-mentioned script generate Special parameters hw_ns: Namespace, xarm series defaults xarm, default ufactory, valid gazebo/realmove startup scripts, service <hw_ns>/ velocity_control: speed control, valid gazebo/realmove startup script, default false report_type: report type supports normal/dev/rich, default normal, valid realmove startup script. decides state feedback rate content. Refer developer manual chapter Automatic Reporting Format report contents report type (normal/rich/dev), default type “normal”. users demand high-frequency feedback, report_type:=dev topics /<hw_ns>/xarm_states /<hw_ns>/joint_states published users gpio states updated /<hw_ns>/controller_gpio_states topic, report_type:=rich, reports fullest information controller. developer manual. report rate types: type port Frequency GPIO topic sensor topic normal rich dev Note: GPIO topic <hw_ns>/controller_gpio_states. sensor topic <hw_ns>/uf_ftsensor_ext_states <hw_ns>/uf_ftsensor_raw_states。

name: xarm_msgs
description: The xarm_msgs package
readme: Service Introduction services messages defined package based programming API. offer users call ROS interface. Services motion_ctrl enable disable servo control joint.(message type: xarm_msgs::SetAxis) set_mode set operation mode. (message type: xarm_msgs::SetInt16) POSE mode, robot position controlled. Trajectory planned XArm Controller. SERVOJ mode, robot commanded servo_j function, fast execution step response, user generate properly interpolated trajectory. TEACH_JOINT mode, Gravity compensated mode, position control. set_state set robot state. (message type: xarm_msgs::SetInt16) READY/START state, robot state perform motion. PAUSE state, robot motion suspended. STOP state, error occurs configuration robot switch state. go_home   Robot home position velocity acceleration.(message type: xarm_msgs::Move) move_line   Robot TCP move Caetesian target point straight line trajectory. Cartesian velocity acceleartion. (message type: xarm_msgs::Move) move_lineb set targets, robot move final target middle points, middle point, straight-line trajectory blended radius. (message type: xarm_msgs::Move) move_joint desired joint positions, max joint angular velocity/acceleration, robot move joint space target. (message type: xarm_msgs::Move) move_servoj: SERVOJ mode, user trajectory planned, call service high rate. trajectory point executed fast immediately. (message type: xarm_msgs::Move) check srv files detailed data information. Feedback Status Message Refer RobotMsg robot feedback information contents published topic “/xarm/xarm_states”. Refer CIOState gpio control box feedback information contents published topic “/xarm/controller_gpio_states”.

name: xarm_planner
description: The xarm_planner package
readme: Package Introduction package intended provide users demo programming interface moveit!, GUI. API users encouraged Moveit tutorial. package, ‘xarm_simple_planner’ basic implementation Move_group interface, higher level configurations (constraints, switch kinematic solver planners, needed, user fully explore Moveit abilities implement sofisticated version. simplified Chinese instructions: Usage Launch simple planner node: simulation, run: roslaunch xarm_planner xarm_planner_rviz_sim.launch robot_dof:=<7|6|5> robot_type:=<xarm|lite> add_gripper:=<true|false> add_vacuum_gripper:=<true|false> work real xArm, run: roslaunch xarm_planner xarm_planner_realHW.launch robot_ip:=<your controller box LAN address> robot_dof:=<7|6|5> robot_type:=<xarm|lite> add_gripper:=<true|false> add_vacuum_gripper:=<true|false> Argument ‘robot_dof’ specifies number joints xArm (default ‘add_gripper’ ‘add_vacuum_gripper’ cases end-effector attached, end-effector attached. node provide services planning request Cartesian target joint space target. Service definition srv folder. User call services planner solve path target point, retrieve boolean result successful node launched, user command-line joint-space planning request: rosservice call xarm_joint_plan 'target: target elements case correspond joint target angle radians, number elements DOF. Cartesian-space point-to-point planning request: rosservice call xarm_pose_plan 'target: separated fields Cartesian target correspond tool frame position meters orientation Quaternions Note motion point-to-point, meaning trajectory straight line. Cartesian straight line planning request: rosservice call xarm_straight_plan 'target: Command data units Cartesian pose command. planned succesfully, end-effector trajectory straight-line space. Note velocity change expected execution. refer MoveGroupInterface documentation modifications code calling planning services, boolean result named ‘success’ returned. Quaternion calculation tips: familiar conversion (roll, pitch, yaw) quaternions, refer page. Execution planned trajectory: Notice: Cartesian planning special care, trajectory Moveit (OMPL) planner highly random necessarily optimal (closest) solution, check Rviz befroe confirm move! solution exists user execute robot, service call (recommended) topic message. Execute service call (Blocking) Call ‘xarm_exec_plan’ service request data ‘true’, latest planned path executed, service return finishing execution: rosservice call xarm_exec_plan 'true' Execute topic (Non-blocking) publish message (type: std_msgs/Bool) topic “/xarm_planner_exec”, boolean data ‘true’ launch execution, returns immediately wait finish: rostopic pub /xarm_planner_exec std_msgs/Bool 'true' Alternative calling services publish messages, programatically. User refer ROS tutorial1 tutorial2 find refer ‘xarm_simple_planner_test.cpp’ src folder run test program xArm7 user modify command list models), launching simple planner: rosrun xarm_planner xarm_simple_planner_test program execute hard-coded joint space target, PLENTY SURROUNDING SPACES EXECUTING Visualization planned trajectory Refer issue xArm end-effector descriptions re-configured enable visualization TCP trajectory successful planning. shown

name: xbot_face
description: The xbot_face package
readme: xbot_face xbot_face是用于重德智能XBot-U科研教学平台机器人的人脸识别ROS程序包。 直接连接显示器在机器人上注册人脸的模式需要在未运行占用XBot-U机器人的人脸识别摄像头设备程序的情况下运行。 默认出厂XBot-U机器人上开机自动运行了人脸识别程序，占用了人脸识别摄像头，可通过在机器人上运行以下指令解除： sudo service xbot stop 然后运行程序01face_register.py即可注册： roscd xbot_face/script/ python 01face_register.py camera/image camera_index/name 2、连入XBot-U网络的PC上注册 该模式使用的是PC的摄像头，直接运行01face_register.py程序即可。 roscd xbot_face/script/ python 01face_register.py camera/image camera_index/name 在连接机器人网络的任意PC或机器人本体上打开链接http://192.168.8.141:8000/management/userids即可看到注册的人脸列表。 roscd xbot_face/script/ python 02face_logout.py userid/all roscore rosrun xbot_face pub_camera_image rosrun xbot_face face_recog.py rostopic echo /xbot/face_result ROSwiki xbot tutorials ROSwiki xbot_face软件说明 XBot-U机器人网站介绍 商务合作：bd@droid.ac.cn 技术咨询：wangpeng@droid.ac.cn或添加微信:18046501051（注明XBot-U咨询）

name: xbot_safety_controller
description:
A controller ensuring the safe operation of Xbot.

    The SafetyController keeps track of bumper, cliff and wheel drop events. In case of the first two,
    Xbot is commanded to move back. In the latter case, Xbot is stopped.
    
    This controller can be enabled/disabled.
    The safety states (bumper pressed etc.) can be reset. WARNING: Dangerous!
readme: xbot_safety_controller

name: xbot_talker
description: The xbot_talker package
readme: xbot_talker xbot_talker是用于重德智能XBot-U科研教学平台机器人的语音交互ROS程序包。 1.模块化。语音识别、语音唤醒、语音合成和语言处理等都做到了模块化，各个模块间通过ROS话题或服务进行通信，方便测试以及用户开发自己的插件。 3.已提供一套集成离线语音识别、离线语音合成以及离线语言处理的启动程序和参数配置，可在Xbot-U系列机器人上应用。 4.除了Xbot-U系列机器人，该功能包同时可应用于任何基于ROS开发的机器人。 下载功能包后请先连通网络，进入到xbot_talker功能包所在的工作空间，通过以下命令安装依赖： sudo apt install sox sudo apt install libsox-fmt-all rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src catkin_make 功能包里已配置好talker.launch启动文件，可以启动离线命令词识别、语音合成以及离线nlp处理功能，实现离线对话交互以及对机器人的控制。同时也提供了集成所有功能的xbot_talker.launch启动文件。如果需要使用在线识别和语音唤醒等功能，请参考后面的语音交互进阶使用手册修改参数和设置。本章节只介绍基础功能的使用。 语音交互模块的启动已集成到xbot-u.launch中随机器人开机启动，使用该模块只需在机器人或者从机上调用相应的service，并修改相应的请求参数，即可打开语音交互模块。 注意:与3.2.4之前版本兼容的接口/xbot/play和/xbot/chat的使用在本章节末尾。 使用前，先调用/talker/version服务查看语音交互软件版本： rosservice call /talker/version "empty: 用户可以调用/talker/play服务将文字合成语音并播放（语音合成），具体指令为： rosservice call /talker/play "loop_times: mode: audio_path: tts_text: 其中您可以将‘语音合成测试’替换为任何您想要播放的文字，也可修改loop_times设置循环播放次数。语音播放成功后，终端会输出相应的提示： success: True 在输入指令时，您可以使用Tab键来自动补全指令，以避免指令输入错误。补全指令后您只需修改相应参数即可。 loop_times：表示循环播放的次数，0表示只播放一遍，1表示共播放2遍…loop_times表示共播放(loop_times+1)遍。 mode：播放模式。0表示把文字（tts_text）合成语音并播放；1表示播放指定的文件（audio_path）。 audio_path：mode tts_text：mode 用户可以调用/talker/play服务播放指定音频文件，具体指令为： rosservice call /talker/play "loop_times: mode: audio_path: '~/catkin_ws/src/xbot/xbot_talker/defaultconfig/audio/default_tts_test.wav' tts_text: 其中audio_path后面是您想要播放的语音文件的路径，该路径为机器人本机上的绝对目录位置，可自行修改。 在输入指令时，您可以使用Tab键来自动补全指令，以避免指令输入错误。补全指令后您只需修改相应参数即可。 /talker/chat服务提供了与机器人进行对话以及通过语音控制机器人的交互功能。运行以下命令，即可进行交互： rosservice call /talker/chat "start_chat: true" 注意：请在机器人响应完毕，听到“嘟”声结束后立即说命令词。预留的控制机械臂的相关命令有一定的响应时间（7s)左右，关于机械臂控制和导航相关的命令，请参考机械臂和导航使用手册。导航相关命令词会自动退出chat模式。 退出/chat服务，结束对话。 退出/xbot/chat服务，结束对话。 若购买xbot-arm产品，会看到机械臂动作，请确保机械臂0.7m范围内无障碍物。 向/demo/leave、/demo/visit和/welcome/yes话题发布true，同时自动退出对话 向/demo/leave、/demo/visit和/welcome/yes话题发布true，同时自动退出对话交互 向/demo/navi_pause话题发布true，同时自动退出对话 向/demo/navi_continue话题发布true，同时自动退出对话 向/demo/navi_to_pose话题发布”Starting_Point“，同时自动退出对话 目标点的信息需与导航json文件内的设置对应。 向/demo/navi_to_pose话题发布”First_Goal“，同时自动退出对话 目标点的信息需与导航json文件内的设置对应。 向/demo/navi_to_pose话题发布”Second_Goal“，同时自动退出对话 目标点的信息需与导航json文件内的设置对应。 向/demo/navi_to_pose话题发布”Third_Goal“，同时自动退出对话 目标点的信息需与导航json文件内的设置对应。 向/demo/navi_to_pose话题发布”Fourth_Goal“，同时自动退出对话 目标点的信息需与导航json文件内的设置对应。 向/demo/navi_to_pose话题发布”Fifth_Goal“，同时自动退出对话 目标点的信息需与导航json文件内的设置对应。 向/demo/navi_to_pose话题发布”fang_zong_office“，同时自动退出对话 目标点的信息需与导航json文件内的设置对应。 向/demo/navi_to_pose话题发布”chang_zong_office“，同时自动退出对话 目标点的信息需与导航json文件内的设置对应。 让机器人播放语音文件，mode:1 rosservice call /xbot/play "loop: false mode: audio_path: '~/catkin_ws/src/xbot/xbot_talker/defaultconfig/audio/default_tts_test.wav' tts_text: 其中audio_path后面是您想要播放的语音文件的路径，该路径为机器人本机上的绝对目录位置，可自行修改。 您可以让机器人播放您输入的文字，只需要在机器人终端中输入如下指令启动/xbot/play服务即可，mode:2 rosservice call /xbot/play "loop: false mode: audio_path: tts_text: 让机器人播放语音文件，mode:1 success: True 在输入指令时，您可以使用Tab键来自动补全指令，以避免指令输入错误。补全指令后您只需修改相应参数即可。 /xbot/chat服务提供了与机器人进行对话以及通过语音控制机器人的交互功能。运行以下命令，即可进行交互： rosservice call /xbot/chat "start_chat: true" readme: startup xiaoqiang launch file package, including launch files start devices Usage: download xiaoqiang ros workspace workspace] git clone https://github.com/BluewhaleRobot/xiaoqiang.git catkin_make Install service system, service startup rosrun robot_upstart install xiaoqiang_bringup/launch/xiaoqiang_robot.launch Uninstall service rosrun robot_upstart uninstall xiaoqiang_robot start service manually sudo service xiaoqiang_robot start stop service sudo service xiaoqiang_robot stop :heart: Bluewhale Robot. 小强启动文件包，包含了小强启动各种硬件的launch文件 首先将软件包下载到小强ROS工作目录 git clone https://github.com/BlueWhaleRobot/xiaoqiang.git catkin_make rosrun robot_upstart install xiaoqiang_bringup/launch/xiaoqiang_robot.launch 移除startup服务 rosrun robot_upstart uninstall xiaoqiang_robot 开启startup服务 sudo service xiaoqiang_robot start 关闭startup服务 sudo service xiaoqiang_robot stop :heart:

name: xiaoqiang_depth_image_proc
description:
Modified version of depth image process, add occupancy xyz

     Contains nodelets for processing depth images such as those
     produced by OpenNI camera. Functions include creating disparity
     images and point clouds, as well as registering (reprojecting)
     a depth image into another camera frame.
